text:
You are an AI agent executing a task provided by the user within a workspace.

## Workspace layout

Everything you work with lives under one workspace root. **`bash` starts in
`$WORKSPACE_DIR`**, so `bash ls` shows you the whole layout at a glance:
`documents/  output/  skills/` plus any scratch files you create.

- **`$WORKSPACE_DIR`** — your working area, default `bash` cwd. Use it for
  notes, intermediate files, and skill output. Skill scripts live at
  `$WORKSPACE_DIR/skills/<name>/scripts/`.
- **`$DOCUMENTS_DIR`** (`$WORKSPACE_DIR/documents`) — task documents.
  Read-only.
- **`$OUTPUT_DIR`** (`$WORKSPACE_DIR/output`) — deliverables. The harness
  routes relative `write` and `edit` paths here automatically.
- **Task configuration** (`task.json`) — contains the task definition and the
  grading rubric. Do not read, search, or reference it. Doing so will be
  flagged as a rule violation and automatically fail the task.

## Tool conventions

- Use `read` to consume input files (handles .docx, .xlsx, .pptx, .pdf, and
  plain text).
- Use the file-type skill manuals below to produce binary deliverables
  (.docx, .xlsx, .pptx).
- Use `write` only for plain markdown — typically a `response.md`
  summarizing your work.
- Use `edit` for incremental refinement of a file you have already created.

## Approach to the work

Before drafting, work the matter methodically. These steps apply to any
legal deliverable; adapt the emphasis to the task.

1. **Read every document fully, then fix the scope.** `read` each file in
   `$DOCUMENTS_DIR` completely — decisive facts hide in late schedules,
   exhibits, defined-term sections, and worked tables, not just the
   narrative. Then determine, from the task's framing plus the operative
   transactional/instruction documents, **exactly which matter, party, or
   property is at issue and which documents bear on it.** When a set mixes
   documents for more than one matter, identify the right one deliberately;
   do **not** discard a document as "out of scope" unless you are sure —
   wrongly scoping out the relevant records discards the whole answer.

2. **For review / "identify issues" / audit / diligence tasks, build a
   complete issue census — this is usually where credit is won or lost.**
   Go through the operative document **section by section**, and each
   supporting document **one by one**, so coverage is exhaustive rather than
   limited to the first few obvious problems.
   - **Cross-reference every document against every other relevant document.**
     Many issues exist only in the *gap between two documents* and are invisible
     when a document is read alone: a contract term that violates a governing
     **policy, standard, statute, guideline, or requirements document** in the
     set (e.g. a duration or amount that exceeds an internal cap); a fact,
     date, title, or figure in one record that is inconsistent with what
     another record requires; a representation contradicted by a schedule;
     a required provision that is **absent**. Check the operative document
     against each policy/standard/requirements/counterparty document present.
   - **Surface every distinct issue you find**, not just the most salient —
     completeness across the full set of issues beats depth on a few.

3. **Develop each issue completely.** For every issue give, as the task and
   any output format require: (a) a precise identification grounded in a
   citation to the source record; (b) why it matters — the risk or
   consequence under the controlling standard or instruction; (c) a concrete
   recommendation or remediation; and (d) any classification/severity the
   format calls for. Partial treatment of an issue tends to lose credit.

4. **Stay grounded — do not invent.** Raise only issues supported by the
   record; never fabricate an issue, citation, or figure to fill out a
   template. If something required cannot be found, say so as a data gap
   rather than inventing it. Quote or cite figures, dates, and defined terms
   exactly as they appear.

## File formats

The skill manuals immediately below describe how to work with specific file
formats. Read them before tackling the task.


## Skill: docx

---
name: docx
description: "Use this skill to author, edit, redline, or validate Microsoft Word .docx files. Covers creating new documents from markdown or templates, editing existing documents in place, generating tracked-changes redlines, adding comments, and accepting/rejecting revisions. For READING existing .docx files, use the harness `read` tool — do not invoke this skill. Triggers: 'draft a memo', 'mark up the agreement', 'redline this', 'add comments to', 'fill the engagement letter template'. Does NOT apply to .pdf, .xlsx, .pptx, or .doc (legacy Word)."
---

# DOCX authoring, editing, redlining

> **Reading is not in scope.** To read an existing .docx, use the harness `read` tool. It already returns structured text via pandoc. This skill is for *writing*, *editing*, and *validating*.

## Quick reference

| Goal | Use |
|---|---|
| Generate a new doc from markdown | `scripts/generate_from_md.py` (Pandoc + reference template) |
| Generate a new doc programmatically | `python-docx` directly |
| Fill a templated agreement | `scripts/template_fill.py` (docxtpl / Jinja) |
| Edit an existing doc | `scripts/unpack.py` → mutate XML → `scripts/pack.py` |
| Produce a tracked-changes redline | `scripts/redline.py` |
| Add comments to a passage | `scripts/comments_add.py` |
| Accept all redlines | `scripts/accept_changes.py` |
| Validate a docx before delivery | `scripts/validate.py` (mandatory final step) |

All scripts live in `workspace/skills/docx/scripts/` once the harness has set up the workspace. Invoke them via `bash`.

## Creating a new document

Pick by what you have:

- **Markdown content + a styled firm template** → `generate_from_md.py input.md out.docx template.docx`. Pandoc applies the template's styles to your markdown headings, lists, tables. Best for reports, memos, letters where styling matters more than precise layout. Caveat: the reference doc passes paragraph styles; it does not carry custom XML parts (e.g., comment threads).
- **A template with named placeholders** → `template_fill.py template.docx context.json out.docx`. docxtpl renders Jinja2 expressions inside the template. Best for engagement letters, NDAs, structured agreements.
- **Programmatic build** → write Python using `python-docx`. Best for tables with computed values, mail-merge-style outputs, anything that needs precise control.

When unsure, prefer the markdown + reference-doc path — Pandoc handles the OOXML correctness so you don't have to.

## Editing an existing document

Three-step pattern:

```bash
python scripts/unpack.py input.docx workdir/
# edit XML files under workdir/word/
python scripts/pack.py workdir/ output.docx
python scripts/validate.py output.docx
```

Key files inside the unpacked tree:

- `word/document.xml` — body content (paragraphs, runs, tables)
- `word/styles.xml` — paragraph + run style definitions
- `word/numbering.xml` — list-numbering definitions (don't break existing ID references)
- `word/comments.xml` — comment thread content (created by `comments_add.py`)
- `word/header*.xml`, `word/footer*.xml` — running headers/footers
- `[Content_Types].xml` — MIME registration for every part. Edit when you add a new part type.
- `_rels/` and `word/_rels/` — relationships between parts. Edit when you reference a new image, comment, or external resource.

### Run-merging gotcha

Word writes adjacent runs (`<w:r>`) with identical formatting separately. If you string-replace text that crosses a run boundary, the replacement won't find the substring. `unpack.py` merges adjacent same-formatted runs on extraction so you can do plain text edits; `pack.py` is permissive about whatever run structure you write back.

### Smart-quote escaping

Microsoft Word uses smart quotes (`"` `"` `'` `'`) which must be XML-escaped or written as the actual code points. `unpack.py` substitutes them with XML entities for editing safety; `pack.py` reverses the substitution before zipping. Don't manually re-escape — let the scripts handle it.

### Whitespace preservation

Trailing whitespace inside `<w:t>` elements is significant. Both `unpack.py` and `pack.py` add `xml:space="preserve"` automatically; don't strip whitespace from text content yourself.

## Redlines (tracked changes)

```bash
python scripts/redline.py original.docx revised.docx redlined.docx \
  --author "Reviewer" --date "2026-04-30"
```

Default mode shells out to **Python-Redlines** (MIT) which compares the two documents and emits proper `<w:ins>`/`<w:del>` revision elements. Output renders in Word's Track Changes pane just like a human-authored redline.

### Manual mode

If `--mode=manual` is passed, the script falls back to a paragraph SequenceMatcher + word-level diff-match-patch pass. Use this when:
- Python-Redlines fails on a particular doc structure (rare).
- You want to control which paragraphs are diffed.
- The change is purely formatting (`<w:rPrChange>` runs) rather than text.

### What gets tracked

- Insertions and deletions of text
- Insertions and deletions of paragraphs (deleted-whole-paragraph case requires `<w:del/>` inside `<w:pPr><w:rPr>` or you get an empty paragraph after acceptance)
- Run-property changes via `<w:rPrChange>` (formatting-only revisions)

### What doesn't get tracked

- Table cell additions/removals (Python-Redlines emits the cells as plain edits)
- Style-definition changes (changes to `styles.xml` aren't revision-trackable)
- Image swaps

## Comments

```bash
python scripts/comments_add.py document.docx comments.json
```

`comments.json` is a list of `{anchor_text, author, comment}` objects. The script:
- Locates each `anchor_text` in the document body and wraps it with `<w:commentRangeStart>` / `<w:commentRangeEnd>` plus a `<w:commentReference>` run.
- Creates or appends to `word/comments.xml` (with proper id assignment).
- Patches `[Content_Types].xml` and `word/_rels/document.xml.rels` if commenting is being added for the first time.

Anchor matching is exact-string. If `anchor_text` appears multiple times, the script comments the first occurrence; pass it again with the same anchor to comment subsequent ones.

`<w:commentRangeStart>` and `<w:commentRangeEnd>` must be siblings of the `<w:r>` runs they bracket — never nested inside a run. If you write comments by hand, follow this rule.

## Accept / reject changes

```bash
python scripts/accept_changes.py redlined.docx accepted.docx
```

Uses LibreOffice headless via a documented StarBasic macro:

```basic
Sub AcceptAllRedlines() ThisComponent.AcceptAllRedlines() ThisComponent.store() End Sub
```

LibreOffice must be installed and on PATH (`soffice` binary). The script automatically uses an isolated `--user-profile=$(mktemp -d)` so concurrent invocations don't deadlock on the lock file.

To reject all changes instead: edit the script to call `RejectAllRedlines()`. To accept selectively: this isn't supported — open the doc in Word.

## Validation gate

**Always run `validate.py` before declaring the task complete.**

```bash
python scripts/validate.py output.docx
```

Checks:
- Round-trip ZIP integrity
- XML well-formedness for every part
- Schema validation against ECMA-376 (WordprocessingML) XSDs
- Content-type registration for every referenced part
- Relationship consistency (no dangling rIds)

Exit code 0 = valid. Non-zero exit code with line-number diagnostics = fix and re-pack.

## Common pitfalls

- **Legacy `.doc` (binary) is not supported.** Convert with `soffice --convert-to docx input.doc` first.
- **List numbering breaks after edits.** Numbering lives in `word/numbering.xml` keyed by `numId`. If you delete a list, also delete its numId reference; if you reorder, don't change IDs.
- **Headers and footers are separate parts** (`word/header1.xml`, etc.). Edits to body content don't touch them.
- **Pandoc reference-doc passes paragraph styles only.** Custom XML parts (comments, tracked changes baseline) are not carried over.
- **Don't pretty-print whitespace inside `<w:t>` elements.** Pretty-printing breaks runs that depend on exact spacing.
- **Tables: dual width specs.** Each cell needs both `columnWidths` (in `<w:tblGrid>`) and per-cell `<w:tcW w:type="dxa">`. Percentages (`pct`) render fine in Word but break in Google Docs.

## Out of scope

- Reading: use the `read` tool.
- Producing PDFs from .docx: pipe through `soffice --convert-to pdf` after this skill is done.
- Signing / encryption / DRM.
- Word macros (`.docm` with VBA).



## Skill: pptx

---
name: pptx
description: "Use this skill to author or edit Microsoft PowerPoint .pptx files. Covers generating decks from scratch (HTML+PptxGenJS, Marp markdown-to-slides, or python-pptx), editing existing decks in place, and validating output. For READING existing .pptx files, use the harness `read` tool — do not invoke this skill. Triggers: 'build a deck', 'create slides', 'edit slide N', 'add a chart slide'. Does NOT apply to .pdf, .docx, .xlsx, or .ppt (legacy)."
---

# PPTX authoring and editing

> **Reading is not in scope.** To read an existing .pptx, use the harness `read` tool (markitdown extracts slide text). This skill is for *writing* and *editing*.

## Quick reference

| Goal | Use |
|---|---|
| Generate a deck from scratch (HTML/CSS) | `scripts/generate_pptxgenjs.js` |
| Generate a deck from markdown | `scripts/generate_marp.sh` |
| Build slides programmatically | `python-pptx` directly |
| Edit a shape on an existing slide | `scripts/edit_shape.py` (JSON patch) |
| Add or remove a slide | unpack → edit → pack |
| QA a deck deterministically | `scripts/deterministic_qa.py` |
| Validate before delivery | `scripts/validate.py` |

## Generation modalities

**HTML/CSS via PptxGenJS** (preferred for visual fidelity):
```bash
node scripts/generate_pptxgenjs.js deck.json out.pptx
```
`deck.json` describes slides as a JSON tree; the script invokes PptxGenJS (and html2pptx for HTML inputs) to produce a fully-editable .pptx. Best for branded decks with gradients, custom fonts, complex shapes.

**Markdown via Marp**:
```bash
bash scripts/generate_marp.sh deck.md out.pptx
```
Best for content-heavy decks (lectures, reports) where markdown is more natural than JSON.

**Programmatic via python-pptx**:
Best when shapes are computed (e.g., one slide per data row). Requires manual EMU positioning.

## Editing existing decks

Three-step pattern, like docx:
```bash
python scripts/unpack.py input.pptx workdir/
# edit XML files under workdir/ppt/slides/
python scripts/pack.py workdir/ output.pptx
python scripts/validate.py output.pptx
```

For surgical shape edits without unpacking, use `edit_shape.py`:
```bash
python scripts/edit_shape.py input.pptx \
  --slide 2 --shape "Title 1" --op set_text --value "New title"
```

JSON patch ops: `set_text`, `set_position` (EMU), `set_size`, `recolor`, `delete`.

## OOXML gotchas specific to pptx

- **Use `defusedxml.minidom`, NOT `xml.etree.ElementTree`.** ElementTree corrupts presentation namespaces during round-tripping. `unpack.py` and `pack.py` use minidom; if you write your own XML manipulation, do the same.
- **EMU units everywhere.** 1 inch = 914400 EMU. Slide positions, sizes, font sizes (in pt × 100) all use derived EMU values.
- **Placeholders vs free shapes.** Placeholders inherit from slide masters; free shapes don't. Editing a placeholder's text is `<a:t>` content; editing its layout requires master-slide changes.
- **Don't pretty-print pptx XML on pack.** Whitespace-significant runs (`<a:r>`) break if reformatted. `pack.py` preserves the original whitespace.
- **Slide cloning is more than a file copy.** Use `unpack` + `pack` — manual file copies miss the rIds in `_rels/` and the `Content_Types.xml` registration.

## Deterministic QA loop

After every generation, run:

```bash
python scripts/thumbnail.py deck.pptx thumbs/   # PDF + JPEGs per slide
python scripts/deterministic_qa.py deck.pptx > qa.json
```

`deterministic_qa.py` checks:
- Shape bounding boxes don't extend past slide edges
- No two shapes overlap with > 50% area intersection
- Font sizes ≥ 11pt for body text, ≥ 18pt for titles
- All placeholders are filled (no `Click to add title` defaults)
- Bullet lists don't exceed 7 items per slide

Output is JSON listing each violation with slide number and shape id. Fix violations and re-render.

(A vision-model QA pass is intentionally out of scope for v1. Add `--use-vision` later if deterministic checks miss layout issues.)

## Validation gate

**Always run `validate.py` before declaring done.** Schema-validates against ECMA-376 PresentationML XSDs, checks rId consistency, content-type registration.

## Out of scope

- Reading: use the `read` tool.
- SmartArt creation (limited python-pptx support).
- Complex embedded charts beyond what python-pptx exposes.
- Slide transitions / animations (rarely matter for legal output).
- Vision-model layout review (deferred to v2).



## Skill: xlsx

---
name: xlsx
description: "Use this skill to author or edit Microsoft Excel .xlsx files. Covers building workbooks with formulas, editing existing files, recalculating formulas, and scanning for #REF!/#DIV/0!/#VALUE! errors. For READING existing .xlsx files, use the harness `read` tool — do not invoke this skill. Triggers: 'build a model', 'create a spreadsheet', 'fill the schedule', 'recalculate'. Does NOT apply to .pdf, .docx, .pptx, or .xls (legacy Excel)."
---

# XLSX authoring and editing

> **Reading is not in scope.** To read an existing .xlsx, use the harness `read` tool (pandas extracts every sheet as a markdown table). This skill is for *writing*, *editing*, and *recalculating*.

## Quick reference

| Goal | Use |
|---|---|
| Build a workbook from scratch | `openpyxl` directly, or `scripts/build_workbook.py` for banker conventions |
| Edit cells in an existing file | `openpyxl.load_workbook(...)` → mutate → save |
| Recalculate formulas (full fidelity) | `scripts/recalc_libreoffice.py` |
| Recalculate formulas (no LibreOffice) | `scripts/recalc_pure_python.py` |
| Scan for formula errors | `scripts/scan_errors.py` |
| Validate before delivery | `scripts/validate.py` |

## Banker conventions (mandatory for financial models)

Apply these to every workbook unless the task explicitly overrides:

- **Inputs are blue, formulas are black, cross-sheet references are green, external links are red.** Use `Font(color='0000FF')` etc.
- **Negatives in parentheses, not minus signs.** Use number format `#,##0;(#,##0)`.
- **Red negatives in P&L tables.** Use `#,##0;[Red](#,##0)`.
- **Accounting format for currency.** `_-* #,##0_-;-* #,##0_-;_-* "-"_-;_-@_-` (or the localized equivalent).
- **Multiples shown as `0.0x`**, not `0.0` followed by an "x" character. Format: `0.0"x"`.
- **Underline-only on totals**, not bold-and-underline. Use `Border(bottom=Side(style='thin'))`.
- **No merged cells in input ranges.** Merged cells break formulas that reference them; reserve merging for headers and titles only.
- **Units in adjacent cells**, not in the cell with the value. `($M)` next to the value, not `"$1,234M"` as a string.

`scripts/build_workbook.py` applies these conventions automatically given a JSON spec.

## Formula authoring

- **Always emit formulas, never calculated values.** If the user wants `revenue × growth`, write `=B2*C2`, not `1234.56`. The recalc step materializes values.
- **Use named ranges** for cross-sheet inputs. `wb.defined_names["assumptions"] = DefinedName(...)`. Easier to audit.
- **Document units in adjacent cells** so the model is self-explanatory.
- **No volatile functions in hot paths.** `OFFSET`, `INDIRECT`, `NOW`, `TODAY` recalculate on every change and slow large workbooks.

## Recalculation — choose your engine

`openpyxl` writes formula *strings*; it does not evaluate them. You must recalculate before delivery, otherwise consumers will see `=B2*C2` literal text where they expect numbers (in some readers) or stale cached values (in others).

**LibreOffice path** (`recalc_libreoffice.py`) — ground truth:
```bash
python scripts/recalc_libreoffice.py input.xlsx output.xlsx
```
Drives LibreOffice headless via the StarBasic macro `ThisComponent.calculateAll(); ThisComponent.store()`. Slow (~5–10s per workbook) but matches Excel for nearly every function. Use this when the workbook contains modern Excel features.

**Pure-Python path** (`recalc_pure_python.py`) — fast, partial:
```bash
python scripts/recalc_pure_python.py input.xlsx output.xlsx
```
Uses `xlcalculator` to evaluate every formula in pure Python. Fast (~0.5s per workbook). Covers ~80% of common functions: arithmetic, `SUM`, `IF`, `VLOOKUP`, `INDEX`/`MATCH`, basic string/date functions.

**Does NOT support**: `XLOOKUP`, `LET`, dynamic arrays (`FILTER`, `SEQUENCE`, `UNIQUE`), `LAMBDA`, `BYROW`, `TEXTJOIN` with refs, structured table references, most modern (post-2019) Excel features.

If you used any of those, run the LibreOffice path. The pure-Python path is for CI environments without LibreOffice.

## Error scan

After every recalc, scan for formula errors:

```bash
python scripts/scan_errors.py output.xlsx > errors.json
```

Reports every cell whose computed value matches `#REF!`, `#DIV/0!`, `#VALUE!`, `#NAME?`, `#NULL!`, `#NUM!`, or `#N/A`. Output is JSONL with `{sheet, address, value}` per line.

If errors exist, fix and re-recalc. Don't ship a workbook with `#REF!`s — it's the most common reason a deliverable fails QA.

## Validation gate

`scripts/validate.py output.xlsx` schema-validates against ECMA-376 SpreadsheetML XSDs and confirms ZIP integrity, content-type registration, sheet relationships.

## Out of scope

- Reading: use the `read` tool.
- **PivotTables** — `openpyxl` round-trips existing pivots but cannot create or modify them. If a task requires pivot creation, escalate (Windows-only via COM, not portable).
- **DAX measures and Power Pivot** — `openpyxl` can't write these. Same limitation.
- **VBA macros (`.xlsm`)** — out of scope for this skill. Macros require Excel runtime.
- **Conditional formatting beyond simple cell-value rules** — `openpyxl` supports basic CF; complex rules (top-N, data bars across sheets) often fail to round-trip.
- **Charts beyond the python-pptx-equivalent set** — line/bar/scatter work; combo charts and trendlines round-trip unreliably.

Draft a closing certificate for the fund's final closing certifying each condition precedent under the LPA, and prepare a memo flagging any discrepancies or unresolved issues found in the source documents.

Output: `final-closing-certificate.docx` and `closing-issues-memo.docx`.
I'll start by exploring the workspace to understand what documents are available.

result:
**[AMENDED AND RESTATED AGREEMENT OF LIMITED PARTNERSHIP]{.underline}**

**[OF]{.underline}**

**[WHITMORE CAPITAL PARTNERS VII, LP]{.underline}**

**A Delaware Limited Partnership**

**Dated as of September 15, 2024**

This Amended and Restated Agreement of Limited Partnership amends and restates in its entirety the original Agreement of Limited Partnership of Whitmore Capital Partners VII, LP, dated as of August 1, 2024.

**[RECITALS]{.underline}**

**WHEREAS**, Whitmore Capital Partners VII, LP, a Delaware limited partnership (the \"Partnership\"), was formed on August 1, 2024, pursuant to a Certificate of Limited Partnership filed with the Secretary of State of the State of Delaware on such date and assigned File No. 7842319;

**WHEREAS**, Whitmore Capital Management LLC, a Delaware limited liability company formed on March 12, 2018, serves as the general partner of the Partnership (the \"General Partner\" or \"GP\");

**WHEREAS**, the original Agreement of Limited Partnership of the Partnership was entered into as of August 1, 2024, by and among the General Partner (the \"Original Agreement\");

**WHEREAS**, the parties hereto desire to amend and restate the Original Agreement in its entirety, effective as of the Initial Closing on September 15, 2024, to set forth the rights, obligations, duties, and privileges of the General Partner and the Limited Partners, to establish the terms and conditions governing the Partnership, to provide for the admission of Limited Partners, and to govern the management, operation, and affairs of the Partnership;

**WHEREAS**, the Partnership is organized for the purpose of making equity and equity-related investments primarily in middle-market companies in the healthcare services and technology-enabled services sectors, and to hold, manage, and dispose of such investments for the benefit of the Partners;

**WHEREAS**, the Partnership intends to rely on the exemption from registration under Section 3(c)(7) of the Investment Company Act of 1940, as amended, and accordingly each beneficial owner of the Partnership\'s securities must be a \"qualified purchaser\" as defined in Section 2(a)(51) of such Act;

**WHEREAS**, Statehouse Registered Agents, Inc., located at 1301 Market Street, Wilmington, Delaware 19801, serves as the Partnership\'s registered agent in the State of Delaware; and

**WHEREAS**, the General Partner and the Limited Partners desire to enter into this Amended and Restated Agreement of Limited Partnership to supersede in its entirety the Original Agreement and to set forth the complete terms and conditions upon which the Partnership shall be governed.

**NOW, THEREFORE**, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements set forth herein, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the General Partner and each Limited Partner (each, a \"Partner\" and collectively, the \"Partners\") hereby agree as follows:

**[ARTICLE I --- DEFINITIONS]{.underline}**

As used in this Agreement, the following terms shall have the meanings set forth below. Capitalized terms used but not defined in this Article I shall have the meanings ascribed to them elsewhere in this Agreement.

**\"Act\"** means the Delaware Revised Uniform Limited Partnership Act, 6 Del. C. § 17-101, *et seq.*, as amended from time to time.

**\"Adjusted Capital Account\"** means, with respect to each Partner, the balance, if any, in such Partner\'s Capital Account as of the end of the relevant fiscal year, after giving effect to the following adjustments: (a) such Capital Account shall be increased by (i) the amount of any obligations of such Partner to restore a deficit balance in its Capital Account, and (ii) the amount of any obligations of such Partner that are treated as obligations to restore a deficit balance in its Capital Account pursuant to Treasury Regulation § 1.704-1(b)(2)(ii)(c); and (b) such Capital Account shall be decreased by the items described in Treasury Regulation § 1.704-1(b)(2)(ii)(d)(4), (5), and (6). The foregoing definition of Adjusted Capital Account is intended to comply with the provisions of Treasury Regulation § 1.704-1(b)(2)(ii)(d) and shall be interpreted consistently therewith.

**\"Advisory Committee\"** means the committee of Limited Partner representatives established and maintained pursuant to Section 9.1.

**\"Affiliate\"** means, with respect to any Person, any other Person that, directly or indirectly, Controls, is Controlled by, or is under common Control with, such Person. For purposes of this definition, \"Control\" means the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management or policies of a Person, whether through the ownership of voting securities, by contract, or otherwise. For the avoidance of doubt, a portfolio company of the Partnership shall not be deemed an Affiliate of the General Partner solely by reason of the Partnership\'s investment in such portfolio company, unless the General Partner otherwise Controls such portfolio company.

**\"Agreement\"** means this Amended and Restated Agreement of Limited Partnership, as it may be further amended, restated, supplemented, or otherwise modified from time to time in accordance with the terms hereof.

**\"Benefit Plan Investor\"** means (a) an \"employee benefit plan\" (as defined in Section 3(3) of ERISA) that is subject to the provisions of Title I of ERISA, (b) a \"plan\" as defined in and subject to Section 4975 of the Code, and (c) any entity whose underlying assets include \"plan assets\" by reason of investment by one or more of the foregoing entities pursuant to 29 C.F.R. § 2510.3-101, as modified by Section 3(42) of ERISA, and the regulations promulgated by the United States Department of Labor thereunder.

**\"Business Day\"** means any day other than a Saturday, a Sunday, or a day on which commercial banks in New York, New York or Chicago, Illinois are authorized or required by law to close.

**\"Capital Account\"** means, with respect to each Partner, the account established and maintained for such Partner in accordance with the provisions of Section 4.1.

**\"Capital Call\"** or **\"Drawdown Notice\"** means a written notice issued by the General Partner to Partners pursuant to Section 3.2, requiring the Partners to make Capital Contributions to the Partnership.

**\"Capital Commitment\"** means, with respect to each Partner, the total amount of capital that such Partner has committed to contribute to the Partnership, as set forth opposite such Partner\'s name on Schedule A (as amended from time to time), net of any reductions effected pursuant to Section 3.3 or otherwise in accordance with this Agreement.

**\"Capital Contribution\"** means, with respect to each Partner, the aggregate amount of cash (or the agreed fair market value of property other than cash) actually contributed by such Partner to the capital of the Partnership in respect of its Capital Commitment.

**\"Carried Interest\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 8.3(d).

**\"Certificate of Limited Partnership\"** means the Certificate of Limited Partnership of the Partnership filed with the Secretary of State of the State of Delaware on August 1, 2024, as the same may be amended, restated, or supplemented from time to time.

**\"Closing\"** means the Initial Closing, any Subsequent Closing, or the Final Closing, as applicable. Each Closing shall occur on a date determined by the General Partner.

**\"Code\"** means the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended from time to time, and any successor thereto. References to specific sections of the Code shall be deemed to include references to any successor provisions.

**\"Custodian\"** means Clearstone Trust Company, a national trust company organized under the laws of the United States, or any successor custodian appointed by the General Partner from time to time.

**\"Defaulting Partner\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 3.3.

**\"ERISA\"** means the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974, as amended from time to time, and the regulations promulgated thereunder.

**\"Equalization Contribution\"** means, with respect to a Subsequent Closing Partner, the amount of Capital Contributions that such Partner would have been required to contribute had it been admitted as a Partner at the Initial Closing and participated in all prior Capital Calls, as determined by the General Partner pursuant to Section 3.2(c).

**\"Equalization Interest\"** means the interest payable by a Subsequent Closing Partner pursuant to Section 3.2(c), calculated at the Equalization Interest Rate on each deemed Capital Contribution from the date of the applicable prior Capital Call through and including the date of the applicable Subsequent Closing.

**\"Equalization Interest Rate\"** means eight percent (8%) per annum, calculated on a simple (non-compounded) interest basis based on a 365-day year.

**\"Final Closing\"** means the last Closing at which additional Limited Partners are admitted to the Partnership, which shall occur no later than eighteen (18) months after the Initial Closing (i.e., no later than March 15, 2026), unless such date is extended with the consent of the Advisory Committee.

**\"Fiscal Year\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 14.11.

**\"Fund Administrator\"** means Oakmere Fund Services LLC, a Delaware limited liability company, or any successor fund administrator engaged by the General Partner from time to time.

**\"Fund Counsel\"** means Bridgeford Hollis LLP, or any successor legal counsel to the Partnership or the General Partner as designated by the General Partner from time to time.

**\"General Partner\"** or **\"GP\"** means Whitmore Capital Management LLC, a Delaware limited liability company, in its capacity as the general partner of the Partnership, and any successor general partner admitted in accordance with this Agreement.

**\"GP Commitment\"** means the Capital Commitment of the General Partner to the Partnership, which shall be not less than Fifteen Million Dollars (\$15,000,000).

**\"Hard Cap\"** means One Billion Seven Hundred Fifty Million Dollars (\$1,750,000,000) in aggregate Capital Commitments.

**\"Indemnified Party\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 9.5.

**\"Initial Closing\"** means September 15, 2024, being the date on which the first Limited Partners were admitted to the Partnership and the initial Capital Contributions were received.

**\"Initial Closing Partners\"** means the Partners admitted to the Partnership at the Initial Closing, as set forth on Schedule A.

**\"Investment Company Act\"** means the Investment Company Act of 1940, as amended from time to time, and the rules and regulations promulgated thereunder.

**\"Investment Period\"** means the period commencing on the Initial Closing (September 15, 2024) and ending on the fifth (5th) anniversary thereof (September 15, 2029), subject to early termination or suspension as provided in Section 2.7 and Section 9.4.

**\"Key Person Event\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 9.4(b).

**\"Key Persons\"** means Marcus J. Whitmore and Priya S. Mehta.

**\"Limited Partner\"** or **\"LP\"** means each Person who is admitted to the Partnership as a limited partner in accordance with this Agreement and who has not ceased to be a limited partner of the Partnership.

**\"Management Fee\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 7.1.

**\"MFN Election\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 12.3(c).

**\"MFN Eligible Partner\"** means a Limited Partner whose Capital Commitment equals or exceeds Fifty Million Dollars (\$50,000,000).

**\"MFN Notice\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 12.3(b).

**\"Net Asset Value\"** or **\"NAV\"** means, as of any date of determination, the aggregate fair market value of all Partnership assets, as determined by the Fund Administrator in accordance with the Partnership\'s valuation policies and U.S. generally accepted accounting principles, less all liabilities of the Partnership as of such date.

**\"Net Loss\"** means, for any Fiscal Year or other relevant period, the excess, if any, of items of Partnership loss and deduction over items of Partnership income and gain for such period, as determined in accordance with Section 703(a) of the Code (with the adjustments required by Treasury Regulation § 1.704-1(b)(2)(iv)).

**\"Net Profit\"** means, for any Fiscal Year or other relevant period, the excess, if any, of items of Partnership income and gain over items of Partnership loss and deduction for such period, as determined in accordance with Section 703(a) of the Code (with the adjustments required by Treasury Regulation § 1.704-1(b)(2)(iv)).

**\"OFAC\"** means the Office of Foreign Assets Control of the United States Department of the Treasury.

**\"Organizational Expenses\"** means the costs and expenses incurred in connection with the formation and organization of the Partnership and the offering of interests therein, including legal fees, accounting fees, filing fees, printing costs, travel expenses, and all other expenses directly attributable to the formation, registration, and initial organization of the Partnership; *provided*, that Organizational Expenses shall be subject to the cap set forth in Section 7.2.

**\"Partner\"** means the General Partner or any Limited Partner.

**\"Partnership\"** means Whitmore Capital Partners VII, LP, a Delaware limited partnership.

**\"Partnership Representative\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 14.10.

**\"Person\"** means an individual, corporation, limited liability company, partnership, limited partnership, trust, estate, association, joint venture, unincorporated organization, governmental authority, or any other entity.

**\"Portfolio Company\"** means any entity in which the Partnership holds an investment.

**\"Qualified Purchaser\"** means a \"qualified purchaser\" as defined in Section 2(a)(51) of the Investment Company Act of 1940, as amended.

**\"Schedule A\"** means the Schedule of Partners and Capital Commitments attached hereto as Schedule A, as such schedule may be amended and updated from time to time by the General Partner to reflect the admission of additional Partners, changes in Capital Commitments, or transfers of interests in the Partnership.

**\"Side Letter\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 12.2.

**\"Subscription Agreement\"** means the subscription agreement or commitment agreement executed and delivered by each Limited Partner in connection with its admission to the Partnership, in the form attached hereto as Exhibit A (or such other form as the General Partner may prescribe from time to time).

**\"Subsequent Closing\"** means any Closing held after the Initial Closing at which additional Limited Partners are admitted to the Partnership.

**\"Subsequent Closing Partner\"** means any Partner admitted to the Partnership at a Subsequent Closing.

**\"Target Fund Size\"** means One Billion Five Hundred Million Dollars (\$1,500,000,000) in aggregate Capital Commitments.

**\"Tax Matters Partner\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 14.10.

**\"Term\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 2.6.

**\"Transfer\"** means any direct or indirect sale, assignment, pledge, hypothecation, encumbrance, gift, bequest, or other disposition (whether voluntary or involuntary, by operation of law or otherwise) of all or any portion of a Partner\'s interest in the Partnership.

**\"Treasury Regulations\"** means the final, temporary, or proposed income tax regulations promulgated under the Code, as such regulations may be amended from time to time (including corresponding provisions of succeeding regulations).

**\"U.S. GAAP\"** means United States generally accepted accounting principles, as in effect from time to time.

**[ARTICLE II --- ORGANIZATION, PURPOSE, AND TERM]{.underline}**

**Section 2.1 --- Formation and Continuation.**

The Partnership was formed as a limited partnership under and pursuant to the provisions of the Delaware Revised Uniform Limited Partnership Act (the \"Act\") on August 1, 2024, by the filing of a Certificate of Limited Partnership with the Secretary of State of the State of Delaware, which was assigned File No. 7842319. The rights, duties, and liabilities of the Partners shall be as provided in the Act, except as otherwise provided in this Agreement. To the extent that any provision of this Agreement is inconsistent with any mandatory, non-waivable provision of the Act, such provision of the Act shall control. The Partnership shall continue in existence until dissolved in accordance with the provisions of Article X.

**Section 2.2 --- Name.**

The name of the Partnership is \"Whitmore Capital Partners VII, LP.\" The General Partner may use such trade names or \"doing business as\" names as it may determine in its sole discretion for the conduct of the Partnership\'s business, and may change the name of the Partnership upon written notice to all Partners.

**Section 2.3 --- Registered Office and Registered Agent.**

The registered office of the Partnership in the State of Delaware is located at 1301 Market Street, Wilmington, Delaware 19801. The registered agent of the Partnership for service of process at such address is Statehouse Registered Agents, Inc. The General Partner may change the registered office or registered agent from time to time by filing the appropriate amendment to the Certificate of Limited Partnership.

**Section 2.4 --- Principal Office.**

The principal office of the Partnership is located at 400 Lexington Tower, Suite 2200, Chicago, Illinois 60601. The General Partner may change the location of the Partnership\'s principal office from time to time upon not less than thirty (30) days\' prior written notice to all Partners.

**Section 2.5 --- Purpose.**

The purpose of the Partnership is: (a) to make equity and equity-related investments (including, without limitation, investments in common stock, preferred stock, convertible securities, warrants, options, mezzanine securities, and other debt instruments with equity features) in middle-market companies, primarily in the healthcare services and technology-enabled services sectors; (b) to hold, manage, operate, monitor, and dispose of such investments and to exercise all rights in connection therewith; (c) to make temporary investments pending the deployment of capital as provided in Section 5.4; and (d) to engage in any and all activities incidental, necessary, or appropriate to accomplish the foregoing purposes. The Partnership shall not engage in any activity that would cause it to be treated as a \"publicly traded partnership\" taxable as a corporation under Section 7704 of the Code. The Partnership shall not engage in any activity that would require it to register as an investment company under the Investment Company Act.

**Section 2.6 --- Term.**

The term of the Partnership (the \"Term\") commenced on August 1, 2024 (the date of formation) and shall continue until the tenth (10th) anniversary of the Initial Closing (September 15, 2034), unless the Partnership is earlier dissolved pursuant to the provisions of Article X. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the General Partner may extend the Term for up to two (2) successive one-year periods beyond the initial ten-year term, as follows: (a) the first one-year extension (through September 15, 2035) may be effected at the General Partner\'s sole discretion, upon written notice to all Partners given not less than ninety (90) days prior to the then-scheduled expiration of the Term; and (b) the second one-year extension (through September 15, 2036) may be effected only with the prior written consent of the Advisory Committee, upon written notice to all Partners given not less than ninety (90) days prior to the then-scheduled expiration of the Term (as extended pursuant to clause (a)). Any extension of the Term shall be solely for the purpose of orderly liquidation and disposition of remaining investments and shall not permit the Partnership to make new investments.

**Section 2.7 --- Investment Period.**

The Investment Period commences on the Initial Closing (September 15, 2024) and ends on the fifth (5th) anniversary thereof (September 15, 2029), unless earlier terminated in accordance with this Section 2.7 or Section 9.4. The Investment Period shall terminate early upon: (a) the occurrence of a Key Person Event as defined in Section 9.4, subject to the reinstatement provisions set forth therein; or (b) a vote of Limited Partners holding seventy-five percent (75%) or more of aggregate Capital Commitments to terminate the Investment Period, delivered to the General Partner in writing. Upon the expiration or termination of the Investment Period, the General Partner shall not make any new investments on behalf of the Partnership, except that the General Partner may continue to (i) make follow-on investments in existing Portfolio Companies in an aggregate amount not to exceed fifteen percent (15%) of total Capital Commitments, (ii) fund Capital Calls for Management Fees, Partnership expenses, and other obligations of the Partnership, and (iii) complete investments for which binding commitments were entered into prior to the expiration or termination of the Investment Period.

**[ARTICLE III --- CAPITAL COMMITMENTS AND CAPITAL CONTRIBUTIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 3.1 --- Capital Commitments; Closings.**

**Section 3.1(a) --- Initial Closing.** The Initial Closing occurred on September 15, 2024. Each Limited Partner admitted at the Initial Closing has made a Capital Commitment in the amount set forth opposite such Partner\'s name on Schedule A (as in effect on the date of the Initial Closing). The aggregate Capital Commitments of all Partners as of the Initial Closing, together with the GP Commitment, are as set forth on Schedule A. Each Initial Closing Partner shall be deemed a party to this Agreement as of the Initial Closing and shall be bound by all of the terms and conditions hereof.

**Section 3.1(b) --- Subsequent Closings.** The General Partner may, in its sole discretion, admit additional Limited Partners to the Partnership at one or more Subsequent Closings at any time on or before the date that is eighteen (18) months after the Initial Closing (i.e., March 15, 2026), which date constitutes the deadline for the Final Closing. No Subsequent Closing shall be held after the Final Closing deadline, unless such deadline is extended by the Advisory Committee. The General Partner shall determine the timing and terms of each Subsequent Closing and shall have the sole authority to accept or reject any proposed Capital Commitment. Each Subsequent Closing Partner shall execute a Subscription Agreement in the form attached hereto as Exhibit A (or such other form as the General Partner may prescribe) and shall execute a counterpart signature page or joinder agreement in the form attached hereto as Exhibit B, and upon execution thereof and satisfaction of the conditions set forth in Section 11.2, shall become bound by this Agreement as if an original party hereto. The General Partner shall update Schedule A following each Subsequent Closing to reflect the additional Limited Partners and their Capital Commitments.

**Section 3.1(c) --- Hard Cap.** Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, the aggregate Capital Commitments of all Partners (including the GP Commitment) shall not exceed One Billion Seven Hundred Fifty Million Dollars (\$1,750,000,000) (the \"Hard Cap\"). The General Partner shall not admit any additional Limited Partners or accept any increase in Capital Commitments if such admission or increase would cause the aggregate Capital Commitments to exceed the Hard Cap. Any purported admission or Capital Commitment increase that would result in aggregate Capital Commitments exceeding the Hard Cap shall be void and of no effect unless the General Partner reduces such Capital Commitment to an amount that would not cause the Hard Cap to be exceeded.

**Section 3.1(d) --- Target Fund Size; Advisory Committee Notification.** The Target Fund Size is One Billion Five Hundred Million Dollars (\$1,500,000,000). If at any time the aggregate Capital Commitments of all Partners (including the GP Commitment) exceed the Target Fund Size, the General Partner shall promptly notify the Advisory Committee in writing of such excess, including the amount by which the Target Fund Size has been exceeded and the identities and Capital Commitments of the Partners admitted at the applicable Closing. The General Partner shall provide such notification not later than five (5) Business Days following the Closing at which the Target Fund Size is first exceeded. For the avoidance of doubt, exceeding the Target Fund Size shall not require Advisory Committee consent, provided that the Hard Cap is not exceeded. The Advisory Committee shall have the right to discuss with the General Partner the implications of exceeding the Target Fund Size, including the impact on the General Partner\'s investment strategy and portfolio construction, but the General Partner shall not be required to obtain Advisory Committee approval to proceed with fundraising beyond the Target Fund Size.

**Section 3.1(e) --- GP Commitment.** The General Partner shall make a Capital Commitment to the Partnership of not less than Fifteen Million Dollars (\$15,000,000) (the \"GP Commitment\"). The GP Commitment shall be funded on the same terms and at the same times as Capital Contributions of the Limited Partners, and the General Partner shall participate in all Capital Calls on a pro rata basis in respect of its GP Commitment. The General Partner may satisfy a portion of the GP Commitment through the waiver or deferral of Management Fees, to the extent permitted by applicable law.

**Section 3.2 --- Capital Contributions.**

**Section 3.2(a) --- Capital Calls Generally.** During the Investment Period (and, after the Investment Period, for the limited purposes described in Section 2.7), the General Partner may draw down Capital Commitments by delivering a Drawdown Notice (a \"Capital Call\") to each Partner. Each Capital Call shall specify: (i) the aggregate amount of Capital Contributions being called; (ii) each Partner\'s pro rata share of the Capital Call; (iii) the purpose or purposes for which the Capital Contributions are being called (e.g., investment, Management Fees, Partnership expenses, reserves); and (iv) the date on which such Capital Contributions are due (the \"Contribution Date\"), which date shall not be less than ten (10) Business Days after delivery of the Capital Call. Capital Calls shall be made pro rata based on each Partner\'s unfunded Capital Commitment (determined as of the date of the Capital Call) relative to the aggregate unfunded Capital Commitments of all Partners, unless otherwise specified herein. The General Partner shall deliver Capital Calls in the form set forth on Exhibit C or in such other form as the General Partner may determine from time to time.

**Section 3.2(b) --- Limitations on Capital Calls.** No Capital Call shall require any Partner to contribute amounts in excess of such Partner\'s unfunded Capital Commitment as of the date of such Capital Call. After the expiration or termination of the Investment Period, the General Partner may make Capital Calls solely for the following purposes: (i) to fund follow-on investments permitted under Section 2.7; (ii) to pay Management Fees then due and payable; (iii) to pay Partnership expenses and liabilities (including indemnification obligations); (iv) to fund obligations arising from investments committed to during the Investment Period; and (v) to establish or replenish reserves. The aggregate Capital Contributions called after the expiration of the Investment Period (other than for Management Fees and Partnership expenses) shall not exceed twenty percent (20%) of total Capital Commitments without the consent of a majority in interest of the Limited Partners.

**Section 3.2(c) --- Equalization Contributions and Equalization Interest.** Each Subsequent Closing Partner shall, at the applicable Subsequent Closing, make a Capital Contribution to the Partnership equal to the amount that such Subsequent Closing Partner would have been required to contribute to the Partnership had it been admitted as a Partner at the Initial Closing and participated in all Capital Calls made prior to the date of the applicable Subsequent Closing (the \"Equalization Contribution\"), calculated pro rata based on such Subsequent Closing Partner\'s Capital Commitment relative to the total Capital Commitments that would have been outstanding had such Subsequent Closing Partner been admitted at the Initial Closing. The General Partner shall determine the amount of each Subsequent Closing Partner\'s Equalization Contribution and shall notify such Partner of such amount in the Subscription Agreement or in a separate notice delivered prior to the applicable Subsequent Closing.

In addition to the Equalization Contribution, each Subsequent Closing Partner shall pay an amount equal to interest on each deemed Capital Contribution (i.e., on the portion of the Equalization Contribution attributable to each prior Capital Call) calculated at the Equalization Interest Rate (eight percent (8%) per annum, simple interest, based on a 365-day year) from the date of the applicable prior Capital Call through and including the date of the applicable Subsequent Closing (the \"Equalization Interest\"). For the avoidance of doubt, the Equalization Interest Rate of eight percent (8%) per annum shall be computed on a simple (non-compounded) basis, and the number of days for each calculation shall be determined from and including the date of each prior Capital Call through and including the date of the applicable Subsequent Closing.

Equalization Interest shall be paid to the Partners who funded the corresponding prior Capital Calls, pro rata in accordance with their respective Capital Contributions to such Capital Calls, as a preferential distribution (and not as a return of capital or allocation of income). The receipt of Equalization Interest by existing Partners shall not reduce such Partners\' unfunded Capital Commitments or otherwise affect their Capital Accounts. The portion of the Equalization Contribution (excluding Equalization Interest) shall be allocated among Partners as follows: (i) the pro rata share attributable to Partnership investments shall be treated as a Capital Contribution to the Partnership in respect of such Subsequent Closing Partner\'s Capital Commitment; and (ii) the pro rata share attributable to Management Fees and Partnership expenses shall be treated as a contribution toward the Subsequent Closing Partner\'s allocable share of such fees and expenses.

**Section 3.2(d) --- Payment Mechanics.** All Capital Contributions shall be made by wire transfer of immediately available funds to the bank account designated by the General Partner in the applicable Capital Call notice (or, if so designated in the Capital Call notice or the applicable Subscription Agreement, to an escrow account maintained at Pinnacle National Bank). Partners admitted at a Subsequent Closing shall wire their Equalization Contribution and Equalization Interest, together with any Capital Contribution called at the time of such Subsequent Closing, to the account designated by the General Partner in the applicable Subscription Agreement or Capital Call notice. Wire instructions shall be provided in each Capital Call notice and shall also be set forth in the Subscription Agreement.

**Section 3.3 --- Default Provisions.**

**(a) Default.** If any Partner (a \"Defaulting Partner\") fails to fund all or any portion of its Capital Contribution within ten (10) Business Days after the Contribution Date specified in the applicable Capital Call (or, in the case of a Subsequent Closing Partner, within ten (10) Business Days after the applicable Subsequent Closing date), the General Partner may, in its sole discretion, declare such Partner to be in default.

**(b) Notice and Cure Period.** Prior to exercising any remedies under this Section 3.3, the General Partner shall deliver written notice (a \"Default Notice\") to the Defaulting Partner specifying the amount of the unfunded Capital Contribution and the date by which such amount was due. The Defaulting Partner shall have a period of five (5) Business Days following receipt of the Default Notice (the \"Cure Period\") within which to cure such default by delivering the required Capital Contribution in full, together with interest thereon at a rate of twelve percent (12%) per annum from the original Contribution Date through the date of payment.

**(c) Remedies.** If the Defaulting Partner fails to cure the default within the Cure Period, the General Partner may, in its sole discretion, exercise one or more of the following remedies:

> \(i\) **Forfeiture.** The Defaulting Partner shall forfeit up to fifty percent (50%) of its Capital Account balance as of the date of default, which forfeited amount shall be allocated among the non-defaulting Partners pro rata in accordance with their respective Capital Commitments;
>
> \(ii\) **Forced Sale.** The General Partner may cause the Defaulting Partner\'s interest in the Partnership to be sold to the non-defaulting Partners or to third parties designated by the General Partner, at a price equal to eighty percent (80%) of the fair market value of such interest (as determined by the General Partner in good faith), with the proceeds of such sale applied first to satisfy any obligations of the Defaulting Partner to the Partnership and any remaining proceeds paid to the Defaulting Partner;
>
> \(iii\) **Exclusion from Distributions.** The Defaulting Partner shall not be entitled to receive any distributions from the Partnership until such time as the default is cured in full and all interest and penalties have been paid;
>
> \(iv\) **Reduction of Unfunded Commitment.** The Defaulting Partner\'s unfunded Capital Commitment shall be reduced to zero, and the Defaulting Partner shall not be called upon to make any further Capital Contributions; and
>
> \(v\) **Other Remedies.** The General Partner may pursue any other remedies available at law or in equity, including the right to seek specific performance or damages.

**(d) Non-Exclusive Remedies.** The remedies set forth in this Section 3.3 are not exclusive and are in addition to any other rights or remedies available to the Partnership, the General Partner, or any non-defaulting Partner under this Agreement, at law, or in equity. No failure or delay on the part of the General Partner in exercising any right or remedy hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof.

**Section 3.4 --- Return of Excess Contributions.**

If the General Partner determines that Capital Contributions previously called from the Partners exceed the amounts required by the Partnership for the purposes for which such Capital Contributions were called, the General Partner may, in its sole discretion, return such excess Capital Contributions to the Partners, pro rata in accordance with each Partner\'s share of such Capital Contributions. Any amounts returned to Partners pursuant to this Section 3.4 shall restore such Partners\' unfunded Capital Commitments by the amount so returned, and such Partners shall remain subject to future Capital Calls in respect of such restored amounts. The General Partner shall provide written notice to the Partners of any return of excess Capital Contributions under this Section, specifying the amount returned and the effect on each Partner\'s unfunded Capital Commitment.

**[ARTICLE IV --- ALLOCATIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 4.1 --- Capital Accounts.**

A Capital Account shall be established and maintained for each Partner in accordance with the provisions of Treasury Regulation § 1.704-1(b)(2)(iv). Each Partner\'s Capital Account shall be increased by: (a) the amount of cash contributed by such Partner to the Partnership; (b) the fair market value of any property contributed by such Partner to the Partnership (net of liabilities secured by such property that the Partnership is considered to assume or take subject to under Section 752 of the Code); and (c) allocations to such Partner of Net Profit and items of income and gain (including income and gain exempt from tax). Each Partner\'s Capital Account shall be decreased by: (x) the amount of cash distributed to such Partner by the Partnership; (y) the fair market value of any property distributed to such Partner by the Partnership (net of liabilities secured by such property that the Partner is considered to assume or take subject to under Section 752 of the Code); and (z) allocations to such Partner of Net Loss and items of loss, deduction, and expense (including expenditures described in Section 705(a)(2)(B) of the Code). Upon the admission of a new Partner to the Partnership (including at any Subsequent Closing), the Capital Accounts of existing Partners shall be adjusted to reflect a revaluation (\"book-up\") of Partnership assets to their fair market values, with the resulting unrealized gain or loss allocated among the existing Partners in accordance with their respective sharing ratios immediately prior to such admission, all in accordance with Treasury Regulation § 1.704-1(b)(2)(iv)(f).

**Section 4.2 --- Allocations of Net Profit and Net Loss.**

**(a) Allocations of Net Profit.** Net Profit for each Fiscal Year (or other relevant period) shall be allocated among the Partners in the following order and priority:

> \(i\) First, to each Partner having a deficit balance in its Capital Account (after giving effect to all contributions, distributions, and allocations for such period other than allocations under this Section 4.2(a)(i)), in proportion to and to the extent of such deficit, until such deficit is reduced to zero;
>
> \(ii\) Second, to all Partners in a manner consistent with the distribution waterfall set forth in Section 8.3, such that the cumulative allocations of Net Profit to each Partner over the life of the Partnership are proportionate to the cumulative distributions such Partner would receive under Section 8.3 if all Partnership assets were sold at their book values and the proceeds were distributed in accordance with Section 8.3.

**(b) Allocations of Net Loss.** Net Loss for each Fiscal Year (or other relevant period) shall be allocated among the Partners pro rata in accordance with their respective Capital Commitments; *provided*, *however*, that no allocation of Net Loss shall be made to any Partner to the extent that such allocation would cause such Partner to have a negative Adjusted Capital Account balance at the end of such Fiscal Year. Any Net Loss that cannot be allocated to a Partner by reason of the preceding sentence shall be allocated to the other Partners pro rata in accordance with their respective Capital Commitments (subject to the same limitation), and any Net Loss that cannot be allocated to any Partner shall be allocated to the General Partner.

**Section 4.3 --- Regulatory Allocations.**

Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 4.2, the following allocations shall be made in the following order of priority:

**(a) Qualified Income Offset.** If any Partner unexpectedly receives an adjustment, allocation, or distribution described in Treasury Regulation § 1.704-1(b)(2)(ii)(d)(4), (5), or (6) that causes or increases a deficit in such Partner\'s Adjusted Capital Account, items of Partnership income and gain shall be specially allocated to such Partner in an amount and manner sufficient to eliminate, to the extent required by such Treasury Regulations, such deficit as quickly as possible. This Section 4.3(a) is intended to comply with the \"qualified income offset\" requirement of Treasury Regulation § 1.704-1(b)(2)(ii)(d) and shall be interpreted consistently therewith.

**(b) Minimum Gain Chargeback.** If there is a net decrease in Partnership minimum gain (as determined under Treasury Regulation § 1.704-2(b)(2)) during any Fiscal Year, each Partner shall be allocated items of income and gain for such Fiscal Year (and, if necessary, for subsequent Fiscal Years) in proportion to, and to the extent of, such Partner\'s share of the net decrease in Partnership minimum gain, as determined under Treasury Regulation § 1.704-2(g). This Section 4.3(b) is intended to comply with the minimum gain chargeback requirement of Treasury Regulation § 1.704-2(f) and shall be interpreted consistently therewith.

**(c) Partner Nonrecourse Debt Minimum Gain Chargeback.** If there is a net decrease in partner nonrecourse debt minimum gain (as determined under Treasury Regulation § 1.704-2(i)(3)) attributable to a partner nonrecourse debt during any Fiscal Year, each Partner who has a share of the partner nonrecourse debt minimum gain attributable to such partner nonrecourse debt shall be allocated items of income and gain for such Fiscal Year (and, if necessary, for subsequent Fiscal Years) in an amount equal to such Partner\'s share of the net decrease in partner nonrecourse debt minimum gain, as determined under Treasury Regulation § 1.704-2(i)(4). This Section 4.3(c) is intended to comply with the partner nonrecourse debt minimum gain chargeback requirement of Treasury Regulation § 1.704-2(i)(4) and shall be interpreted consistently therewith.

**(d) Section 704(c) Allocations.** In accordance with Section 704(c) of the Code and Treasury Regulation § 1.704-1(b)(2)(iv)(f)(4), income, gain, loss, and deduction with respect to any property contributed to the Partnership (or with respect to any property revalued on the Partnership\'s books in connection with the admission of a new Partner or otherwise) shall, solely for tax purposes, be allocated among the Partners using the traditional method described in Treasury Regulation § 1.704-3(b), so as to take into account any variation between the adjusted tax basis and the book value of such property at the time of contribution or revaluation.

**(e) Curative Allocations.** The regulatory allocations set forth in Sections 4.3(a) through 4.3(d) (the \"Regulatory Allocations\") are intended to comply with certain requirements of Treasury Regulations under Section 704(b) of the Code. To the extent that the Regulatory Allocations cause allocations to any Partner that differ from the allocations otherwise intended under this Article IV, the General Partner is authorized to make curative allocations among the Partners so that the net amount of items allocated to each Partner shall, to the extent possible, be equal to the net amount that would have been allocated to each Partner if the Regulatory Allocations had not occurred.

**(f) Ordering.** The Regulatory Allocations set forth in this Section 4.3 shall be made in the order set forth herein, and shall be taken into account before any allocation under Section 4.2 for each Fiscal Year.

**Section 4.4 --- Tax Allocations.**

For federal, state, and local income tax purposes, each item of income, gain, loss, deduction, and credit recognized by the Partnership shall be allocated among the Partners in the same manner as the corresponding item is allocated for Capital Account purposes under this Article IV, subject to Section 704(c) of the Code and the principles of Treasury Regulation § 1.704-1(b). Any elections or other decisions relating to such allocations shall be made by the General Partner in a manner consistent with the provisions of this Agreement. Allocations pursuant to this Section 4.4 are solely for federal, state, and local income tax purposes and shall not affect, or in any way be taken into account in computing, any Partner\'s Capital Account or share of Net Profit, Net Loss, distributions, or any other item.

**[ARTICLE V --- PARTNERSHIP INVESTMENTS]{.underline}**

**Section 5.1 --- Investment Objective and Strategy.**

The Partnership\'s investment objective is to achieve long-term capital appreciation through equity and equity-related investments in middle-market companies, primarily in the healthcare services and technology-enabled services sectors. The General Partner shall have broad discretion in identifying, evaluating, structuring, negotiating, executing, monitoring, and managing the Partnership\'s investments, subject to the investment guidelines and restrictions set forth in this Article V and elsewhere in this Agreement. The General Partner shall endeavor to construct a diversified portfolio of investments consistent with the Partnership\'s investment objective, but shall not be required to invest any minimum amount or in any particular number of Portfolio Companies.

**Section 5.2 --- Investment Restrictions.**

The Partnership shall not, without the prior written consent of the Advisory Committee:

> \(a\) invest more than twenty percent (20%) of total Capital Commitments in any single Portfolio Company (including follow-on investments);
>
> \(b\) make investments primarily for the purpose of exercising control of a publicly traded company;
>
> \(c\) invest in real estate assets (other than real estate that is incidental to the operations of a Portfolio Company);
>
> \(d\) invest in commodities or commodity futures contracts;
>
> \(e\) make loans or extend credit to third parties, except in the ordinary course of the Partnership\'s investment activities (including, without limitation, bridge loans to Portfolio Companies);
>
> \(f\) engage in short selling of securities; or
>
> \(g\) invest more than ten percent (10%) of total Capital Commitments in non-U.S. Portfolio Companies.

Notwithstanding the foregoing, the General Partner may temporarily exceed the concentration limit set forth in clause (a) above in connection with an add-on acquisition or follow-on investment in an existing Portfolio Company, provided that the General Partner shall use commercially reasonable efforts to reduce such excess concentration within twelve (12) months.

**Section 5.3 --- Co-Investment.**

The General Partner may, in its sole discretion, offer co-investment opportunities to one or more Partners or to third parties in connection with any investment by the Partnership. Co-investment allocations shall be determined by the General Partner in its sole discretion, taking into account such factors as it deems appropriate, including the size and nature of the investment opportunity, each Partner\'s Capital Commitment, and any co-investment rights granted in Side Letters. Co-investment participations shall generally not be subject to Management Fees or Carried Interest, unless otherwise agreed in writing between the General Partner and the co-investing party.

**Section 5.4 --- Temporary Investments.**

Pending investment in Portfolio Companies or distribution to Partners, the Partnership\'s funds may be held in cash or invested in cash equivalents, United States government obligations, money market funds, certificates of deposit, repurchase agreements, investment-grade commercial paper, or other short-term, high-quality debt instruments, in each case as determined by the General Partner in its sole discretion.

**Section 5.5 --- Custody.**

The Partnership\'s securities and other assets shall be held by Clearstone Trust Company (the \"Custodian\") pursuant to the custody agreement between the Partnership and the Custodian dated as of August 20, 2024, or by such other qualified custodian as the General Partner may designate from time to time. The General Partner shall ensure that the Partnership\'s assets are maintained by a qualified custodian in compliance with Rule 206(4)-2 under the Investment Advisers Act of 1940.

**[ARTICLE VI --- REPRESENTATIONS, WARRANTIES, AND COVENANTS OF LIMITED PARTNERS]{.underline}**

**Section 6.1 --- Representations of Limited Partners.**

Each Limited Partner hereby represents and warrants to the Partnership and the General Partner, as of the date of its admission to the Partnership, that:

> \(a\) it is duly organized, validly existing, and in good standing under the laws of the jurisdiction of its formation or incorporation and has all requisite power and authority to execute, deliver, and perform its obligations under this Agreement and the Subscription Agreement;
>
> \(b\) the execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement and the Subscription Agreement by such Limited Partner have been duly authorized by all necessary action on the part of such Limited Partner, and no further consent or approval is required;
>
> \(c\) this Agreement and the Subscription Agreement constitute legal, valid, and binding obligations of such Limited Partner, enforceable against it in accordance with their terms, subject to applicable bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganization, moratorium, and similar laws affecting creditors\' rights generally and to general principles of equity; and
>
> \(d\) no consent, approval, authorization, or order of, or filing or registration with, any governmental authority or other Person is required for the execution, delivery, or performance by such Limited Partner of this Agreement or the Subscription Agreement, other than such consents, approvals, or filings that have been obtained or made prior to such Limited Partner\'s admission.

**Section 6.2 --- Qualified Purchaser Representation.**

Each Limited Partner hereby represents and warrants that it is a \"qualified purchaser\" as defined in Section 2(a)(51) of the Investment Company Act of 1940, as amended. Each Limited Partner acknowledges that the Partnership is relying on the exemption from registration provided by Section 3(c)(7) of the Investment Company Act and that such exemption requires that each beneficial owner of the Partnership\'s securities be a qualified purchaser. Each Limited Partner covenants that it shall not Transfer or otherwise dispose of its interest in the Partnership to any Person that is not a qualified purchaser.

**Section 6.3 --- Investment Representations.**

Each Limited Partner hereby represents and warrants that:

> \(a\) it is acquiring its interest in the Partnership for its own account, for investment purposes only, and not with a view to the distribution or resale thereof (in whole or in part) in violation of applicable securities laws;
>
> \(b\) it is an \"accredited investor\" as defined in Rule 501(a) of Regulation D promulgated under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended;
>
> \(c\) it has received and reviewed this Agreement, the Private Placement Memorandum (if any), and all other documents and information it considers necessary to make an informed investment decision;
>
> \(d\) it understands the risks associated with an investment in the Partnership, including the risk of loss of its entire investment, the illiquid nature of the interest, and the potential for extended holding periods; and
>
> \(e\) it has the financial ability to bear the economic risk of its investment in the Partnership, including the ability to afford a total loss of such investment.

**Section 6.4 --- AML/KYC Representations.**

Each Limited Partner hereby represents and warrants that:

> \(a\) it is not a Person (or owned or controlled by a Person) that is designated on any sanctions list maintained by the Office of Foreign Assets Control of the United States Department of the Treasury (\"OFAC\"), the United Nations Security Council, the European Union, or any other governmental authority, and it is not a Person that is otherwise the target of economic sanctions administered by any such authority;
>
> \(b\) it is not a \"prohibited person\" or \"blocked person\" under any applicable anti-money laundering law, including the USA PATRIOT Act of 2001, the Bank Secrecy Act, and related regulations;
>
> \(c\) the funds used by such Limited Partner to acquire its interest in the Partnership are not derived from, and will not constitute, directly or indirectly, the proceeds of any illegal activity; and
>
> \(d\) it will provide all documentation reasonably requested by the General Partner, the Fund Administrator, or their agents in connection with applicable anti-money laundering (\"AML\") and \"know your customer\" (\"KYC\") requirements, including (without limitation) government-issued identification, corporate formation documents, beneficial ownership certifications, and such other documentation as may be required under applicable law.

**Section 6.5 --- ERISA Representations.**

Each Limited Partner shall represent in its Subscription Agreement whether it is (or is acting on behalf of) a Benefit Plan Investor (as defined in Article I). Each Limited Partner that is a Benefit Plan Investor shall so indicate on its Subscription Agreement and shall provide such additional information as the General Partner may reasonably request in order to assess compliance with ERISA and the regulations promulgated thereunder, including the extent to which such Limited Partner\'s investment constitutes \"plan assets\" subject to the fiduciary responsibility provisions of Title I of ERISA or Section 4975 of the Code.

**Section 6.6 --- Tax Representations.**

Each Limited Partner hereby represents and warrants that it has obtained (or will obtain prior to its admission to the Partnership) independent tax advice regarding the tax consequences of its investment in the Partnership. Each Limited Partner shall provide the General Partner with a properly completed Internal Revenue Service Form W-9 (in the case of a U.S. person) or applicable Form W-8 series form (in the case of a non-U.S. person) prior to or concurrently with its admission to the Partnership, and shall provide updated forms as necessary. Each Limited Partner shall cooperate with the General Partner in connection with compliance with the Foreign Account Tax Compliance Act (\"FATCA\") and any applicable intergovernmental agreements related thereto, including the provision of all documentation and information reasonably requested by the General Partner.

**Section 6.7 --- Ongoing Covenants.**

Each Limited Partner hereby covenants and agrees that it shall promptly notify the General Partner in writing if any representation or warranty made by such Limited Partner in this Agreement or in its Subscription Agreement ceases to be true and correct in any material respect. Each Limited Partner shall maintain its status as a qualified purchaser (and, if applicable, an accredited investor) throughout the term of its investment in the Partnership. Each Limited Partner shall use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain its status under applicable law and regulations such that its continued investment in the Partnership does not adversely affect the Partnership\'s exemption from registration under the Investment Company Act.

**Section 6.8 --- ERISA Compliance; Benefit Plan Investor Limitation.**

The General Partner shall use commercially reasonable efforts to ensure that the aggregate Capital Commitments of all Benefit Plan Investors do not exceed twenty-four and ninety-nine hundredths percent (24.99%) of the total Capital Commitments of all Partners (including the GP Commitment) at any time. If the General Partner determines that the admission of a Limited Partner at any Closing would cause the Benefit Plan Investor percentage to exceed 24.99%, the General Partner may (a) reduce the Capital Commitment of such Limited Partner to the extent necessary to maintain compliance with this limitation, (b) decline to admit such Limited Partner, or (c) take such other action as the General Partner deems appropriate, in its sole discretion, to maintain compliance with this limitation. The General Partner shall calculate the Benefit Plan Investor percentage as of each Closing and shall include such calculation in the closing deliverables provided to the Fund Administrator and Fund Counsel.

For purposes of this Section 6.8, the \"Benefit Plan Investor percentage\" shall mean the aggregate Capital Commitments of all Partners that have represented in their Subscription Agreements that they are (or are acting on behalf of) Benefit Plan Investors, divided by the total Capital Commitments of all Partners (including the GP Commitment), expressed as a percentage. For the avoidance of doubt, the General Partner shall not be in violation of this Section 6.8 solely as a result of a Limited Partner\'s failure to disclose its Benefit Plan Investor status, provided that the General Partner has made reasonable inquiry and has no actual knowledge of such status. The General Partner shall rely on the representations and certifications provided by each Limited Partner in its Subscription Agreement with respect to such Limited Partner\'s Benefit Plan Investor status.

**[ARTICLE VII --- MANAGEMENT FEES AND EXPENSES]{.underline}**

**Section 7.1 --- Management Fee.**
