**(a) During the Investment Period.** During the Investment Period, the Management Fee shall be an amount equal to two percent (2.0%) per annum of the aggregate Capital Commitments of all Limited Partners (for the avoidance of doubt, excluding the GP Commitment). The Management Fee shall be payable quarterly in advance on the first Business Day of each calendar quarter, commencing on the first Business Day of the first full calendar quarter following the Initial Closing. The Management Fee for any partial quarter (including the quarter in which the Initial Closing occurs) shall be prorated based on the actual number of days remaining in such quarter divided by the total number of days in such quarter.

**(b) After the Investment Period.** Following the expiration or termination of the Investment Period, the Management Fee shall be reduced to an amount equal to one and one-half percent (1.5%) per annum of the aggregate invested capital of the Partnership, calculated at cost (net of write-downs approved by the General Partner and realized losses). The Management Fee after the Investment Period shall continue to be payable quarterly in advance on the first Business Day of each calendar quarter. The Management Fee for any partial quarter following the expiration or termination of the Investment Period shall be prorated.

**(c) General.** The Management Fee shall be borne solely by the Limited Partners and shall not be payable by the General Partner in respect of the GP Commitment. The Management Fee shall be funded through Capital Calls to the Limited Partners, and each Limited Partner\'s share of the Management Fee shall be determined pro rata based on its Capital Commitment (during the Investment Period) or its share of invested capital (after the Investment Period).

**Section 7.2 --- Organizational Expenses.**

The Partnership shall bear Organizational Expenses incurred in connection with the formation, organization, and initial offering of interests in the Partnership, up to a maximum aggregate amount of Two Million Five Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$2,500,000). Any Organizational Expenses in excess of such cap shall be borne by the General Partner and shall not be charged to or reimbursed by the Partnership. Organizational Expenses include, without limitation, legal fees and expenses of Fund Counsel and other counsel, accounting and auditing fees, filing and registration fees, printing and reproduction costs, travel expenses, and all other costs and expenses directly attributable to the formation, qualification, registration, and initial organization of the Partnership and the offering of interests therein.

**Section 7.3 --- Partnership Expenses.**

The Partnership shall bear all costs and expenses incurred in connection with the operation, administration, and management of the Partnership and its investments, including, without limitation: (a) accounting, auditing, and tax preparation fees; (b) legal fees and expenses (other than those constituting Organizational Expenses); (c) fees and expenses of the Fund Administrator (Oakmere Fund Services LLC); (d) fees and expenses of the Custodian (Clearstone Trust Company); (e) insurance premiums (including directors\' and officers\' liability insurance and errors and omissions insurance); (f) travel expenses reasonably incurred in connection with the evaluation, acquisition, monitoring, and disposition of investments; (g) broken-deal costs and expenses; (h) regulatory filing fees, including Form D filings and blue sky filings; (i) taxes, fees, and other governmental charges imposed on the Partnership; (j) all costs and expenses related to the acquisition, holding, financing, monitoring, restructuring, and disposition of investments, including brokerage commissions, finder\'s fees, and transaction costs; (k) costs of investor meetings and Advisory Committee meetings; and (l) costs of preparing and distributing reports, financial statements, Schedule K-1s, and other communications to the Partners.

**Section 7.4 --- Fee Offsets.**

The Management Fee shall be offset (reduced, but not below zero) by one hundred percent (100%) of the Partnership\'s allocable share of any transaction fees, break-up fees, monitoring fees, directors\' fees, consulting fees, advisory fees, or similar fees received by the General Partner or its Affiliates from Portfolio Companies or their affiliates in connection with investments by the Partnership. The General Partner shall report to the Limited Partners on an annual basis the aggregate amount of such fee offsets applied during the preceding Fiscal Year.

**[ARTICLE VIII --- DISTRIBUTIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 8.1 --- Timing and Form of Distributions.**

Distributions shall be made at such times and in such amounts as the General Partner, in its sole discretion, shall determine. Distributions shall be made in cash, unless the General Partner determines, with the prior written consent of the Advisory Committee, that a distribution in kind (of securities or other non-cash assets) is in the best interests of the Partners. Any distribution in kind shall be valued at fair market value as determined by the General Partner in good faith, and Partners shall be notified of such valuation. Distributions shall be made pro rata to Partners in accordance with their respective Capital Commitments, subject to the distribution waterfall set forth in Section 8.3.

**Section 8.2 --- Restrictions on Distributions.**

No distribution shall be made if such distribution would (a) violate the Act or any applicable law, (b) render the Partnership unable to meet its obligations as they become due in the ordinary course of business, or (c) cause a breach of any material agreement to which the Partnership is a party. The General Partner may withhold from distributions amounts required for reserves to pay or provide for taxes, liabilities, contingencies, or other obligations of the Partnership, in such amounts as the General Partner, in its sole discretion, deems reasonably necessary.

**Section 8.3 --- Distribution Waterfall.**

Distributions of Distributable Proceeds from the Partnership shall be made in the following order and priority:

**Section 8.3(a) --- Return of Capital.** First, one hundred percent (100%) to the Partners (pro rata in accordance with their respective Capital Contributions) until each Partner has received cumulative distributions equal to its aggregate Capital Contributions (including Equalization Contributions but excluding Equalization Interest paid pursuant to Section 3.2(c)).

**Section 8.3(b) --- Preferred Return.** Second, one hundred percent (100%) to the Partners (pro rata in accordance with their respective Capital Contributions) until each Partner has received cumulative distributions under this Section 8.3(a) and 8.3(b) sufficient to provide such Partner with a cumulative preferred return equal to an eight percent (8%) per annum internal rate of return on its net funded Capital Contributions, computed from the date of each Capital Contribution (and, in the case of Equalization Contributions, from the date of the applicable Subsequent Closing at which such Equalization Contribution was made) through the date of each distribution, taking into account all prior distributions to such Partner under Sections 8.3(a) and 8.3(b). For purposes of computing the preferred return, Capital Contributions returned to Partners pursuant to Section 3.4 shall be treated as distributions as of the date returned.

**Section 8.3(c) --- GP Catch-Up.** Third, one hundred percent (100%) to the General Partner until the General Partner has received, in the aggregate under this Section 8.3(c) and Section 8.3(d), an amount equal to twenty percent (20%) of the sum of all amounts distributed under Sections 8.3(b) and 8.3(c).

**Section 8.3(d) --- Carried Interest Split.** Fourth, eighty percent (80%) to the Partners (pro rata in accordance with their respective Capital Commitments) and twenty percent (20%) to the General Partner (such twenty percent (20%) allocation to the General Partner being referred to herein as the \"Carried Interest\").

**Section 8.4 --- Tax Distributions.**

Notwithstanding the foregoing, the General Partner may, in its sole discretion, make distributions to the Partners in advance of regular distributions under Section 8.3 to enable the Partners to satisfy their federal, state, and local tax obligations arising from allocations of Partnership income to such Partners (each, a \"Tax Distribution\"). Any Tax Distribution made to a Partner shall be treated as an advance against, and shall reduce, future distributions to which such Partner would otherwise be entitled under Section 8.3. The amount of any Tax Distribution shall be determined by the General Partner based on an assumed marginal tax rate deemed appropriate by the General Partner.

**Section 8.5 --- Clawback.**

Upon the dissolution and final liquidation of the Partnership (or at such earlier time as the General Partner may determine), if the General Partner has received cumulative distributions (including Carried Interest) in excess of the amount that the General Partner would have received if the distribution waterfall in Section 8.3 had been applied on a cumulative basis over the life of the Partnership (after giving effect to all investments, dispositions, expenses, and distributions), the General Partner shall return such excess (the \"Clawback Amount\") to the Partnership for distribution to the Limited Partners in accordance with their respective shares under Section 8.3. The Clawback Amount shall be net of taxes actually paid (or reasonably estimated to have been paid) by the General Partner on the distributed amounts, with the General Partner\'s assumed marginal tax rate deemed to be the highest combined federal and state income tax rate applicable to individuals in the relevant jurisdiction. The General Partner\'s clawback obligation under this Section 8.5 shall survive the termination and dissolution of the Partnership for a period of three (3) years. The General Partner may satisfy its clawback obligation by payment from its members personally, and each member of the General Partner shall be jointly and severally liable for the Clawback Amount (up to the amount of Carried Interest distributions received by such member, net of taxes).

**Section 8.6 --- Escrow for Distributions.**

The General Partner may, in its sole discretion, establish reserves or escrow accounts for contingent or anticipated liabilities of the Partnership, including potential indemnification obligations, pending litigation, taxes, and other contingencies, and may withhold distributions otherwise payable to the Partners in an amount sufficient to fund such reserves or escrow accounts. Amounts held in reserve or escrow shall be distributed to the Partners as soon as practicable after the General Partner determines, in its sole discretion, that such reserves or escrow amounts are no longer required.

**[ARTICLE IX --- MANAGEMENT; ADVISORY COMMITTEE; KEY PERSONS]{.underline}**

**Section 9.1 --- Advisory Committee.**

**Section 9.1(a) --- Establishment and Composition.** The General Partner hereby establishes an Advisory Committee, which shall consist of five (5) representatives of Limited Partners. The initial members of the Advisory Committee shall be appointed by the General Partner at or promptly following the Initial Closing. Members of the Advisory Committee shall serve at the pleasure of the General Partner; *provided*, that the General Partner shall endeavor to ensure that the Advisory Committee includes representatives from diverse categories of Limited Partners (e.g., public pension funds, endowments and foundations, insurance companies, and other institutional investors). No member of the Advisory Committee shall be required to be a Limited Partner, but each member shall be a duly authorized representative of a Limited Partner.

**Section 9.1(b) --- Appointment and Vacancies.** The General Partner shall appoint the initial members of the Advisory Committee and may fill vacancies from time to time, in its sole discretion. In the event that a seat on the Advisory Committee is vacant following any Subsequent Closing, the General Partner shall use commercially reasonable efforts to appoint a representative of a Limited Partner admitted at such Subsequent Closing to fill such vacancy within thirty (30) days of the applicable Closing. The Advisory Committee members as of the Initial Closing are:

> 1\. Rachel M. Torres, representative of Silvermark Capital Advisors;
>
> 2\. Kenneth W. Park, representative of Commonwealth Public Employees\' Retirement Fund;
>
> 3\. Diane L. Voss, representative of Graystone Foundation; and
>
> 4\. Two (2) additional seats to be filled from Limited Partners admitted at Subsequent Closings.

Any Advisory Committee member may resign at any time by delivering written notice to the General Partner, and the General Partner may remove any Advisory Committee member at any time, with or without cause, upon written notice to such member and the Limited Partner such member represents.

**Section 9.1(c) --- Functions of the Advisory Committee.** The Advisory Committee shall have the following functions and responsibilities:

> \(i\) review and approve or disapprove potential conflicts of interest presented to it by the General Partner, including any transaction between the Partnership and the General Partner or its Affiliates;
>
> \(ii\) provide or withhold consent to the second one-year extension of the Term pursuant to Section 2.6;
>
> \(iii\) receive notifications required to be delivered under this Agreement, including notification of the aggregate Capital Commitments exceeding the Target Fund Size pursuant to Section 3.1(d);
>
> \(iv\) provide or withhold consent to distributions in kind (non-cash distributions) pursuant to Section 8.1;
>
> \(v\) approve one or more replacement Key Persons designated by the General Partner following a Key Person Event pursuant to Section 9.4(c);
>
> \(vi\) provide or withhold consent to the extension of the Final Closing deadline pursuant to Section 3.1(b); and
>
> \(vii\) such other matters as are specified in this Agreement or as the General Partner may delegate to the Advisory Committee from time to time.

The Advisory Committee shall act in an advisory capacity only and shall not have the power or authority to manage or control the business and affairs of the Partnership or to bind the Partnership or the General Partner. The Advisory Committee shall not owe any fiduciary or other duty to any Partner or to the Partnership.

**Section 9.1(d) --- Meetings and Procedures.** The Advisory Committee shall meet at least two (2) times per calendar year, or more frequently as called by the General Partner or by a majority of the Advisory Committee members. Meetings may be held in person at the Partnership\'s principal office, by telephone conference, or by video conference. A majority of the members of the Advisory Committee then serving shall constitute a quorum for the transaction of business. The Advisory Committee may act by the affirmative vote of a majority of the members present at a meeting at which a quorum is present, or by written consent signed by a majority of the members then serving.

**Section 9.1(e) --- Liability of Advisory Committee Members.** No member of the Advisory Committee shall have any personal liability for any action taken or omitted to be taken in his or her capacity as a member of the Advisory Committee, except for liability arising from such member\'s own fraud, willful misconduct, or gross negligence. Members of the Advisory Committee shall be indemnified by the Partnership to the same extent as the Indemnified Parties are indemnified under Section 9.5. Service on the Advisory Committee shall not, by itself, make any member a fiduciary of the Partnership or of any Partner, and no member of the Advisory Committee shall be deemed to owe any fiduciary duty to the Partnership or any Partner by reason of such service.

**Section 9.2 --- Authority and Duties of the General Partner.**

The General Partner shall have full, exclusive, and complete authority, power, and discretion to manage, control, operate, and conduct the business and affairs of the Partnership and to do all things necessary or appropriate in furtherance thereof, without the need to obtain the consent or approval of any Limited Partner or any other Person, except as expressly provided in this Agreement. No Limited Partner shall take part in the management, operation, or control of the Partnership\'s business or affairs, transact any business in the Partnership\'s name, or have the power or authority to bind the Partnership or any other Partner, by virtue of being a Limited Partner. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the General Partner is hereby authorized to:

> \(a\) identify, evaluate, negotiate, structure, execute, monitor, and dispose of investments on behalf of the Partnership;
>
> \(b\) execute, deliver, and perform any and all documents, agreements, instruments, certificates, and other writings on behalf of the Partnership;
>
> \(c\) engage and compensate legal counsel, accountants, auditors, appraisers, investment bankers, consultants, administrators, custodians, and other advisors and service providers;
>
> \(d\) borrow money and issue guarantees on behalf of the Partnership (subject to the investment restrictions set forth in Section 5.2), and to pledge, hypothecate, or grant security interests in Partnership assets as security therefor;
>
> \(e\) open, maintain, and close bank and brokerage accounts in the name of the Partnership;
>
> \(f\) institute, prosecute, defend, settle, compromise, and dismiss lawsuits, arbitrations, and other proceedings on behalf of the Partnership;
>
> \(g\) acquire, hold, manage, and dispose of investments, including through wholly-owned subsidiaries, blocker corporations, and other holding entities;
>
> \(h\) make all elections, filings, and registrations on behalf of the Partnership under applicable federal, state, local, and foreign tax, securities, and regulatory laws, including all required filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission; and
>
> \(i\) take all other actions and exercise all other powers necessary, appropriate, or incidental to the management, operation, and conduct of the Partnership\'s business and affairs.

The General Partner is a registered investment adviser with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (CRD No. 298417, SEC File No. 801-79834) and shall maintain such registration throughout the Term, except to the extent exempt from such registration requirement. The General Partner shall cause the Partnership to comply with all applicable laws, regulations, and orders.

**Section 9.3 --- Delegation.**

The General Partner may delegate any of its ministerial, administrative, or investment management functions to its Affiliates, members, officers, employees, agents, or to third-party service providers, including (without limitation) the Fund Administrator (Oakmere Fund Services LLC) and the Custodian (Clearstone Trust Company). Any such delegation shall not relieve the General Partner of its obligations and liabilities hereunder, and the General Partner shall remain responsible for the acts and omissions of any Person to whom it delegates any of its duties or responsibilities. The General Partner shall exercise reasonable care in selecting and monitoring any delegates.

**Section 9.4 --- Key Persons.**

**(a) Identification.** The \"Key Persons\" of the Partnership are Marcus J. Whitmore (Founder and Chief Investment Officer of the General Partner) and Priya S. Mehta (Managing Director of the General Partner). Each Key Person shall devote substantially all of his or her business time and efforts to the affairs of the Partnership during the Investment Period. For purposes of this Section 9.4, \"substantially all\" means not less than seventy-five percent (75%) of each Key Person\'s business time, after giving effect to time devoted to other funds managed by the General Partner that are in their harvesting or wind-down phase and not actively making new investments. Each Key Person shall be permitted to engage in charitable, civic, educational, and passive personal investment activities, provided that such activities do not materially interfere with such Key Person\'s duties to the Partnership.

**(b) Key Person Event.** A \"Key Person Event\" shall occur if either Key Person (i) ceases to be actively engaged in the management of the Partnership\'s investments on a substantially full-time basis (as defined in clause (a) above); (ii) dies or becomes permanently disabled (meaning unable to perform his or her duties for a period of ninety (90) consecutive days or one hundred twenty (120) days in any twelve (12)-month period); or (iii) ceases to be employed by or affiliated with the General Partner or its Affiliates for any reason. Upon the occurrence of a Key Person Event, the Investment Period shall be automatically suspended (and no new investments or Capital Calls for new investments shall be made during such suspension), and the General Partner shall promptly (but in no event later than five (5) Business Days after the occurrence of such event) notify all Partners and the Advisory Committee of such Key Person Event.

**(c) Reinstatement.** Following a Key Person Event, the Investment Period may be reinstated if: (i) within one hundred eighty (180) days after the occurrence of such Key Person Event, the Advisory Committee approves one or more replacement individuals designated by the General Partner (which approval shall not be unreasonably withheld), and such approved replacement individuals assume the duties of the departing Key Person; or (ii) Limited Partners holding a majority in interest of Capital Commitments (excluding the General Partner) vote in writing to reinstate the Investment Period. If the Investment Period is not reinstated within such one hundred eighty (180)-day period, it shall be permanently terminated, and the Partnership shall proceed to wind down and dispose of its investments in an orderly manner over the remaining Term.

**(d) Certification at Each Closing.** The General Partner shall certify at each Closing that each Key Person continues to devote substantially all of his or her business time and efforts to the Partnership and that no Key Person Event has occurred or is reasonably expected to occur as of the date of such Closing. Such certification shall be delivered in the closing certificate or other closing deliverable provided in connection with such Closing. The General Partner acknowledges that the Limited Partners are relying on the continued involvement of each Key Person in making their investment decisions and funding their Capital Commitments.

**Section 9.5 --- Exculpation and Indemnification.**

**(a) Exculpation.** The General Partner, its members, officers, directors, employees, and agents (each, an \"Indemnified Party\") shall not be liable, responsible, or accountable in damages or otherwise to the Partnership or any Partner for any act performed or omission made by any of them in connection with the business or affairs of the Partnership, *provided* that such act or omission was performed or omitted in good faith and in a manner reasonably believed to be in, or not opposed to, the best interests of the Partnership, and *provided further* that the conduct of such Indemnified Party did not constitute fraud, willful misconduct, or gross negligence. The Indemnified Parties shall be entitled to rely upon the advice of legal counsel, accountants, appraisers, and other experts and professionals retained by the Partnership, and any act or omission taken or suffered in reliance upon such advice shall not constitute fraud, willful misconduct, or gross negligence.

**(b) Indemnification.** The Partnership shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless each Indemnified Party from and against any and all losses, claims, damages, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys\' fees and expenses, court costs, and amounts paid in settlement) (collectively, \"Losses\") arising out of, relating to, or in connection with any act performed or omission made by such Indemnified Party in connection with the business and affairs of the Partnership, *provided* that such act or omission was performed or omitted in good faith and in a manner reasonably believed to be in, or not opposed to, the best interests of the Partnership, and *provided further* that such Losses were not the result of the Indemnified Party\'s own fraud, willful misconduct, or gross negligence. The right to indemnification under this Section 9.5(b) shall include the right to advancement of expenses (including attorneys\' fees) incurred in defending any proceeding, subject to the delivery of an undertaking by the Indemnified Party to repay such amounts if it is ultimately determined that such Indemnified Party was not entitled to indemnification.

**(c) Insurance.** The General Partner may, in its sole discretion and at the expense of the Partnership, purchase and maintain insurance on behalf of the Indemnified Parties against any liability asserted against or incurred by them in connection with the Partnership\'s business and affairs, whether or not the Partnership would have the power to indemnify such Indemnified Parties against such liability under this Section 9.5.

**(d) Survival.** The provisions of this Section 9.5 shall survive the dissolution, liquidation, and termination of the Partnership and the withdrawal of any Partner.

**[ARTICLE X --- DISSOLUTION AND WINDING UP]{.underline}**

**Section 10.1 --- Events of Dissolution.**

The Partnership shall be dissolved upon the earliest to occur of:

> \(a\) the expiration of the Term (including any extensions thereof pursuant to Section 2.6);
>
> \(b\) a determination by the General Partner, with the prior written consent of the Advisory Committee, that the Partnership should be dissolved;
>
> \(c\) a vote by Limited Partners holding eighty percent (80%) or more of the aggregate Capital Commitments (excluding the GP Commitment) that the Partnership should be dissolved;
>
> \(d\) the entry of a decree of judicial dissolution of the Partnership under Section 17-802 of the Act; or
>
> \(e\) the occurrence of any event that makes it unlawful for the business of the Partnership to be carried on or for the Partners to carry on such business.

The withdrawal, removal, bankruptcy, dissolution, or incapacity of the General Partner shall not cause a dissolution of the Partnership if, within ninety (90) days thereafter, Limited Partners holding a majority of Capital Commitments elect in writing to continue the business of the Partnership and appoint a successor general partner.

**Section 10.2 --- Winding Up.**

Upon the dissolution of the Partnership, the General Partner (or, if the General Partner is unable or unwilling to act, a liquidating trustee appointed by the Advisory Committee) shall wind up the affairs of the Partnership. The General Partner or liquidating trustee shall: (i) liquidate and reduce to cash the assets of the Partnership in an orderly manner so as to maximize the value received therefor, which liquidation may occur over such period as the General Partner or liquidating trustee deems reasonable and appropriate; (ii) pay or discharge (or make reasonable provision for) all debts, liabilities, and obligations of the Partnership, including debts owed to the General Partner and its Affiliates (to the extent not subordinated), and distribute any reserves for contingent or unliquidated liabilities; and (iii) distribute the remaining assets (or proceeds thereof) to the Partners in accordance with Section 8.3 and Section 10.3.

**Section 10.3 --- Liquidating Distributions.**

Upon the winding up and final liquidation of the Partnership, and after the payment or adequate provision for all debts and liabilities, remaining assets shall be distributed to the Partners in accordance with their positive Capital Account balances, after giving effect to all final allocations of Net Profit and Net Loss in accordance with Article IV. The General Partner shall use commercially reasonable efforts to ensure that the final allocations and distributions are consistent with the distribution waterfall set forth in Section 8.3. The Certificate of Limited Partnership shall be cancelled upon completion of the winding up and final distribution.

**[ARTICLE XI --- ADMISSION OF PARTNERS; CLOSINGS; CONDITIONS PRECEDENT]{.underline}**

**Section 11.1 --- Admission of Limited Partners.**

Limited Partners shall be admitted to the Partnership at each Closing upon satisfaction (or waiver, to the extent permitted by Section 11.3) of the conditions set forth in Section 11.2. Each Limited Partner admitted at a Closing shall execute and deliver a Subscription Agreement in the form attached hereto as Exhibit A (or such other form as the General Partner may prescribe) and a counterpart signature page to this Agreement or a joinder agreement in the form attached hereto as Exhibit B. Upon execution and delivery of such documents and satisfaction of the applicable conditions precedent, such Limited Partner shall be bound by all terms and conditions of this Agreement as if an original party hereto. Upon admission, such Limited Partner\'s name and Capital Commitment shall be added to Schedule A. The General Partner shall cause the Fund Administrator to deliver an updated Schedule A to all Partners within ten (10) Business Days following each Closing.

**Section 11.2 --- Conditions Precedent to Each Closing.**

The General Partner shall not consummate any Closing unless each of the following conditions has been satisfied or waived by the General Partner (to the extent such condition is waivable in accordance with Section 11.3):

**(1) Subscription Agreement and LPA.** Each incoming Limited Partner shall have duly executed and delivered (i) a Subscription Agreement, in the form provided by the General Partner, containing all representations, warranties, and covenants required by Article VI, and (ii) a counterpart signature page to this Agreement or a joinder agreement in the form attached hereto as Exhibit B.

**(2) Capital Contributions.** All Capital Contributions due at such Closing (including, for Subsequent Closings, Equalization Contributions and Equalization Interest as set forth in Section 3.2(c)) shall have been received in immediately available funds in the bank account or escrow account designated by the General Partner. The Fund Administrator shall confirm receipt of all required funds prior to consummation of the Closing.

**(3) AML/KYC Compliance.** The Fund Administrator shall have confirmed in writing that satisfactory anti-money laundering and \"know your customer\" documentation has been received and verified for each incoming Limited Partner, including (without limitation) OFAC screening results, government-issued identification or corporate formation documents (as applicable), and, where applicable, beneficial ownership certifications under the Corporate Transparency Act and the Customer Due Diligence Rule (31 C.F.R. § 1010.230). The Fund Administrator shall deliver a written AML/KYC completion certificate to the General Partner prior to each Closing.

**(4) Qualified Purchaser Status.** Each incoming Limited Partner shall have represented and warranted (in its Subscription Agreement) that it is a \"qualified purchaser\" as defined in Section 2(a)(51) of the Investment Company Act of 1940, and the General Partner shall have no reason to believe that any such representation is untrue. The General Partner shall have reviewed each such representation and determined that it is consistent with the information provided by the incoming Limited Partner in its Subscription Agreement and supporting documentation.

**(5) ERISA Compliance.** The General Partner shall have determined that, after giving effect to the admission of all incoming Limited Partners at such Closing, the aggregate Capital Commitments of Benefit Plan Investors do not exceed the limitation set forth in Section 6.8 (i.e., twenty-four and ninety-nine hundredths percent (24.99%) of total Capital Commitments). The General Partner shall document such determination in writing and include such calculation in the closing deliverables provided to the Fund Administrator and Fund Counsel.

**(6) No Material Adverse Change; Key Persons.** No material adverse change shall have occurred with respect to the General Partner, the Partnership, or the General Partner\'s ability to manage the Partnership since the immediately prior Closing (or, in the case of the Initial Closing, since the formation of the Partnership). Each Key Person (as defined in Section 9.4) shall continue to devote substantially all of his or her business time and efforts to the Partnership, and no Key Person Event shall have occurred or be reasonably expected to occur as of the date of such Closing. The General Partner shall deliver a certificate confirming compliance with the foregoing in connection with each Closing.

**(7) Legal Opinions.** Fund Counsel shall have delivered to the General Partner and the Fund Administrator legal opinions, dated as of the date of the applicable Closing, in customary form and substance for transactions of this type, addressing: (i) the due formation and valid existence of the Partnership as a limited partnership under the laws of the State of Delaware; (ii) the enforceability of this Agreement against the Partnership and the General Partner; and (iii) such other matters as are customary for private equity fund closings, including the limited liability of the Limited Partners.

**(8) Updated Schedule A.** The General Partner shall have prepared (or caused the Fund Administrator to prepare) an updated Schedule A reflecting the Capital Commitments of all Partners after giving effect to such Closing, including the names, Capital Commitments, dates of admission, Benefit Plan Investor status, and qualified purchaser confirmations of all Partners. Such updated Schedule A shall have been delivered (or be deliverable concurrently with the Closing) to all Partners and the Fund Administrator.

**(9) Hard Cap Compliance.** The General Partner shall have confirmed that, after giving effect to the admission of all incoming Limited Partners at such Closing, the aggregate Capital Commitments of all Partners (including the GP Commitment) do not exceed the Hard Cap. The General Partner shall document such confirmation in writing.

**(10) Representations and Warranties.** The General Partner shall certify that all representations and warranties of the General Partner contained in this Agreement are true and correct in all material respects as of the date of such Closing as though made on and as of such date (except for representations and warranties that by their terms relate to a specific date, which shall be true and correct in all material respects as of such specified date). Such certification shall be delivered in writing as part of the closing deliverables.

**Section 11.3 --- Waiver of Conditions.**

The General Partner may, in its sole discretion, waive any of the conditions set forth in Section 11.2; *provided*, *however*, that the conditions set forth in Section 11.2(3) (AML/KYC Compliance), Section 11.2(4) (Qualified Purchaser Status), and Section 11.2(5) (ERISA Compliance) may not be waived under any circumstances. Any waiver of a condition under this Section 11.3 shall be documented in writing and shall be effective only with respect to the specific Closing and the specific incoming Limited Partner(s) for which such waiver is granted. No waiver of any condition with respect to any Closing shall constitute a waiver of such condition with respect to any other Closing.

**[ARTICLE XII --- CONFIDENTIALITY; SIDE LETTERS; MFN]{.underline}**

**Section 12.1 --- Confidentiality.**

Each Partner shall hold in strict confidence, and shall not disclose to any Person (other than as expressly permitted herein), all non-public information regarding the Partnership, its investments, its Portfolio Companies, and the terms and conditions of this Agreement and any Side Letter (collectively, \"Confidential Information\"). The foregoing confidentiality obligation shall not apply to disclosures: (a) to such Partner\'s legal counsel, tax advisors, accountants, financial advisors, or other professional advisors who are under a duty of confidentiality and have a bona fide need to know such information in connection with the Partner\'s investment in the Partnership; (b) required by applicable law, regulation, judicial process, or governmental order or request (including disclosures required under the Securities Act of 1933, the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, ERISA, and applicable state securities laws), subject to Section 12.4; (c) to prospective transferees of such Partner\'s interest in the Partnership, in connection with a permitted transfer under Article XIII, provided that such prospective transferee agrees in writing to be bound by the confidentiality provisions of this Section 12.1; (d) to governmental or regulatory authorities having jurisdiction over such Partner, to the extent required in connection with such Partner\'s regulatory filings or examinations; and (e) to the extent such information becomes generally available to the public through no fault of such Partner.

**Section 12.2 --- Side Letters.**

The General Partner may enter into side letters or other written agreements with one or more Limited Partners (each, a \"Side Letter\") that have the effect of supplementing, modifying, or waiving certain terms of this Agreement with respect to such Limited Partner(s). Side Letters shall be binding only on the General Partner and the applicable Limited Partner(s) and shall not create any rights in favor of, or obligations on behalf of, any other Partner, except as provided in Section 12.3. To the extent any Side Letter grants rights that are more favorable than the terms of this Agreement with respect to the applicable Limited Partner, such rights shall be subject to the MFN election provisions of Section 12.3.

**Section 12.3 --- Most Favored Nation (MFN) Elections.**

**(a) MFN Eligible Partners.** Each Limited Partner whose Capital Commitment equals or exceeds Fifty Million Dollars (\$50,000,000) (each, an \"MFN Eligible Partner\") shall be entitled to elect to receive the benefit of any provision contained in any Side Letter entered into by the General Partner with any other Limited Partner that grants such other Limited Partner economic or non-economic terms that are more favorable than the terms of this Agreement, subject to the exclusions set forth in Section 12.3(d) below.

**(b) MFN Notice.** Within thirty (30) days following the Final Closing, the General Partner shall provide each MFN Eligible Partner with a written summary (the \"MFN Notice\") of all Side Letter provisions that are subject to MFN election pursuant to this Section 12.3, without identifying the Limited Partner(s) to whom such provisions were originally granted. The MFN Notice shall describe each electable provision in reasonable detail sufficient to enable each MFN Eligible Partner to make an informed election. The General Partner shall concurrently deliver a copy of the MFN Notice to Fund Counsel for its records.

**(c) Election Period.** Each MFN Eligible Partner shall have thirty (30) days from its receipt of the MFN Notice (the \"Election Period\") to deliver written notice to the General Partner (an \"MFN Election\") electing to receive the benefit of one or more of the Side Letter provisions identified in the MFN Notice. An MFN Eligible Partner may elect to receive the benefit of any or all electable provisions. Any provision with respect to which an MFN Eligible Partner does not make an affirmative election within the Election Period shall be deemed to have been waived by such MFN Eligible Partner, and such MFN Eligible Partner shall have no further right to elect such provision.

**(d) Exclusions.** The following Side Letter provisions shall not be subject to MFN election and need not be included in the MFN Notice:

> \(i\) provisions that are specific to the legal, regulatory, or tax status of a particular Limited Partner, including (without limitation) FOIA carve-outs for public pension funds, accommodations for non-U.S. entities to comply with foreign tax laws, and ERISA-specific provisions required to avoid prohibited transactions;
>
> \(ii\) co-investment rights that are specifically limited in capacity or scope and are granted in consideration of a particular Limited Partner\'s strategic value or relationship;
>
> \(iii\) Advisory Committee seats or rights to appoint Advisory Committee representatives;
>
> \(iv\) provisions that by their terms are personal to the applicable Limited Partner and would not be capable of being extended to other Partners without the consent of a third party; and
>
> \(v\) provisions relating solely to the form or timing of reporting or communication (e.g., additional reporting obligations specific to a Partner\'s regulatory requirements).

**(e) Effect of Election.** Any MFN Election duly made by an MFN Eligible Partner within the Election Period shall be deemed to amend such MFN Eligible Partner\'s Side Letter (or, if no Side Letter exists for such MFN Eligible Partner, to create a new Side Letter between the General Partner and such MFN Eligible Partner) to include the elected provisions, effective as of the date of such MFN Election. The General Partner shall prepare and deliver to each MFN Eligible Partner that has made an MFN Election a written confirmation of the elected provisions within fifteen (15) days following the expiration of the Election Period.

**Section 12.4 --- Public Records / FOIA.**

The General Partner acknowledges that certain Limited Partners (particularly public pension funds, governmental entities, and other public bodies) may be subject to the Freedom of Information Act, state open records laws, or other public disclosure requirements (collectively, \"FOIA Laws\"). The General Partner shall use commercially reasonable efforts to seek confidential treatment of Partnership information in response to any public records request, to the extent permitted by applicable law. Each Partner that is subject to FOIA Laws shall promptly notify the General Partner (and, to the extent practicable, in advance of any disclosure) upon receipt of any request for Partnership information under applicable FOIA Laws, and shall cooperate with the General Partner in seeking to protect the confidentiality of such information. Separate and specific accommodations for Partners subject to FOIA Laws may be addressed in Side Letters.

**[ARTICLE XIII --- TRANSFERS]{.underline}**

**Section 13.1 --- Restrictions on Transfer.**

No Partner may Transfer all or any portion of its interest in the Partnership without the prior written consent of the General Partner, which consent may be withheld in the General Partner\'s sole and absolute discretion. No Transfer shall be permitted if such Transfer would: (a) violate the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, or any applicable state securities or \"blue sky\" law; (b) cause the Partnership to be treated as a \"publicly traded partnership\" taxable as a corporation under Section 7704 of the Code; (c) cause the Partnership to hold \"plan assets\" under ERISA or the regulations promulgated thereunder, or cause a non-exempt prohibited transaction under Section 406 of ERISA or Section 4975 of the Code; (d) result in the Partnership having more than two thousand (2,000) beneficial owners (taking into account applicable \"look-through\" rules) or otherwise jeopardize the Partnership\'s exemption from registration under Section 3(c)(7) of the Investment Company Act; (e) cause any adverse tax consequences to the Partnership or the other Partners; or (f) violate any applicable law, regulation, or order. The General Partner shall determine, in its sole discretion, whether any proposed Transfer satisfies the foregoing requirements.

**Section 13.2 --- Permitted Transfers.**

Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 13.1, the General Partner shall consent to Transfers of all or any portion of a Partner\'s interest to an Affiliate or related entity of the transferring Partner (including any wholly-owned subsidiary, parent entity, or entity under common control with the transferring Partner), *provided* that all conditions set forth in Section 13.3 are satisfied. The General Partner may, in its discretion, provide the other Partners with a right of first refusal with respect to any proposed Transfer, on such terms as the General Partner deems appropriate.

**Section 13.3 --- Conditions to Transfer.**

Any Transfer permitted or consented to by the General Partner shall be subject to the following conditions: (a) the General Partner shall have received a legal opinion from counsel reasonably satisfactory to the General Partner to the effect that such Transfer may be effected without registration under the Securities Act of 1933 and without violation of applicable state securities laws; (b) the proposed transferee shall have executed and delivered a joinder agreement or other instrument, in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the General Partner, pursuant to which such transferee agrees to be bound by all of the terms and conditions of this Agreement; (c) the proposed transferee shall have represented and warranted that it is a Qualified Purchaser and, if applicable, have satisfied the AML/KYC requirements of Section 6.4; (d) the General Partner shall have updated Schedule A to reflect the Transfer; and (e) all costs and expenses incurred by the Partnership or the General Partner in connection with such Transfer (including legal fees) shall be borne by the transferring Partner. The General Partner may impose additional conditions on any Transfer as it deems appropriate.

**[ARTICLE XIV --- GENERAL PROVISIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 14.1 --- Amendments.**

This Agreement may be amended, modified, or supplemented only by a written instrument executed by the General Partner and Limited Partners holding a majority (more than fifty percent (50%)) of the aggregate Capital Commitments of all Limited Partners. Notwithstanding the foregoing, no amendment that would adversely affect a Limited Partner in a manner disproportionate to other Limited Partners shall be effective without the prior written consent of such adversely affected Limited Partner. The General Partner may, without the consent of any Limited Partner, make ministerial or non-substantive amendments to this Agreement, including (without limitation) amendments to update Schedule A to reflect the admission of new Partners, changes in Capital Commitments, transfers of interests, corrections of typographical or clerical errors, and changes required to comply with applicable law. Any such ministerial amendment shall be promptly communicated to all Partners.

**Section 14.2 --- Notices.**

All notices, requests, demands, consents, waivers, and other communications required or permitted to be given under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be delivered by hand delivery, by nationally recognized overnight courier, or by electronic mail (with confirmation of receipt), addressed as follows:

Any Partner may change its notice address by delivering written notice of such change to the General Partner and the Fund Administrator.

**Section 14.3 --- Governing Law.**

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without giving effect to any choice of law or conflict of law rules or provisions that would cause the application of the laws of any other jurisdiction.

**Section 14.4 --- Dispute Resolution.**

Any dispute, controversy, or claim arising out of or relating to this Agreement, or the breach, termination, or validity thereof, shall be resolved by binding arbitration administered by the American Arbitration Association in accordance with its Commercial Arbitration Rules then in effect. The arbitration shall be seated in Chicago, Illinois, and shall be conducted by a panel of three (3) arbitrators. Each party to the dispute shall select one (1) arbitrator, and the two (2) party-appointed arbitrators shall select the third arbitrator, who shall serve as chair of the panel. Each party shall bear its own costs and expenses (including attorneys\' fees) incurred in connection with the arbitration; *provided*, *however*, that the prevailing party shall be entitled to recover its reasonable attorneys\' fees and expenses from the non-prevailing party, as determined by the arbitration panel. The decision of the arbitrators shall be final and binding on all parties and may be enforced in any court of competent jurisdiction.

**Section 14.5 --- Entire Agreement.**

This Agreement, together with the Subscription Agreements executed by each Limited Partner, the Side Letters, the schedules and exhibits attached hereto, and such other documents as are expressly incorporated by reference herein, constitutes the entire agreement among the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements, negotiations, discussions, representations, warranties, commitments, and understandings, whether written or oral, relating to such subject matter.

**Section 14.6 --- Severability.**

If any provision of this Agreement, or the application of any such provision to any Person or circumstance, shall be held by a court of competent jurisdiction or arbitration panel to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable in any respect, such invalidity, illegality, or unenforceability shall not affect any other provision of this Agreement, and the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect. The Partners shall negotiate in good faith to replace any invalid, illegal, or unenforceable provision with a valid, legal, and enforceable provision that achieves, to the greatest extent possible, the original intent of such provision.

**Section 14.7 --- Counterparts.**

This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original and all of which, taken together, shall constitute one and the same agreement. Delivery of an executed counterpart of a signature page to this Agreement by facsimile transmission or by electronic mail in portable document format (.pdf) shall be effective as delivery of a manually executed counterpart. Electronic signatures (including signatures made using commercially available e-signature platforms such as DocuSign or Adobe Sign) shall have the same legal effect as original ink signatures.

**Section 14.8 --- No Third-Party Beneficiaries.**

Except as expressly set forth in Section 9.5 (with respect to Indemnified Parties), this Agreement is for the sole benefit of the parties hereto and their respective permitted successors and assigns, and nothing in this Agreement, express or implied, is intended to or shall confer upon any other Person any legal or equitable right, benefit, or remedy of any nature whatsoever under or by reason of this Agreement.

**Section 14.9 --- Power of Attorney.**

Each Limited Partner hereby irrevocably constitutes and appoints the General Partner and any authorized officer or agent thereof as such Limited Partner\'s true and lawful attorney-in-fact, with full power of substitution, in such Limited Partner\'s name, place, and stead, to execute, acknowledge, deliver, swear to, file, and record (as applicable) any and all documents, instruments, certificates, and other writings, including (without limitation): (a) amendments to this Agreement and the Certificate of Limited Partnership; (b) certificates of assumed name or \"doing business as\" certificates; (c) any filings required under the Act or any other applicable law in connection with the formation, qualification, continuation, or dissolution of the Partnership; and (d) any other documents or instruments that the General Partner deems necessary or appropriate in connection with the business and affairs of the Partnership. This power of attorney is coupled with an interest and shall be irrevocable, and shall survive and not be affected by the disability, incapacity, death, dissolution, bankruptcy, or insolvency of any Limited Partner, or the Transfer by any Limited Partner of all or any part of its interest in the Partnership.

**Section 14.10 --- Tax Matters.**

The General Partner shall serve as the \"Tax Matters Partner\" under Section 6231 of the Code (as in effect prior to amendment by the Bipartisan Budget Act of 2015) with respect to any taxable year for which such designation is applicable, and as the \"Partnership Representative\" under Section 6223 of the Code (as amended by the Bipartisan Budget Act of 2015) with respect to any taxable year for which such designation is applicable. In either capacity, the General Partner shall have the authority to make all tax elections on behalf of the Partnership, to file or cause to be filed all tax returns of the Partnership, and to represent the Partnership in connection with any tax audit, examination, or other proceeding before the Internal Revenue Service or any other taxing authority. The General Partner shall make reasonable efforts to cause Schedule K-1s (or equivalent tax reporting statements) to be prepared and delivered to each Partner within ninety (90) days after the end of each Fiscal Year, or as soon as reasonably practicable thereafter. Each Partner shall cooperate with the General Partner and the Partnership\'s tax advisors in connection with all tax matters affecting the Partnership and shall provide such information and execute such documents as the General Partner may reasonably request.

**Section 14.11 --- Fiscal Year.**

The fiscal year of the Partnership (the \"Fiscal Year\") shall be the calendar year ending December 31.

**Section 14.12 --- Books and Records; Reporting.**

The General Partner shall maintain (or cause the Fund Administrator to maintain) the books and records of the Partnership in accordance with U.S. GAAP. Annual audited financial statements, audited by a nationally recognized independent public accounting firm selected by the General Partner, shall be provided to all Partners within one hundred twenty (120) days after the end of each Fiscal Year. Quarterly unaudited financial statements and investor reports shall be provided to all Partners within sixty (60) days after the end of each calendar quarter. The General Partner shall cause the Fund Administrator (Oakmere Fund Services LLC) to prepare and distribute such investor reports, including capital account statements, portfolio summaries, and such other information as the General Partner deems appropriate. Each Partner and its duly authorized representatives may inspect the books and records of the Partnership during normal business hours at the Partnership\'s principal office, upon reasonable prior written notice to the General Partner.

**Section 14.13 --- Banking.**

The Partnership shall maintain its operating accounts and any escrow accounts at Pinnacle National Bank, or such other financial institution or institutions as the General Partner may designate from time to time. The General Partner and the Fund Administrator (or such other Persons as the General Partner may authorize) shall be the authorized signatories on all Partnership accounts.

**Section 14.14 --- Insurance.**

The General Partner may, in its sole discretion and at the expense of the Partnership (as a Partnership expense), purchase and maintain insurance policies for the benefit of the Partnership, the General Partner, and their respective officers, directors, employees, and agents, including directors\' and officers\' liability insurance, errors and omissions insurance, and such other insurance policies as the General Partner deems appropriate.

**Section 14.15 --- Waiver of Partition.**

Each Partner hereby irrevocably waives any right that it may have to maintain an action for partition with respect to the property of the Partnership.
