**CONFIDENTIAL --- ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGED** **FOR INTERNAL USE ONLY**

**[MEMORANDUM]{.underline}**

**TO:** Marcus J. Whitmore, Chief Investment Officer and Managing Member, Whitmore Capital Management LLC

**FROM:** Sandra K. Liu, Chief Compliance Officer and General Counsel, Whitmore Capital Management LLC

**DATE:** January 28, 2025

**RE:** Final Closing Readiness Summary --- Whitmore Capital Partners VII, LP (January 31, 2025)

This memorandum summarizes the status of all conditions, deliverables, and readiness items in connection with the final closing of Whitmore Capital Partners VII, LP (the \"Fund\" or \"Fund VII\"), currently scheduled for January 31, 2025. This memorandum has been prepared for internal use by the General Partner and its principals and should be treated as attorney-client privileged and work product. It should not be shared with any limited partner, prospective investor, or third party without my prior written consent.

The sections that follow address (i) an executive summary of closing readiness, (ii) the incoming limited partners, (iii) aggregate commitment reconciliation, (iv) equalization interest, (v) regulatory and compliance matters, (vi) conditions precedent under the LPA, (vii) key person status, (viii) portfolio activity, (ix) service provider status, (x) side letters and MFN matters, (xi) advisory committee composition, (xii) outstanding action items, and (xiii) my overall recommendation.

**[I. Executive Summary]{.underline}**

Whitmore Capital Partners VII, LP is a Delaware limited partnership (Delaware Secretary of State File No. 7842319), formed on August 1, 2024, pursuant to a Certificate of Limited Partnership filed with the Delaware Secretary of State on that date. The General Partner of the Fund is Whitmore Capital Management LLC, a Delaware limited liability company formed on March 12, 2018 (Delaware Secretary of State File No. 6591082). The Fund was organized to make private equity investments in middle-market companies in the healthcare services and technology-enabled services sectors, in accordance with the investment strategy and parameters set forth in the Confidential Private Placement Memorandum dated July 15, 2024 (the \"PPM\"), and the Amended and Restated Agreement of Limited Partnership, dated as of September 15, 2024, as amended by Amendment No. 1, dated November 30, 2024 (the \"LPA\").

The Fund held its initial closing on September 15, 2024, at which time eighteen (18) limited partners were admitted with aggregate LP capital commitments of \$940,000,000, and the General Partner committed \$15,000,000, bringing the total commitments at the initial closing to \$955,000,000. A second closing was held on November 30, 2024, at which time nine (9) additional limited partners were admitted with aggregate LP capital commitments of \$375,000,000, bringing the cumulative total Fund commitments to \$1,330,000,000.

The Fund is now scheduled to conduct its final closing on January 31, 2025. At the final closing, the Fund will admit six (6) new limited partners with aggregate capital commitments of \$285,000,000. Following the final closing, total Fund commitments will be \$1,615,000,000 (comprised of \$1,600,000,000 in aggregate LP commitments and the \$15,000,000 GP commitment). This total is below the hard cap of \$1,750,000,000 as set forth in Section 3.1 of the LPA.

This memorandum addresses the following matters in connection with the final closing:

> \(i\) a summary of the six incoming final closing limited partners;
>
> \(ii\) a reconciliation of aggregate Fund commitments across all three closings;
>
> \(iii\) equalization interest and catch-up contribution mechanics;
>
> \(iv\) regulatory and compliance matters, including SEC registration, AML/KYC, ERISA compliance, and Investment Company Act status;
>
> \(v\) a review of each condition precedent to closing under Section 11.2 of the LPA;
>
> \(vi\) key person status under Section 9.4 of the LPA;
>
> \(vii\) a summary of portfolio activity and investment period status;
>
> \(viii\) the status of the Fund\'s service providers;
>
> \(ix\) side letter and most-favored nation matters;
>
> \(x\) Advisory Committee composition; and
>
> \(xi\) outstanding items and action items required prior to or at the final closing.

Based on my review, the Fund is **substantially ready** to proceed with the final closing on January 31, 2025, subject to the completion of certain outstanding deliverables and action items described in detail in Section XII below. I do not believe any of the outstanding items present a material impediment to closing on the scheduled date.

**[II. Final Closing Limited Partners]{.underline}**

Six new limited partners will be admitted to the Fund at the final closing. A summary of each incoming LP, including its commitment amount, entity type, jurisdiction, and ERISA status, is set forth below.

**1. Meridian State Teachers\' Retirement System**

> Commitment: \$100,000,000 Entity Type: Public pension fund Jurisdiction: United States ERISA Status: Benefit plan investor (ERISA plan investor)

**2. Haverfield Family Office LLC**

> Commitment: \$40,000,000 Entity Type: Family office Jurisdiction: United States ERISA Status: Non-ERISA

**3. Northbridge Endowment Fund**

> Commitment: \$60,000,000 Entity Type: Endowment Jurisdiction: United States ERISA Status: Non-ERISA

**4. Cascade Re Insurance Ltd.**

> Commitment: \$35,000,000 Entity Type: Reinsurance company Jurisdiction: Bermuda ERISA Status: Non-ERISA

**5. Sentry Municipal Employees\' Pension Trust**

> Commitment: \$30,000,000 Entity Type: Public pension trust Jurisdiction: United States ERISA Status: Benefit plan investor (ERISA plan investor)

**6. Kingsgate Ventures Fund-of-Funds III, LP**

> Commitment: \$20,000,000 Entity Type: Fund-of-funds Jurisdiction: United States ERISA Status: Non-ERISA (look-through analysis completed; underlying investors confirmed to be below the 25% benefit plan investor threshold)

**Total Final Closing LP Commitments: \$285,000,000**

Subscription agreements have been received from all six incoming limited partners. Executed signature pages to the Amended and Restated Agreement of Limited Partnership (as amended by Amendment No. 1, dated November 30, 2024) have been delivered by each incoming LP and are currently being held in escrow by Bridgeford Hollis LLP pending the consummation of the final closing. All six incoming LPs have made qualified purchaser representations in their respective subscription agreements, as required under the Fund\'s reliance on the Section 3(c)(7) exemption under the Investment Company Act of 1940, as amended.

With respect to Cascade Re Insurance Ltd., I note that this entity is domiciled in Bermuda and is subject to regulatory and tax reporting requirements specific to its jurisdiction. A side letter has been negotiated providing certain regulatory and tax reporting accommodations for Cascade Re, including accommodations regarding information reporting under the Bermuda Monetary Authority framework and modifications to the Fund\'s standard tax distribution provisions to account for Cascade Re\'s non-U.S. tax status. This side letter was finalized on January 22, 2025, by David R. Kasten and Emily T. Naraghi of Bridgeford Hollis LLP, following negotiations with Cascade Re\'s counsel, Connors, Valemont & Calverley Limited.

Across all three closings, a total of fourteen (14) side letters have been executed on behalf of the Fund. Additional detail regarding side letter provisions and most-favored nation matters is provided in Section X below.

**[III. Aggregate Commitment Reconciliation]{.underline}**

The following table summarizes aggregate Fund commitments across all three closings:

  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Closing**       **Date**             **No. of LPs**    **LP Commitments**    **GP Commitment**   **Cumulative Total**
  ----------------- -------------------- ----------------- --------------------- ------------------- ----------------------
  Initial Closing   September 15, 2024   18                \$940,000,000         \$15,000,000        \$955,000,000

  Second Closing    November 30, 2024    9                 \$375,000,000         ---                 \$1,330,000,000

  Final Closing     January 31, 2025     6                 \$285,000,000         ---                 \$1,615,000,000

  **Totals**                             **33 LPs + GP**   **\$1,600,000,000**   **\$15,000,000**    **\$1,615,000,000**
  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Fund\'s hard cap is \$1,750,000,000, as set forth in Section 3.1 of the LPA. Total Fund commitments following the final closing will be \$1,615,000,000, which is \$135,000,000 below the hard cap. The hard cap condition is therefore satisfied.

Following the final closing, the Fund will have a total of thirty-three (33) limited partners plus the General Partner. The Fund relies on the Section 3(c)(7) exemption under the Investment Company Act of 1940, which permits beneficial ownership by up to 2,000 qualified purchasers. The Fund\'s investor count of 34 (33 LPs plus the GP) is well within this limitation, and no concerns regarding the availability of the 3(c)(7) exemption have been identified.

All commitment amounts reflected in the table above have been confirmed against the executed subscription agreements for each closing and are consistent with the records maintained by Oakmere Fund Services LLC, the Fund\'s administrator. The updated Schedule A reflecting all three closings is being prepared by Oakmere and is under review by Bridgeford Hollis LLP, as discussed further in Section XII.

**[IV. Equalization Interest and Catch-Up Contributions]{.underline}**

Under Section 3.2(c) of the LPA, limited partners admitted at any subsequent closing (including the final closing) are required to fund their pro rata share of all capital calls issued prior to their admission, together with equalization interest calculated at the rate of eight percent (8%) per annum (simple interest) on each such catch-up contribution, accruing from the date of the applicable prior capital call through the date of the subsequent closing at which such limited partner is admitted (in this case, January 31, 2025).

Two capital calls have been issued to date:

**Capital Call #1 --- Dated October 10, 2024**

> Capital Call #1 was issued on October 10, 2024, calling 10% of each then-admitted partner\'s capital commitment. At the time of Capital Call #1, only the initial closing limited partners and the General Partner had been admitted. The total amount drawn was \$95,500,000, comprised of \$94,000,000 from the eighteen initial closing LPs and \$1,500,000 from the General Partner. Proceeds from Capital Call #1 were used to fund the Fund\'s equity investment in MedBridge Health Systems, Inc. (\$72,000,000) and to fund working capital, organizational expenses, and management fee obligations.

**Capital Call #2 --- Dated December 15, 2024**

> Capital Call #2 was issued on December 15, 2024, calling 5% of each then-admitted partner\'s capital commitment. At the time of Capital Call #2, the initial closing LPs, the second closing LPs, and the General Partner had been admitted. The total amount drawn was \$66,500,000, comprised of \$47,000,000 from the initial closing LPs, \$18,750,000 from the second closing LPs, and \$750,000 from the General Partner. Proceeds from Capital Call #2 were used to fund the Fund\'s equity investment in Altius Digital Solutions, LLC (\$45,000,000) and to fund working capital and management fee obligations.

**Catch-Up Contributions for Final Closing LPs:**

Each of the six final closing LPs will be required to fund catch-up contributions with respect to both Capital Call #1 and Capital Call #2. The aggregate catch-up capital contribution for the final closing LPs is calculated as follows:

> Capital Call #1 catch-up: \$285,000,000 × 10% = \$28,500,000
>
> Capital Call #2 catch-up: \$285,000,000 × 5% = \$14,250,000
>
> Total catch-up capital: \$42,750,000

Equalization interest on each catch-up contribution will accrue at 8% per annum (simple interest) from the date of the relevant capital call through January 31, 2025. Oakmere Fund Services LLC has prepared the equalization interest calculations for each incoming LP, and such calculations are currently under review by Bridgeford Hollis LLP. The specific equalization interest amounts will be set forth in the final closing capital call notice to be issued to each incoming LP.

Upon receipt, equalization interest amounts will be distributed to the existing limited partners (admitted at the initial and second closings) in proportion to their respective capital contributions to Capital Call #1 and Capital Call #2, net of the General Partner\'s share. These distributions will be processed by Oakmere Fund Services LLC following the final closing.

**[V. Regulatory and Compliance Matters]{.underline}**

**V.A. SEC Registration and Form D**

Whitmore Capital Management LLC is registered as an investment adviser with the United States Securities and Exchange Commission under the Investment Advisers Act of 1940, as amended (CRD No. 298417; SEC File No. 801-79834). The registration remains in good standing as of the date of this memorandum, and no deficiency letters, examination notices, or enforcement inquiries have been received from the SEC or any state securities regulator.

The Fund\'s Form D was initially filed with the SEC on September 20, 2024, within 15 calendar days following the initial closing, as required. An amended Form D was filed on December 5, 2024, following the second closing, to reflect updated aggregate offering information and the increased number of investors.

A further amendment to Form D will be required following the final closing to reflect the six additional investors and the updated aggregate offering amount. The amendment should be filed within fifteen (15) days of the final closing --- that is, by no later than February 15, 2025. I will coordinate the preparation and filing of the amended Form D with Bridgeford Hollis LLP. Responsibility for this item is assigned to me, with support from David R. Kasten at Bridgeford Hollis.

**V.B. AML/KYC Compliance**

Oakmere Fund Services LLC serves as the Fund\'s administrator and is responsible for conducting anti-money laundering (\"AML\") and know-your-customer (\"KYC\") verification procedures for all incoming limited partners. Jonathan C. Prewitt, Director of Fund Operations at Oakmere, confirmed via email on January 24, 2025, that AML/KYC checks for the final closing investors have been completed and that no adverse findings were identified.

I have reviewed Mr. Prewitt\'s email and consider this item to be satisfied for purposes of the final closing. A copy of the email is included as Enclosure C to this memorandum. No further action is required with respect to AML/KYC compliance.

**V.C. ERISA Compliance**

Section 6.8 of the LPA provides that benefit plan investor participation in the Fund shall not exceed 24.99% of total Fund commitments, in order to avoid the Fund\'s assets being treated as \"plan assets\" under Section 3(42) of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974, as amended (\"ERISA\"), and the regulations promulgated thereunder by the U.S. Department of Labor (29 C.F.R. § 2510.3-101, as modified by Section 3(42) of ERISA).

Based on our review, benefit plan investors (as defined under ERISA) currently represent approximately \$225,300,000 in aggregate commitments. Following the final closing, total Fund commitments will be approximately \$1,140,000,000. Accordingly, the benefit plan investor percentage is approximately 19.8% (\$225,300,000 / \$1,140,000,000), which remains well below the 24.99% cap set forth in Section 6.8 of the LPA.

The Fund\'s ERISA compliance has been monitored on an ongoing basis in coordination with Oakmere Fund Services LLC, which maintains the benefit plan investor tracking schedule as part of its investor onboarding and closing procedures. The look-through analysis for Kingsgate Ventures Fund-of-Funds III, LP was completed by Oakmere and reviewed by Bridgeford Hollis LLP, confirming that Kingsgate\'s underlying investors do not cause it to be classified as a benefit plan investor for purposes of the 25% threshold. Based on the foregoing, I consider the ERISA compliance condition to be satisfied.

**V.D. Investment Company Act Exemption**

The Fund relies on the exemption from registration under Section 3(c)(7) of the Investment Company Act of 1940, as amended, which exempts from the definition of \"investment company\" any issuer whose outstanding securities are owned exclusively by persons who, at the time of acquisition of such securities, are \"qualified purchasers\" (as defined in Section 2(a)(51) of the Investment Company Act), provided that the issuer does not make or propose to make a public offering of its securities.

All six incoming final closing limited partners have made qualified purchaser representations in their respective subscription agreements. The total investor count following the final closing will be 33 limited partners plus the General Partner, totaling 34 beneficial owners, well below the 2,000-qualified-purchaser limit applicable under Section 3(c)(7). No issues have been identified with respect to the continued availability of this exemption.

**[VI. Conditions Precedent Under LPA Section 11.2]{.underline}**

Section 11.2 of the LPA sets forth ten conditions precedent that must be satisfied at or prior to any subsequent closing. The status of each condition is summarized below.

**Condition 1: Subscription Agreements and LPA Signature Pages**

> *Status: Complete.* All six final closing limited partners have executed and delivered their respective subscription agreements and signature pages to the Amended and Restated Agreement of Limited Partnership (as amended by Amendment No. 1). Originals are being held by Bridgeford Hollis LLP in escrow pending the consummation of the final closing.

**Condition 2: Payment of Capital Contributions (Including Equalization Interest)**

> *Status: Pending.* Capital contributions from the final closing limited partners, including catch-up contributions and equalization interest as described in Section IV above, are due at the final closing. Funds are expected to be wired to the Fund\'s escrow account at Pinnacle National Bank (account ending 9213) on or before January 31, 2025. Wire instructions were distributed to all incoming limited partners on January 20, 2025. Oakmere Fund Services LLC will confirm receipt of all funds on the closing date.

**Condition 3: AML/KYC Documentation**

> *Status: Complete.* Per the confirmation email from Jonathan C. Prewitt of Oakmere Fund Services LLC, dated January 24, 2025, AML/KYC checks for all final closing investors have been completed. See Section V.B above.

**Condition 4: Qualified Purchaser Representations**

> *Status: Complete.* All six final closing limited partners have confirmed their status as qualified purchasers in their respective subscription agreements. See Section V.D above.

**Condition 5: ERISA Compliance**

> *Status: Satisfied.* The benefit plan investor percentage is approximately 19.8%, which is well below the 24.99% cap set forth in Section 6.8 of the LPA. See Section V.C above for the detailed analysis.

**Condition 6: No Material Adverse Change to GP or Key Persons**

> *Status: Satisfied.* Marcus J. Whitmore continues to serve as Chief Investment Officer and Managing Member of the General Partner and remains actively engaged in the management of Fund VII on a full-time basis. There has been no material adverse change with respect to the General Partner or its principals since the second closing on November 30, 2024. No event has occurred that would give rise to a notification obligation or a key person event under the LPA.

**Condition 7: Legal Opinions from Fund Counsel**

> *Status: In Process.* Bridgeford Hollis LLP (David R. Kasten, lead partner) is preparing the required legal opinions to be delivered at or prior to closing, including: (a) a valid formation opinion for the Fund, confirming that the Fund has been duly formed and is validly existing as a limited partnership under the laws of the State of Delaware; (b) an enforceability opinion with respect to the LPA; and (c) a securities law opinion regarding the availability of the private placement exemption under Regulation D. Drafts of each opinion have been circulated and are expected to be finalized and delivered at or prior to the closing.

**Condition 8: Updated Schedule A (Commitment Schedule)**

> *Status: In Process.* Oakmere Fund Services LLC is preparing the updated Schedule A to the LPA reflecting the commitments of all limited partners across all three closings. A preliminary draft has been circulated to Bridgeford Hollis LLP for review and comment. The final version of Schedule A will be attached to the closing certificate executed at the final closing.

**Condition 9: Aggregate Commitments Do Not Exceed Hard Cap**

> *Status: Satisfied.* Total Fund commitments following the final closing will be \$1,615,000,000, which is \$135,000,000 below the hard cap of \$1,750,000,000 as set forth in Section 3.1 of the LPA. See Section III above.

**Condition 10: GP Representations and Warranties**

> *Status: Satisfied.* All representations and warranties of the General Partner set forth in Article VII of the LPA remain true and correct in all material respects as of the date hereof and are expected to remain true and correct as of the final closing date. No event has occurred since the second closing that would render any such representation or warranty materially inaccurate.

**[VII. Key Person Status]{.underline}**

Section 9.4 of the LPA designates Marcus J. Whitmore and Priya S. Mehta as the \"Key Persons\" of the Fund. Under Section 9.4, each Key Person is required to devote substantially all of his or her business time and attention to the affairs of the Fund and the General Partner during the investment period. A \"Key Person Event\" is triggered under Section 9.4 if any Key Person ceases to be actively engaged in the management of the Fund or otherwise fails to devote substantially all of his or her business time to Fund affairs.

As noted above, Marcus J. Whitmore continues to devote substantially all of his business time to the affairs of Fund VII and the General Partner. No Key Person Event has been triggered under Section 9.4 of the LPA. This condition remains satisfied for purposes of the final closing.

**[VIII. Portfolio Activity and Investment Period Status]{.underline}**

The Fund\'s investment period commenced on September 15, 2024 (the date of the initial closing) and will continue through September 15, 2029, unless earlier terminated in accordance with Section 8.1 of the LPA. Two portfolio investments have been made during the investment period to date:

**1. MedBridge Health Systems, Inc.**

> Investment Amount: \$72,000,000 (equity) Closing Date: November 1, 2024 Sector: Healthcare services Funded from: Capital Call #1 proceeds

**2. Altius Digital Solutions, LLC**

> Investment Amount: \$45,000,000 (equity) Closing Date: January 8, 2025 Sector: Technology-enabled services Funded from: Capital Call #2 proceeds

Total invested capital as of the date of this memorandum is \$117,000,000. Both investments are consistent with the Fund\'s stated strategy of pursuing control and significant minority investments in middle-market companies in the healthcare services and technology-enabled services sectors, as described in the PPM. Each investment was approved in accordance with the General Partner\'s investment committee procedures and funded from the proceeds of the capital calls described in Section IV above.

The final closing limited partners will participate in these existing portfolio investments through their catch-up contributions and equalization interest payments pursuant to Section 3.2(c) of the LPA. Upon admission, the final closing LPs will be allocated their pro rata share of the Fund\'s unrealized gains, losses, and expenses attributable to the period from the initial closing through the final closing date.

**[IX. Fund Administration and Service Provider Status]{.underline}**

The Fund\'s key service providers are listed below. Each engagement remains in good standing and no material service provider issues have been identified.

**Fund Administrator:** Oakmere Fund Services LLC

> Address: 1200 Harbor Boulevard, Suite 800, Stamford, CT 06902
>
> Primary Contact: Jonathan C. Prewitt, Director of Fund Operations Services: Investor onboarding, capital call processing, AML/KYC verification, NAV calculations, financial reporting, and closing mechanics Status: Performing satisfactorily; no issues identified

**Fund Counsel:** Bridgeford Hollis LLP

> Address: 77 West Monroe Street, Suite 4400, Chicago, IL 60603
>
> Lead Partner: David R. Kasten Associate: Emily T. Naraghi Services: All legal closing deliverables, LP negotiations, side letters, legal opinions, and ongoing fund counsel services Status: Actively engaged on all final closing deliverables

**Custodian:** Clearstone Trust Company

> Address: 500 Congress Avenue, Suite 1500, Austin, TX 78701
>
> Custody Agreement: Dated August 20, 2024; remains in full force and effect Status: No issues identified

**Registered Agent:** Statehouse Registered Agents, Inc.

> Address: 1301 Market Street, Wilmington, DE 19801
>
> Role: Delaware registered agent for Whitmore Capital Partners VII, LP Status: Current and in good standing

**Depository Bank:** Pinnacle National Bank

> Operating Account: Account ending 8847 (ABA routing number: 071923456) Escrow Account: Account ending 9213 (for subscription funds and capital call proceeds) Status: Both accounts active; no issues identified

**[X. Side Letters and MFN Matters]{.underline}**

A total of fourteen (14) side letters have been executed in connection with the Fund across all three closings. The material provisions negotiated in the side letters include the following:

> • **Co-investment rights:** Granted to two (2) limited partners, entitling them to participate in co-investment opportunities on a deal-by-deal basis, subject to the General Partner\'s discretion and allocation policies.
>
> • **FOIA/transparency carve-outs:** Granted to three (3) public pension fund limited partners, providing certain limitations on the confidentiality obligations set forth in the LPA to the extent necessary to permit such LPs to comply with applicable freedom of information or public records laws in their respective jurisdictions.
>
> • **Bermuda regulatory/tax accommodation:** Granted to Cascade Re Insurance Ltd. pursuant to its side letter dated January 22, 2025, providing accommodations for regulatory reporting obligations under the Bermuda Monetary Authority framework and modifications to the Fund\'s standard tax distribution provisions.

Under Section 12.3 of the LPA, most-favored nation (\"MFN\") election rights are available to limited partners committing \$50,000,000 or more to the Fund. MFN notices summarizing the material terms of all side letters executed in connection with the Fund must be circulated to eligible LPs following the final closing to permit such LPs to elect to receive the benefit of any side letter provision granted to another LP (subject to certain carve-outs for provisions specific to a particular LP\'s regulatory status or circumstances).

Among the final closing limited partners, Meridian State Teachers\' Retirement System (\$100,000,000 commitment) and Northbridge Endowment Fund (\$60,000,000 commitment) meet the MFN eligibility threshold. MFN notices will need to be prepared and circulated to these LPs, as well as to any other eligible LPs who have not yet received an updated MFN notice reflecting side letters executed at or after the second closing. Bridgeford Hollis LLP will prepare the MFN summary and coordinate distribution following the final closing.

**[XI. Advisory Committee]{.underline}**

The Advisory Committee of the Fund is constituted pursuant to Section 9.1(b) of the LPA, which provides for a total of five (5) Advisory Committee seats. The current composition of the Advisory Committee is as follows:

> 1\. **Rachel M. Torres** --- Silvermark Capital Advisors (appointed at the initial closing)
>
> 2\. **Kenneth W. Park** --- Commonwealth Public Employees\' Retirement Fund (appointed at the initial closing)
>
> 3\. **Diane L. Voss** --- Graystone Foundation (appointed at the initial closing)
>
> 4\. **Thomas A. Riley** --- Birchwood Insurance Co. (appointed at the second closing, November 30, 2024)
>
> 5\. **Vacant** --- to be filled from a final closing LP representative per Section 9.1(b)

The fifth seat on the Advisory Committee remains vacant and should be filled from among the limited partners admitted at the final closing. I recommend that we approach Meridian State Teachers\' Retirement System as a potential candidate for the vacant seat, given the size of its commitment (\$100,000,000) and the institutional nature of the organization. Outreach to final closing LPs regarding the Advisory Committee appointment should be initiated promptly following the final closing.

**[XII. Outstanding Items and Action Items Prior to Closing]{.underline}**

The following items remain outstanding or require action at or prior to the final closing on January 31, 2025:

> **1. Legal Opinions from Bridgeford Hollis LLP.** Draft opinions (formation, enforceability, and securities law) have been circulated. Final execution copies are expected to be delivered at or prior to closing. *Responsible party: David R. Kasten, Bridgeford Hollis LLP.*
>
> **2. Updated Schedule A.** A preliminary draft of the updated Schedule A reflecting all three closings is under review by Bridgeford Hollis LLP. The final version will be appended to the closing certificate. *Responsible party: Jonathan C. Prewitt, Oakmere Fund Services LLC; reviewed by Bridgeford Hollis LLP.*
>
> **3. Closing Certificate.** The closing certificate is to be prepared by Bridgeford Hollis LLP for execution by Marcus J. Whitmore on behalf of the General Partner. A draft is expected to be circulated by January 29, 2025, for review and comment. *Responsible party: Emily T. Naraghi, Bridgeford Hollis LLP.*
>
> **4. Escrow Funding.** Capital contributions from the six final closing LPs, including catch-up contributions and equalization interest, are to be wired to Pinnacle National Bank escrow account (ending 9213) on or before January 31, 2025. Oakmere Fund Services LLC will monitor and confirm receipt of all incoming wires on the closing date. *Responsible party: Jonathan C. Prewitt, Oakmere Fund Services LLC.*
>
> **5. Form D Amendment.** An amended Form D must be filed with the SEC within fifteen (15) days following the final closing (i.e., by February 15, 2025). I will coordinate the preparation and filing with Bridgeford Hollis LLP. *Responsible party: Sandra K. Liu, with support from Bridgeford Hollis LLP.*
>
> **6. MFN Notice.** MFN notices must be prepared and circulated to eligible final closing LPs (Meridian State Teachers\' Retirement System and Northbridge Endowment Fund) and any other eligible LPs who have not yet received updated MFN notices. *Responsible party: Bridgeford Hollis LLP (post-closing item).*
>
> **7. Advisory Committee Fifth Seat.** Outreach to final closing LPs to fill the vacant fifth seat on the Advisory Committee, with a recommended approach to Meridian State Teachers\' Retirement System. *Responsible party: Marcus J. Whitmore and Sandra K. Liu (post-closing item).*
>
> **8. Blue Sky Filings.** State notice filings, if any, required in connection with the admission of the final closing LPs should be confirmed and completed as applicable. I will review applicable state filing requirements and coordinate with Bridgeford Hollis LLP. *Responsible party: Sandra K. Liu, with support from Bridgeford Hollis LLP.*

I believe each of the foregoing items is on track for timely completion and does not present a material risk to the closing timeline.

**[XIII. Conclusion and Recommendation]{.underline}**

Based on the foregoing review, I believe that the General Partner is substantially prepared to proceed with the final closing of Whitmore Capital Partners VII, LP on January 31, 2025. All material conditions precedent under Section 11.2 of the LPA are either satisfied or are expected to be satisfied at or prior to the closing date. The outstanding deliverables described in Section XII are ministerial in nature and are on track for completion.

I recommend that Marcus J. Whitmore, in his capacity as Managing Member of the General Partner, authorize the final closing to proceed as scheduled, subject to the satisfactory completion of the outstanding items identified in Section XII above. I will continue to coordinate with Bridgeford Hollis LLP, Oakmere Fund Services LLC, and the Fund\'s other service providers through the closing date to ensure that all deliverables are in order and that the closing is executed smoothly.

Please do not hesitate to contact me if you have any questions regarding this memorandum or any aspect of the final closing readiness assessment.

Sandra K. Liu Chief Compliance Officer and General Counsel Whitmore Capital Management LLC 400 Lexington Tower, Suite 2200 Chicago, IL 60601 Direct: (312) 555-4718

Email: sliu@whitmorecapital.com

**[Enclosures]{.underline}**

The following documents are referenced herein and enclosed separately for your review:

> **Enclosure A:** Draft Schedule A --- Commitment Schedule (All Closings) \[prepared by Oakmere Fund Services LLC\]
>
> **Enclosure B:** Final Closing LP Subscription Agreement Summary \[prepared by Bridgeford Hollis LLP\]
>
> **Enclosure C:** AML/KYC Completion Email from Oakmere Fund Services LLC, dated January 24, 2025 (from Jonathan C. Prewitt)
>
> **Enclosure D:** Capital Call Notice No. 1 (October 10, 2024) and Capital Call Notice No. 2 (December 15, 2024)

CONFIDENTIAL --- ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGED --- FOR INTERNAL USE ONLY This memorandum is prepared by counsel for Whitmore Capital Management LLC in its capacity as General Partner of Whitmore Capital Partners VII, LP. This memorandum constitutes attorney-client privileged communication and attorney work product. It is intended solely for the use of the addressee and should not be distributed, reproduced, or disclosed to any third party without the prior written consent of Sandra K. Liu.

**[AMENDMENT NO. 1]{.underline}**

**[TO THE]{.underline}**

**[AMENDED AND RESTATED AGREEMENT OF LIMITED PARTNERSHIP]{.underline}**

**[OF]{.underline}**

**[WHITMORE CAPITAL PARTNERS VII, LP]{.underline}**

Dated as of November 30, 2024

This **Amendment No. 1 to the Amended and Restated Agreement of Limited Partnership** (this \"Amendment\") of **Whitmore Capital Partners VII, LP**, a Delaware limited partnership (Delaware Secretary of State File No. 7842319) (the \"Partnership\" or the \"Fund\"), is entered into as of November 30, 2024, by **Whitmore Capital Management LLC**, a Delaware limited liability company, in its capacity as the general partner of the Partnership (the \"General Partner\"). This Amendment amends the Amended and Restated Agreement of Limited Partnership of the Partnership, dated as of September 15, 2024 (as the same may be amended, restated, supplemented, or otherwise modified from time to time, the \"Partnership Agreement\" or the \"Original Agreement\").

**[RECITALS]{.underline}**

**WHEREAS**, the Partnership was formed as a Delaware limited partnership on August 1, 2024, by the filing of a Certificate of Limited Partnership with the Secretary of State of the State of Delaware (File No. 7842319), pursuant to and in accordance with the Delaware Revised Uniform Limited Partnership Act, 6 Del. C. § 17-101, *et seq.* (the \"DRULPA\");

**WHEREAS**, the General Partner, Whitmore Capital Management LLC, and the limited partners party thereto entered into the Amended and Restated Agreement of Limited Partnership dated as of September 15, 2024 (the \"Original Agreement\"), in connection with the initial closing of the Partnership (the \"Initial Closing\");

**WHEREAS**, the Initial Closing occurred on September 15, 2024, at which time eighteen (18) limited partners were admitted to the Partnership with aggregate Limited Partner Capital Commitments of Nine Hundred Forty Million Dollars (\$940,000,000), together with the General Partner\'s Capital Commitment of Fifteen Million Dollars (\$15,000,000), for aggregate Capital Commitments of Nine Hundred Fifty-Five Million Dollars (\$955,000,000);

**WHEREAS**, Section 11.1(b) of the Original Agreement authorizes the General Partner to hold one or more subsequent closings following the Initial Closing, at which additional Limited Partners may be admitted to the Partnership and existing Limited Partners may increase their Capital Commitments, in each case subject to the terms and conditions set forth in the Original Agreement;

**WHEREAS**, Section 14.3 of the Original Agreement permits the General Partner to amend the Partnership Agreement without the consent of the Limited Partners (a) to reflect the admission of new Limited Partners at subsequent closings and the related updating of Schedule A thereto, and (b) to make technical corrections, clarifications, or ministerial changes that do not materially and adversely affect any Limited Partner;

**WHEREAS**, the General Partner desires to hold a second closing (the \"Second Closing\") on the date hereof, at which nine (9) additional Limited Partners will be admitted to the Partnership with aggregate Capital Commitments of Three Hundred Seventy-Five Million Dollars (\$375,000,000);

**WHEREAS**, after giving effect to the Second Closing, the aggregate Capital Commitments to the Partnership will be One Billion Three Hundred Thirty Million Dollars (\$1,330,000,000), comprised of One Billion Three Hundred Fifteen Million Dollars (\$1,315,000,000) of Limited Partner Capital Commitments and Fifteen Million Dollars (\$15,000,000) of General Partner Capital Commitments, which amount does not exceed the Hard Cap of One Billion Seven Hundred Fifty Million Dollars (\$1,750,000,000) established under Section 3.1(a) of the Original Agreement and remains below the Target Fund Size of One Billion Five Hundred Million Dollars (\$1,500,000,000) established thereunder;

**WHEREAS**, in connection with the Second Closing, the General Partner has identified a technical error in Section 8.3(b) of the Original Agreement relating to the distribution waterfall that the General Partner desires to correct pursuant to this Amendment;

**WHEREAS**, the General Partner has determined that such correction is a technical clarification that does not materially and adversely affect any Limited Partner and is permitted under Section 14.3(b) of the Original Agreement without the consent of the Limited Partners;

**WHEREAS**, Thomas A. Riley, a duly authorized representative of Birchwood Insurance Co. (a Second Closing Limited Partner), has been appointed to the Advisory Committee pursuant to Section 9.1(b) of the Original Agreement, filling one of the five (5) Advisory Committee seats designated under the Original Agreement; and

**WHEREAS**, capitalized terms used herein and not otherwise defined shall have the meanings ascribed to such terms in the Original Agreement.

**NOW, THEREFORE**, in consideration of the foregoing recitals and other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the General Partner hereby amends the Original Agreement as follows:

**[SECTION 1 --- AMENDMENTS TO THE PARTNERSHIP AGREEMENT]{.underline}**

**Section 1.1 --- Admission of Second Closing Limited Partners**

\(a\) Schedule A to the Original Agreement is hereby amended and restated in its entirety to reflect the admission of nine (9) additional Limited Partners as of the Second Closing Date (November 30, 2024), as set forth on the Revised Schedule A attached hereto as **Exhibit A** (the \"Revised Schedule A\"). The Revised Schedule A shall replace, in its entirety, the prior Schedule A to the Original Agreement, and all references in the Original Agreement to \"Schedule A\" shall hereafter be deemed to refer to the Revised Schedule A, as the same may be further amended from time to time.

\(b\) The aggregate Capital Commitments of the Second Closing Limited Partners are Three Hundred Seventy-Five Million Dollars (\$375,000,000). Each Second Closing Limited Partner has executed and delivered to the General Partner a Subscription Agreement in the form prescribed by the General Partner and has executed and delivered signature pages to the Partnership Agreement, and has satisfied all conditions precedent to admission set forth in Section 11.2 of the Original Agreement. The General Partner hereby confirms that each Second Closing Limited Partner is a \"qualified purchaser\" within the meaning of Section 2(a)(51) of the Investment Company Act of 1940, as amended, and an \"accredited investor\" within the meaning of Regulation D under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended.

\(c\) Anti-money laundering and know-your-customer verification for each Second Closing Limited Partner has been completed by Oakmere Fund Services LLC, the Fund\'s third-party administrator, in accordance with the General Partner\'s compliance policies and applicable law.

\(d\) In accordance with Section 3.2(c) of the Original Agreement, equalization contributions and equalization interest are due from each Second Closing Limited Partner with respect to Capital Call No. 1 (dated October 10, 2024, which called ten percent (10%) of Capital Commitments from all then-existing partners). Equalization interest shall be calculated at the rate of eight percent (8%) per annum (simple interest) from October 10, 2024 through and including November 30, 2024, on the pro rata share of the capital call amount attributable to each such Second Closing Limited Partner. The General Partner shall deliver to each Second Closing Limited Partner a notice setting forth its respective equalization contribution and equalization interest amounts promptly following the Second Closing Date.

**Section 1.2 --- Technical Correction to Section 8.3(b) (Distribution Waterfall)**

\(a\) The General Partner has identified a technical error in Section 8.3(b) of the Original Agreement relating to the second tier of the distribution waterfall (the General Partner\'s \"catch-up\" allocation). As originally drafted, Section 8.3(b) stated that distributions in the second tier would be made to the General Partner in an amount equal to \"20% of the aggregate amount distributed pursuant to Section 8.3(a) and this Section 8.3(b).\" This formulation was technically imprecise and could be read to provide for a flat allocation of twenty percent (20%) of first-tier distributions to the General Partner, rather than the intended true catch-up mechanism pursuant to which one hundred percent (100%) of distributable proceeds are allocated to the General Partner until the General Partner has received, in the aggregate, twenty percent (20%) of all amounts distributed under the first two tiers of the waterfall combined.

\(b\) Section 8.3(b) of the Original Agreement is hereby deleted in its entirety and replaced with the following:

> \"**Second**, 100% to the General Partner until the cumulative amount distributed to the General Partner pursuant to this Section 8.3(b) equals twenty percent (20%) of the sum of (x) the cumulative amounts distributed to the Limited Partners pursuant to Section 8.3(a) and (y) the cumulative amounts distributed to the General Partner pursuant to this Section 8.3(b) (the \'GP Catch-Up\');\"

\(c\) The General Partner hereby confirms that this correction is a technical clarification that does not alter the economic intent of the distribution waterfall as described to the Limited Partners in the Fund\'s Confidential Private Placement Memorandum dated July 2024 and is fully consistent with the terms presented to prospective investors during the fundraising process. The correction is authorized under Section 14.3(b) of the Original Agreement as a technical correction that does not materially and adversely affect any Limited Partner.

**Section 1.3 --- Appointment to Advisory Committee**

\(a\) The General Partner hereby confirms that Thomas A. Riley, as duly authorized representative of Birchwood Insurance Co. (a Second Closing Limited Partner with a Capital Commitment of Seventy-Five Million Dollars (\$75,000,000)), is hereby appointed as a member of the Advisory Committee of the Partnership pursuant to Section 9.1(b) of the Original Agreement, effective as of the Second Closing Date.

\(b\) Following such appointment, the Advisory Committee is comprised of four (4) members, as follows:

> \(1\) Rachel M. Torres, representative of Silvermark Capital Advisors;
>
> \(2\) Kenneth W. Park, representative of Commonwealth Public Employees\' Retirement Fund;
>
> \(3\) Diane L. Voss, representative of Graystone Foundation; and
>
> \(4\) Thomas A. Riley, representative of Birchwood Insurance Co.

One (1) seat on the Advisory Committee remains vacant and may be filled from among the Limited Partners admitted at a subsequent closing, as provided in Section 9.1(b) of the Original Agreement.

**Section 1.4 --- Updated Schedule A**

The Revised Schedule A attached hereto as **Exhibit A** reflects: (a) all Limited Partners admitted at the Initial Closing (eighteen (18) Limited Partners, with aggregate Limited Partner Capital Commitments of \$940,000,000); (b) the General Partner\'s Capital Commitment of \$15,000,000; and (c) all Limited Partners admitted at the Second Closing (nine (9) Limited Partners, with aggregate Limited Partner Capital Commitments of \$375,000,000). Total aggregate Capital Commitments reflected on the Revised Schedule A are One Billion Three Hundred Thirty Million Dollars (\$1,330,000,000). The Revised Schedule A shall be further amended and restated to reflect any additional Limited Partners admitted at subsequent closings, including the final closing, if any.

**[SECTION 2 --- REPRESENTATIONS AND CONFIRMATIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 2.1 --- No Other Amendments**

Except as expressly amended by this Amendment, the Original Agreement remains in full force and effect and is hereby ratified and confirmed in all respects. All references in the Original Agreement to \"this Agreement,\" \"the Partnership Agreement,\" \"the LPA,\" \"hereof,\" \"herein,\" \"hereunder,\" or words of similar import shall hereafter refer to the Original Agreement as amended by this Amendment.

**Section 2.2 --- General Partner Representations**

The General Partner hereby represents and warrants to the Limited Partners as follows, in each case as of the Second Closing Date:

> \(a\) All representations and warranties of the General Partner set forth in Article XII of the Original Agreement are true and correct in all material respects as of the Second Closing Date, with the same force and effect as though such representations and warranties had been made on and as of such date.
>
> \(b\) No Key Person Event (as defined in Section 9.4 of the Original Agreement) has occurred. Both Marcus J. Whitmore and Priya S. Mehta (each a \"Key Person\" under the Original Agreement) continue to devote substantially all of their business time and professional efforts to the investment activities and affairs of the Partnership, as required under Section 9.4 of the Original Agreement.
>
> \(c\) No material adverse change has occurred with respect to the General Partner, the Investment Manager, or their respective key persons since the Initial Closing Date that would reasonably be expected to have a material adverse effect on the ability of the General Partner to fulfill its obligations under the Original Agreement.
>
> \(d\) The execution and delivery of this Amendment has been duly authorized by all necessary action on the part of the General Partner, and this Amendment constitutes the legal, valid, and binding obligation of the General Partner, enforceable against the General Partner in accordance with its terms, subject to applicable bankruptcy, insolvency, and similar laws affecting creditors\' rights generally and to general principles of equity.

**Section 2.3 --- Conflict Provision**

In the event of any conflict or inconsistency between the terms and provisions of this Amendment and the terms and provisions of the Original Agreement, the terms and provisions of this Amendment shall govern and control.

**[SECTION 3 --- MISCELLANEOUS]{.underline}**

**Section 3.1 --- Governing Law**

This Amendment shall be governed by, and construed and enforced in accordance with, the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to conflicts of law principles thereof that would result in the application of the laws of any other jurisdiction. To the extent not superseded by the terms of this Amendment or the Original Agreement, the provisions of the Delaware Revised Uniform Limited Partnership Act (6 Del. C. § 17-101, *et seq.*) shall apply to the Partnership and this Amendment.

**Section 3.2 --- Counterparts**

This Amendment may be executed in one or more counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Delivery of an executed counterpart of this Amendment by electronic transmission, including by portable document format (PDF) or other electronic means, shall be equally as effective as delivery of a manually executed counterpart of this Amendment.

**Section 3.3 --- Binding Effect**

This Amendment shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors, permitted assigns, heirs, executors, administrators, and legal representatives. Each Limited Partner admitted at the Second Closing shall be bound by and subject to all of the terms and provisions of the Original Agreement, as amended by this Amendment, as if such Limited Partner had been an original signatory thereto.

**Section 3.4 --- Entire Agreement**

The Original Agreement, as amended by this Amendment, together with the Subscription Agreements, the Side Letters, and any other agreements entered into in connection with the Partnership, constitutes the entire agreement among the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements, negotiations, representations, and understandings with respect thereto.

**Section 3.5 --- Effectiveness**

This Amendment shall be effective as of November 30, 2024 (the \"Second Closing Date\").

*\[Signature Page Follows\]*

**IN WITNESS WHEREOF**, the undersigned has executed this Amendment No. 1 to the Amended and Restated Agreement of Limited Partnership of Whitmore Capital Partners VII, LP as of the date first written above.

**GENERAL PARTNER:**

**WHITMORE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LLC** *in its capacity as General Partner of Whitmore Capital Partners VII, LP*

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Name: Marcus J. Whitmore

Title: Managing Member and Chief Investment Officer

Date: November 30, 2024

Address:

400 Lexington Tower, Suite 2200 Chicago, IL 60601

**ACKNOWLEDGMENT REGARDING LIMITED PARTNER CONSENT**

Pursuant to Section 14.3 of the Original Agreement, this Amendment does not require the consent of the Limited Partners, as it (a) reflects the admission of Limited Partners at a subsequent closing authorized under Section 11.1(b) of the Original Agreement and the related updating of Schedule A thereto, and (b) effects a technical correction to Section 8.3(b) that does not materially and adversely affect any Limited Partner, as permitted under Section 14.3(b) of the Original Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Advisory Committee has been consulted regarding the technical correction to Section 8.3(b) as a matter of good governance, and no objection to such correction was raised by any member of the Advisory Committee.

This Subscription Agreement (the \"Subscription Agreement\") is entered into by and between the undersigned investor (the \"Subscriber\" or \"Limited Partner\") and **Whitmore Capital Management LLC**, a Delaware limited liability company (the \"General Partner\"), acting on behalf of **Whitmore Capital Partners VII, LP**, a Delaware limited partnership (the \"Fund\" or the \"Partnership\").

The Fund was formed as a Delaware limited partnership on August 1, 2024, pursuant to a Certificate of Limited Partnership filed with the Secretary of State of the State of Delaware (File No. 7842319). The Fund is governed by that certain Amended and Restated Agreement of Limited Partnership, dated as of September 15, 2024, as amended by Amendment No. 1, dated November 30, 2024 (as further amended, restated, supplemented, or otherwise modified from time to time, the \"Partnership Agreement\" or \"LPA\"). The General Partner\'s principal office is located at 400 Lexington Tower, Suite 2200, Chicago, IL 60601 (Employer Identification Number: 84-3291056). The Fund\'s registered agent in the State of Delaware is Statehouse Registered Agents, Inc., 1301 Market Street, Wilmington, DE 19801.

The Fund is conducting its final closing (the \"Final Closing\") on January 31, 2025, having previously held an initial closing on September 15, 2024 (the \"Initial Closing\") and a second closing on November 30, 2024 (the \"Second Closing\"). The Final Closing is the last closing at which investors may be admitted to the Fund as Limited Partners.

The Fund\'s investment objective is to generate superior risk-adjusted returns by investing primarily in middle-market buyout opportunities in the healthcare services and technology-enabled services sectors, as more fully described in the LPA. The Fund seeks to acquire controlling or significant minority equity positions in established companies within these sectors, with enterprise values generally ranging between \$150,000,000 and \$750,000,000 at the time of initial investment.

The Fund\'s target aggregate Capital Commitments are \$1,500,000,000 (the \"Fund Target\"), subject to a hard cap of \$1,750,000,000 (the \"Hard Cap\") as set forth in Section 3.1 of the LPA. As of the Final Closing, aggregate Capital Commitments (including the Subscriber\'s commitment and those of all other investors being admitted at the Final Closing) are expected to total \$1,615,000,000, consisting of \$1,600,000,000 in LP commitments and \$15,000,000 in GP commitments. This amount exceeds the Fund Target of \$1,500,000,000 by \$115,000,000, or approximately 7.67%, but remains below the Hard Cap of \$1,750,000,000 by \$135,000,000. Pursuant to Section 3.1(d) of the LPA, the General Partner is required to notify the Advisory Committee when aggregate Capital Commitments exceed the Fund Target, and such notification is a condition to the acceptance of commitments above the Fund Target.

The Fund relies on the exemption from registration as an investment company provided by Section 3(c)(7) of the Investment Company Act of 1940, as amended (the \"Investment Company Act\"), which requires that each beneficial owner of the Fund\'s outstanding securities is a \"qualified purchaser\" as defined in Section 2(a)(51) of the Investment Company Act. The Fund\'s investment adviser, Whitmore Capital Management LLC, is registered with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the \"SEC\") under the Investment Advisers Act of 1940, as amended (CRD No. 298417; SEC File No. 801-79834).

Capitalized terms used but not defined in this Subscription Agreement shall have the meanings ascribed to such terms in the LPA.

**NOW, THEREFORE**, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements contained herein and in the LPA, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the Subscriber and the General Partner agree as follows:

**[SECTION 1: SUBSCRIPTION AND CAPITAL COMMITMENT]{.underline}**

**1.1 Subscription.** The Subscriber hereby irrevocably subscribes for a limited partnership interest (the \"Interest\") in the Fund in the amount set forth on the signature page hereto as the Subscriber\'s Capital Commitment (the \"Capital Commitment\"). By executing this Subscription Agreement, the Subscriber agrees to be bound by all of the terms and conditions of the LPA, as amended from time to time, and this Subscription Agreement, and to perform all obligations required of a Limited Partner thereunder. The Subscriber acknowledges and agrees that the General Partner may, in its sole and absolute discretion, accept or reject this subscription, in whole or in part, for any reason or for no reason, and may reduce the amount of the Subscriber\'s Capital Commitment. Upon acceptance of this subscription by the General Partner, the Subscriber shall be admitted to the Fund as a Limited Partner as of the Final Closing Date (January 31, 2025) and shall thereafter have all of the rights and obligations of a Limited Partner under the LPA. The General Partner shall notify the Subscriber in writing of its acceptance or rejection of this subscription promptly following the Final Closing.

**1.2 Capital Contributions**

The Subscriber agrees to make capital contributions to the Fund from time to time in accordance with capital calls (each, a \"Capital Call\") issued by the General Partner pursuant to Section 3.2(a) of the LPA, in amounts up to the Subscriber\'s unfunded Capital Commitment. Capital Calls shall be made upon not less than ten (10) Business Days\' prior written notice to the Subscriber, specifying the amount of the capital contribution required, the purpose thereof, and the date on which such capital contribution is due (the \"Due Date\"). Each Capital Call notice shall be delivered in accordance with the notice provisions set forth in Section 8.6 hereof and the LPA. The Subscriber acknowledges that failure to fund a Capital Call on or before the applicable Due Date shall constitute a default under the LPA, subjecting the Subscriber to the default remedies set forth in Section 3.5 of the LPA, which may include, among other things, the forfeiture of a portion of the Subscriber\'s Interest, the charging of default interest at a rate of the lesser of 18% per annum and the maximum rate permitted by applicable law, and the forced sale or transfer of the Subscriber\'s Interest.

**1.3 Equalization Contributions and Interest**

The Subscriber acknowledges and agrees that, as a Subsequent Closing Investor being admitted at the Final Closing, the Subscriber is required pursuant to Section 3.2(c) of the LPA to fund, on or prior to the Final Closing Date, the following amounts:

> \(a\) A catch-up capital contribution (the \"Catch-Up Contribution\") equal to the Subscriber\'s pro rata share of all Capital Calls made by the General Partner prior to the Final Closing Date, calculated as if the Subscriber had been admitted to the Fund as a Limited Partner at the Initial Closing; and
>
> \(b\) Equalization interest (the \"Equalization Interest\") on the Catch-Up Contribution at a rate of eight percent (8%) per annum (simple interest), calculated from the date of each prior Capital Call through the Final Closing Date.

The General Partner has made two (2) Capital Calls prior to the Final Closing:

> \(i\) **Capital Call No. 1**, dated October 10, 2024, in an amount equal to ten percent (10%) of each Limited Partner\'s Capital Commitment; and
>
> \(ii\) **Capital Call No. 2**, dated December 15, 2024, in an amount equal to five percent (5%) of each Limited Partner\'s Capital Commitment.

Accordingly, the Subscriber\'s aggregate Catch-Up Contribution shall equal fifteen percent (15%) of the Subscriber\'s Capital Commitment (representing the sum of Capital Call No. 1 at 10% and Capital Call No. 2 at 5%).

Equalization Interest on the Catch-Up Contribution shall be calculated as follows:

> \(i\) With respect to the portion of the Catch-Up Contribution attributable to Capital Call No. 1: the product of (A) the Subscriber\'s Capital Commitment multiplied by ten percent (10%), multiplied by (B) eight percent (8%), multiplied by (C) a fraction, the numerator of which is 113 and the denominator of which is 365, where 113 represents the number of days from October 10, 2024 through January 31, 2025; and
>
> \(ii\) With respect to the portion of the Catch-Up Contribution attributable to Capital Call No. 2: the product of (A) the Subscriber\'s Capital Commitment multiplied by five percent (5%), multiplied by (B) eight percent (8%), multiplied by (C) a fraction, the numerator of which is 47 and the denominator of which is 365, where 47 represents the number of days from December 15, 2024 through January 31, 2025.

The total amount due from the Subscriber at the Final Closing (the \"Final Closing Payment\") shall be the sum of the Catch-Up Contribution and the Equalization Interest, as calculated by Oakmere Fund Services LLC, the Fund\'s administrator (the \"Fund Administrator\"), and set forth in a closing statement to be delivered to the Subscriber prior to the Final Closing Date. The Equalization Interest paid by the Subscriber shall be distributed to the Limited Partners admitted at prior closings as additional return on their capital contributions, in accordance with Section 3.2(c)(iii) of the LPA. The aggregate Catch-Up Contribution and Equalization Interest for all Final Closing investors is set forth in Schedule I hereto.

**1.4 Payment Instructions**

All capital contributions due at the Final Closing, including the Catch-Up Contribution and Equalization Interest, shall be made by wire transfer of immediately available funds in United States dollars to the following escrow account:

> **Bank:** Pinnacle National Bank **Account Name:** Whitmore Capital Partners VII, LP --- Subscription Escrow **Account Number:** ending in 9213 **ABA Routing Number:** 071923456 **Reference:** \[Subscriber Name\] --- Final Closing Capital Contribution

The Subscriber\'s Final Closing Payment must be received in the escrow account no later than 12:00 p.m. (Eastern Time) on January 31, 2025. Funds received after such time may, in the General Partner\'s sole discretion, be deemed not timely received and may result in the rejection of the Subscriber\'s subscription. Upon acceptance of the subscription and completion of the Final Closing, funds held in escrow shall be released by the escrow agent to the Fund\'s operating account at Pinnacle National Bank (Account No. ending in 8847) in accordance with the terms of the escrow agreement entered into by the General Partner, the Fund, and Pinnacle National Bank.

If the Subscriber\'s Capital Commitment equals or exceeds the MFN Threshold, the General Partner shall, within thirty (30) days following the Final Closing, provide the Subscriber with a written summary of material side letter provisions granted to other Limited Partners that are eligible for MFN elections pursuant to Section 12.3 of the LPA. The Subscriber acknowledges that its obligation to fund Capital Calls is unconditional and is not subject to any right of set-off, counterclaim, defense, or abatement. The Subscriber further acknowledges that the General Partner and the other Limited Partners will be relying on the Subscriber\'s commitment to fund Capital Calls in a timely manner.

**4.2 Confidentiality.** The Subscriber agrees to maintain the confidentiality of all non-public information regarding the Fund, the General Partner, and the Fund\'s portfolio investments, including, without limitation, the terms of the LPA, information regarding the identities and commitments of other Limited Partners, and financial, operational, and performance information relating to the Fund and its portfolio companies (collectively, \"Confidential Information\"). The Subscriber shall not disclose any Confidential Information to any third party, except for disclosures (a) required by applicable law, regulation, or judicial or administrative process (including, but not limited to, a subpoena, civil investigative demand, or order of a court or governmental authority), provided that the Subscriber shall, to the extent legally permitted, provide the General Partner with prompt written notice of any such required disclosure; (b) made to the Subscriber\'s officers, directors, employees, advisors, auditors, legal counsel, and consultants on a need-to-know basis, provided that such persons are subject to confidentiality obligations at least as protective as those set forth herein; and (c) permitted under any side letter entered into between the Subscriber and the General Partner. The Subscriber acknowledges that if it is a governmental plan or other public entity, its confidentiality obligations may be subject to applicable freedom-of-information, public records, or open meeting laws, and any such limitations shall be addressed in the applicable side letter, if any.

**4.3 Notification Obligations**

The Subscriber covenants and agrees to promptly notify the General Partner in writing if, at any time during the term of the Fund:

> \(a\) Any representation or warranty made by the Subscriber in this Subscription Agreement ceases to be true and correct in any material respect;
>
> \(b\) The Subscriber undergoes a \"change of control\" (as defined in the LPA), a merger, consolidation, or reorganization, or any change in its organizational structure that could affect its obligations under this Subscription Agreement or the LPA;
>
> \(c\) The Subscriber\'s ERISA status changes, including any change that would cause the Subscriber to become, or to cease to be, a \"benefit plan investor\" as defined in Section 3(42) of ERISA; or
>
> \(d\) The Subscriber, or any Related Person, becomes the subject of any Sanctions or becomes listed on any OFAC sanctions list or any similar list maintained by a governmental authority.

**4.4 Cooperation with Regulatory Requirements**

The Subscriber covenants and agrees to cooperate with the General Partner and the Fund Administrator in connection with any regulatory filings required to be made by the Fund or the General Partner, including, without limitation, Form D filings with the SEC and applicable state securities regulators, blue sky filings, Form PF filings, FATCA/CRS reporting, beneficial ownership reporting, and any other filings required under the Securities Act, the Exchange Act, the Investment Advisers Act of 1940, or other applicable federal, state, or foreign law. The Subscriber shall provide such information and documentation as may be reasonably requested by the General Partner or the Fund Administrator in a timely manner to facilitate such filings.

**[SECTION 5: CONDITIONS TO ACCEPTANCE]{.underline}**

**5.1 Conditions to Acceptance of Subscription**

The obligation of the General Partner to accept this subscription and admit the Subscriber as a Limited Partner of the Fund at the Final Closing is subject to the satisfaction (or waiver by the General Partner in its sole discretion) of each of the following conditions:

> \(a\) The General Partner shall have received this Subscription Agreement, duly executed and delivered by the Subscriber, together with the Subscriber\'s duly executed counterpart signature page to the LPA and all other documents required to be delivered by the Subscriber in connection with the Final Closing;
>
> \(b\) The General Partner shall have received satisfactory AML/KYC documentation for the Subscriber, as verified by Oakmere Fund Services LLC as Fund Administrator, and the General Partner shall have determined, in its reasonable discretion, that the results of such verification are satisfactory;
>
> \(c\) The Subscriber shall have confirmed, through the representations contained herein and in the Investor Questionnaire, its status as a \"qualified purchaser\" within the meaning of Section 2(a)(51) of the Investment Company Act;
>
> \(d\) The admission of the Subscriber shall not cause the aggregate Capital Commitments of the Fund (including the GP Commitment) to exceed the Hard Cap of \$1,750,000,000 as set forth in Section 3.1 of the LPA;
>
> \(e\) The admission of the Subscriber shall not cause the aggregate Capital Commitments of benefit plan investors (as determined in accordance with Section 6.8 of the LPA) to exceed 24.99% of total Capital Commitments;
>
> \(f\) All conditions precedent to the Final Closing set forth in Section 11.2 of the LPA shall have been satisfied or waived by the General Partner;
>
> \(g\) The General Partner shall have received the Subscriber\'s Final Closing Payment (including the Catch-Up Contribution and Equalization Interest) in immediately available funds in the escrow account designated in Section 1.4 hereof; and
>
> \(h\) The General Partner shall have determined, in its sole and absolute discretion, that the Subscriber is an appropriate investor for the Fund, taking into account such factors as the General Partner deems relevant, including the Subscriber\'s financial condition, investment sophistication, regulatory status, and the potential impact of the Subscriber\'s admission on the Fund and the other Limited Partners.

As of the Final Closing, aggregate Capital Commitments are expected to total \$1,615,000,000, which is below the Hard Cap of \$1,750,000,000, and condition (d) is expected to be satisfied. The Fund Target of \$1,500,000,000 is exceeded by \$115,000,000, which triggers the Advisory Committee notification requirement under Section 3.1(d) of the LPA. As of the Final Closing, benefit plan investor commitments are expected to total \$355,300,000, representing approximately 22.00% of total Capital Commitments, which is below the ERISA Threshold of 24.99%, and condition (e) is expected to be satisfied.

**[SECTION 6: SIDE LETTERS]{.underline}**

**6.1 Side Letter Provisions**

The Subscriber acknowledges that the General Partner has entered into, or may enter into, side letters or supplemental agreements with certain Limited Partners granting supplemental, modified, or additional terms with respect to such Limited Partners\' investments in the Fund. If the Subscriber and the General Partner enter into a side letter in connection with the Final Closing, such side letter shall be deemed a part of this Subscription Agreement and the LPA solely as between the Subscriber and the General Partner and shall not modify the rights or obligations of any other Limited Partner, except as expressly provided in the LPA or the applicable side letter. Side letter provisions are subject to the MFN election rights described in Section 3.6 above and Section 12.3 of the LPA, as applicable. The Subscriber acknowledges that certain side letter provisions may be excluded from the MFN election process to the extent that such provisions relate to a particular Limited Partner\'s specific legal, regulatory, tax, or organizational circumstances and are not generally applicable to other Limited Partners.

**[SECTION 7: INDEMNIFICATION]{.underline}**

**7.1 Subscriber Indemnification**

The Subscriber agrees to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the Fund, the General Partner, and their respective affiliates, members, managers, partners, officers, directors, employees, agents, and representatives (collectively, the \"Indemnified Parties\") from and against any and all losses, claims, damages, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys\' fees and expenses of investigation) arising out of, resulting from, or relating to:

> \(a\) Any breach of, or any inaccuracy in, any representation, warranty, or covenant made by the Subscriber in this Subscription Agreement or in the Investor Questionnaire;
>
> \(b\) Any inaccuracy in, or breach of, any information provided by the Subscriber in connection with this Subscription Agreement, the Investor Questionnaire, AML/KYC documentation, or any other document or instrument delivered by the Subscriber to the Fund, the General Partner, or the Fund Administrator; or
>
> \(c\) Any failure by the Subscriber to fund capital contributions when due in accordance with the LPA and Capital Call notices issued by the General Partner.

The Subscriber\'s indemnification obligations under this Section 7.1 shall survive the execution and delivery of this Subscription Agreement, the termination or expiration of this Subscription Agreement, the dissolution, winding up, and final liquidation of the Fund, and the withdrawal, removal, or transfer of the Subscriber\'s Interest in the Fund. The indemnification provided herein is in addition to, and not in limitation of, any other indemnification or remedies available to the Indemnified Parties under the LPA or applicable law.

**[SECTION 8: GENERAL PROVISIONS]{.underline}**

**8.1 Governing Law**

This Subscription Agreement shall be governed by, and construed and enforced in accordance with, the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to principles of conflicts of law that would result in the application of the laws of any other jurisdiction.

**8.2 Dispute Resolution**

Any dispute, controversy, or claim arising out of or relating to this Subscription Agreement, or the breach, termination, or validity thereof, shall be resolved in accordance with the dispute resolution provisions set forth in Section 14.6 of the LPA, which provisions are incorporated herein by this reference.

**8.3 Entire Agreement**

This Subscription Agreement, together with the LPA (including all amendments thereto), any side letter entered into between the Subscriber and the General Partner in connection with the Fund, the Investor Questionnaire (Exhibit A hereto), and all schedules and exhibits hereto, constitutes the entire agreement between the Subscriber and the General Partner with respect to the Subscriber\'s subscription for and investment in the Fund and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous negotiations, discussions, agreements, and understandings, whether written or oral, relating to the subject matter hereof.

**8.4 Amendment.** This Subscription Agreement may not be amended, modified, or supplemented except by a written instrument duly executed by both the Subscriber and the General Partner. No waiver of any provision of this Subscription Agreement shall be effective unless in writing and signed by the party granting such waiver.

**8.5 Severability.** If any provision of this Subscription Agreement is held by a court of competent jurisdiction or other governmental authority to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, such invalidity, illegality, or unenforceability shall not affect any other provision of this Subscription Agreement, and the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect. To the extent permitted by applicable law, any invalid, illegal, or unenforceable provision shall be modified to the minimum extent necessary to render it valid, legal, and enforceable while preserving the original intent of the parties.

**8.6 Notices.** All notices, requests, consents, demands, and other communications under this Subscription Agreement shall be in writing and shall be delivered in accordance with the notice provisions of the LPA. Notices to the General Partner shall be addressed to:

> Whitmore Capital Management LLC 400 Lexington Tower, Suite 2200 Chicago, IL 60601

Notices to the Fund Administrator shall be addressed to:

>

Notices to the Subscriber shall be addressed to the address set forth on the Subscriber\'s signature page hereto, or to such other address as the Subscriber may specify in writing to the General Partner from time to time.

**8.7 Counterparts; Electronic Execution**

This Subscription Agreement may be executed in one or more counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original and all of which, taken together, shall constitute one and the same instrument. Facsimile signatures and electronic signatures (including signatures made through DocuSign, Adobe Sign, or similar electronic signature platforms) shall be treated as original signatures for all purposes and shall be legally binding and enforceable.

**8.8 Assignment.** The Subscriber may not assign or transfer this Subscription Agreement or any of its rights or obligations hereunder, in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of the General Partner, which consent may be withheld in the General Partner\'s sole discretion. Any attempted assignment in violation of this Section 8.8 shall be null and void. The foregoing restriction shall not limit the Subscriber\'s right to transfer its Interest in the Fund in accordance with the transfer provisions of Article X of the LPA.

**8.9 Power of Attorney**

The Subscriber hereby irrevocably constitutes and appoints the General Partner, and each of its authorized officers and representatives, as the Subscriber\'s true and lawful attorney-in-fact and agent, with full power of substitution, coupled with an interest, to execute, acknowledge, swear to, deliver, file, and record, in the Subscriber\'s name, place, and stead, any and all documents, instruments, and certificates that may be necessary, appropriate, or advisable in connection with the formation, qualification, continuation, operation, and dissolution of the Fund, including, without limitation, amendments to the Certificate of Limited Partnership and the LPA, as more fully described in Section 14.2 of the LPA. This power of attorney shall survive the death, dissolution, disability, incapacity, or bankruptcy of the Subscriber and the transfer of the Subscriber\'s Interest in the Fund, and shall extend to the Subscriber\'s heirs, successors, assigns, and legal representatives.
