**[I. Authorization and LPA Reference]{.underline}**

Ladies and Gentlemen:

Whitmore Capital Management LLC (the \"General Partner\") hereby issues this Capital Call Notice No. 1 (this \"Notice\") pursuant to **Section 3.2(a)** of the Amended and Restated Agreement of Limited Partnership of Whitmore Capital Partners VII, LP (the \"Fund\"), dated as of September 15, 2024 (as amended, supplemented, or otherwise modified from time to time, the \"Partnership Agreement\" or \"LPA\"). Section 3.2(a) of the Partnership Agreement authorizes the General Partner to call capital from the Partners from time to time during the Investment Period upon not less than ten (10) business days\' prior written notice. The Investment Period commenced on September 15, 2024 and, unless earlier terminated in accordance with the terms of the Partnership Agreement, will continue through September 15, 2029. Capitalized terms used but not defined in this Notice shall have the meanings ascribed to such terms in the Partnership Agreement.

**[II. Capital Call Details]{.underline}**

The General Partner hereby calls **ten percent (10%)** of each Partner\'s Capital Commitment, as set forth below:

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
                                                        **Amount**
  ----------------------------------------------------- -----------------------------------
  Total LP Capital Commitments (Initial Closing)        \$940,000,000

  GP Capital Commitment                                 \$15,000,000

  **Total Capital Commitments as of the date hereof**   **\$955,000,000**
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The aggregate Capital Contributions being called pursuant to this Notice are as follows:

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
                          **Call Percentage**     **Capital Contribution Due**
  ----------------------- ----------------------- ------------------------------
  LP Portion              10%                     \$94,000,000

  GP Portion              10%                     \$1,500,000

  **Total**               **10%**                 **\$95,500,000**
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each Partner\'s individual Capital Contribution due in connection with this Capital Call is set forth on **Exhibit A** attached hereto.

**Drawdown Date.** All Capital Contributions called pursuant to this Notice must be received on or before **October 25, 2024** (the \"Drawdown Date\"), which date is ten (10) business days from the date of this Notice.

**[III. Purpose of Capital Call]{.underline}**

The Capital Contributions called pursuant to this Notice will be used for the following purposes, in such amounts and proportions as the General Partner shall determine in its sole discretion:

> **(a) Fund Investments.** To fund, in whole or in part, one or more portfolio investments in accordance with the Fund\'s investment strategy of pursuing middle-market buyout opportunities in the healthcare services and technology-enabled services sectors, including any related acquisition costs, transaction expenses, and co-investment arrangements;
>
> **(b) Fund Expenses.** To pay organizational expenses, management fees, broken deal expenses, and other Fund-related expenses as authorized under the Partnership Agreement; and
>
> **(c) Reserves.** To establish and maintain working capital reserves in such amounts as the General Partner deems necessary or appropriate in connection with the operations of the Fund and its portfolio investments.

**[IV. Wire Transfer Instructions]{.underline}**

All Capital Contributions must be made in United States dollars by wire transfer of immediately available funds to the following account:

> **Bank:** Pinnacle National Bank **ABA Routing Number:** 071923456 **Account Name:** Whitmore Capital Partners VII, LP --- Operating Account **Account Number:** xxxxxxxx8847 **Reference:** \"Capital Call No. 1 --- \[Partner Name\]\"

Each Partner should notify the Fund\'s administrator, Oakmere Fund Services LLC, upon completion of its wire transfer by sending written confirmation via email to the attention of Jonathan C. Prewitt, Director of Fund Operations, Oakmere Fund Services LLC, 1200 Harbor Boulevard, Suite 800, Stamford, CT 06902 (email: jprewitt@oakmerefundservices.com). Partners are requested to provide the wire confirmation number, the amount remitted, and the date of transfer.

**[V. Consequences of Failure to Fund]{.underline}**

Pursuant to **Section 3.5** of the Partnership Agreement, any Partner that fails to fund its Capital Contribution on or before the Drawdown Date shall be deemed a \"Defaulting Partner\" and shall be subject to the remedies set forth therein, including without limitation: (i) an interest charge on the delinquent amount at a rate of twelve percent (12%) per annum (or the maximum rate permitted by applicable law, if lower), accruing from the Drawdown Date until the date such amount is received in full; (ii) forfeiture of up to fifty percent (50%) of such Defaulting Partner\'s existing Capital Account balance as of the date of default; and (iii) such other remedies as may be available to the General Partner and the non-defaulting Partners under the Partnership Agreement or applicable law, including at equity. The General Partner hereby expressly reserves all rights and remedies under the Partnership Agreement and at law or in equity with respect to any failure by a Partner to fund its Capital Contribution when due.

**[VI. Equalization Provision Notice]{.underline}**

Partners are advised that, pursuant to **Section 3.2(c)** of the Partnership Agreement, any Limited Partners admitted to the Fund at a subsequent closing after the Initial Closing (each, a \"Subsequent Closing\") will be required to fund their pro rata share of all Capital Calls made prior to their admission, together with equalization interest thereon at the rate of **eight percent (8%) per annum**, computed on a simple interest basis, from the date of each such prior Capital Call through the date of the applicable Subsequent Closing at which such Limited Partner is admitted. Accordingly, this Capital Call Notice and the date hereof (**October 10, 2024**) shall be used in computing the equalization interest due from any Partners admitted at a Subsequent Closing with respect to their pro rata catch-up contribution related to this Capital Call.

**[VII. General Provisions]{.underline}**

This Notice is being delivered to all Partners in accordance with **Section 14.1** (Notices) of the Partnership Agreement. Delivery of this Notice is being made via email and overnight courier to each Partner\'s address or email address as set forth in the Partnership Agreement or as otherwise provided by such Partner in writing to the General Partner.

A copy of this Notice has been provided to Oakmere Fund Services LLC, in its capacity as Fund Administrator, and to Bridgeford Hollis LLP, in its capacity as Fund Counsel to the Fund.

Nothing in this Notice shall be deemed to modify, amend, supplement, or waive any provision of the Partnership Agreement or any Partner\'s obligations thereunder. In the event of any conflict or inconsistency between the terms of this Notice and the terms of the Partnership Agreement, the Partnership Agreement shall control in all respects.

**WHITMORE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LLC** 400 Lexington Tower, Suite 2200 Chicago, IL 60601

**CAPITAL CALL NOTICE NO. 2**

December 15, 2024

**To: All Partners of Whitmore Capital Partners VII, LP**

**[I. Authority and Background]{.underline}**

This Capital Call Notice No. 2 (this \"Notice\") is issued by Whitmore Capital Management LLC (the \"General Partner\"), in its capacity as the general partner of Whitmore Capital Partners VII, LP, a Delaware limited partnership (the \"Partnership\" or the \"Fund\"), pursuant to Section 3.2(a) of the Amended and Restated Agreement of Limited Partnership of the Partnership, dated as of September 15, 2024, as amended by Amendment No. 1 thereto dated November 30, 2024 (as so amended, the \"Partnership Agreement\" or \"LPA\"). Capitalized terms used but not defined herein shall have the meanings ascribed to such terms in the Partnership Agreement.

This Notice constitutes the second capital call issued by the General Partner since the formation of the Partnership. Capital Call Notice No. 1 was issued on October 10, 2024 and called ten percent (10%) of the Capital Commitments of all limited partners admitted at the Initial Closing on September 15, 2024. The Funding Date for Capital Call Notice No. 1 was October 24, 2024, and all amounts due thereunder were timely funded. This Notice is being delivered to all Partners admitted to the Partnership as of the date hereof, including all limited partners admitted at the Initial Closing and all limited partners admitted at the Second Closing on November 30, 2024, as well as the General Partner.

**[II. Capital Call Amount and Percentage]{.underline}**

The General Partner hereby calls **five percent (5%)** of each Partner\'s Capital Commitment (each such Partner\'s share, its \"Capital Call Amount\"), payable in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth below and in the Partnership Agreement. This capital call is being made from all Partners admitted to the Partnership as of the date of this Notice, December 15, 2024, comprising the following:

> \(a\) All eighteen (18) limited partners admitted at the Initial Closing on September 15, 2024, with aggregate Capital Commitments of Nine Hundred Forty Million Dollars (\$940,000,000);
>
> \(b\) All nine (9) limited partners admitted at the Second Closing on November 30, 2024, with aggregate Capital Commitments of Three Hundred Seventy-Five Million Dollars (\$375,000,000); and
>
> \(c\) The General Partner, with a Capital Commitment of Fifteen Million Dollars (\$15,000,000).

The aggregate amounts called under this Capital Call Notice No. 2 are as follows:

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Partner Category**                    **Aggregate Capital Commitments**   **Call Percentage**   **Capital Call No. 2 Amount**
  --------------------------------------- ----------------------------------- --------------------- -------------------------------
  Initial Closing Limited Partners (18)   \$940,000,000                       5%                    \$47,000,000

  Second Closing Limited Partners (9)     \$375,000,000                       5%                    \$18,750,000

  General Partner                         \$15,000,000                        5%                    \$750,000

  **Total**                               **\$1,330,000,000**                 **5%**                **\$66,500,000**
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each Partner\'s individual Capital Call Amount is set forth on **Exhibit A** attached hereto.

After giving effect to this Capital Call, the cumulative capital called from Initial Closing Partners will be fifteen percent (15%) of their respective Capital Commitments (being ten percent (10%) under Capital Call Notice No. 1 and five percent (5%) under this Notice), and the cumulative capital called from Second Closing Partners will be five percent (5%) of their respective Capital Commitments. The General Partner\'s cumulative capital called will be fifteen percent (15%) of its Capital Commitment.

**[III. Purpose of Capital Call]{.underline}**

The proceeds of this capital call will be applied by the General Partner for the following purposes:

> \(a\) **Portfolio Investments.** To fund prospective equity and equity-related investments in one or more portfolio companies consistent with the Fund\'s investment strategy of pursuing middle-market buyout opportunities in the healthcare services and technology-enabled services sectors, as described in the Partnership\'s Private Placement Memorandum dated August 1, 2024;
>
> \(b\) **Management Fees.** To fund the management fee obligations of the Partnership for the current quarter, as provided in Section 6.1 of the Partnership Agreement; and
>
> \(c\) **Organizational and Partnership Expenses.** To pay ongoing organizational expenses and partnership expenses as described in Section 7.2 of the Partnership Agreement, including legal, accounting, and administrative costs of the Partnership.

**[IV. Funding Date and Payment Instructions]{.underline}**

Each Partner must remit its Capital Call Amount in immediately available U.S. dollar funds no later than **ten (10) business days** from the date of this Notice, being **December 31, 2024** (the \"Funding Date\"), in accordance with Section 3.2(a) of the Partnership Agreement. Amounts must be received by the Partnership no later than 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time on the Funding Date.

All payments shall be made by wire transfer to the following account:

> **Bank:** Pinnacle National Bank **Account Name:** Whitmore Capital Partners VII, LP --- Operating Account **Account Number:** xxxxxxxxx8847 **ABA Routing Number:** 071923456 **Reference:** \[Partner Name\] --- Capital Call No. 2

Each Partner should notify the Fund Administrator, Oakmere Fund Services LLC, upon initiation of its wire transfer. Notification should be directed to:

> Jonathan C. Prewitt Director of Fund Operations Oakmere Fund Services LLC 1200 Harbor Boulevard, Suite 800 Stamford, CT 06902
>
> Email: jprewitt@oakmerefundservices.com
>
> Telephone: (203) 555-4180

**[V. Default Provisions]{.underline}**

Each Partner is reminded that, pursuant to Section 3.5 of the Partnership Agreement, any Partner that fails to fund its Capital Call Amount by the Funding Date shall be deemed a \"Defaulting Partner\" under the Partnership Agreement. A Defaulting Partner may be subject to, among other remedies available to the General Partner: (i) a default interest charge on the unpaid amount at a rate of twelve percent (12%) per annum, compounding quarterly, from the Funding Date until the date of payment; (ii) forfeiture of up to fifty percent (50%) of such Defaulting Partner\'s interest in the Partnership; (iii) mandatory sale of such Defaulting Partner\'s interest at a discount to be determined by the General Partner in its reasonable discretion; and (iv) suspension or loss of voting rights and rights to participate in future investment allocations, each as more fully described in Section 3.5 of the Partnership Agreement. The General Partner hereby expressly reserves all rights and remedies available to it under the Partnership Agreement and applicable law with respect to any default hereunder.

**[VI. Equalization for Subsequent Closing Partners]{.underline}**

Pursuant to Section 3.2(c) of the Partnership Agreement, any limited partners admitted at any Subsequent Closing occurring after the date of this Capital Call Notice will be required to fund their pro rata share of this Capital Call (being five percent (5%) of their respective Capital Commitments) as a catch-up capital contribution at the time of such Subsequent Closing. In addition, such subsequently admitted limited partners shall be required to pay equalization interest on their catch-up capital contribution at a rate of **eight percent (8%) per annum (simple interest)**, calculated from the date of this Capital Call Notice, **December 15, 2024**, through and including the date of such Subsequent Closing, as provided in Section 3.2(c) of the Partnership Agreement.

**[VII. Cumulative Capital Contributions Summary]{.underline}**

The following table summarizes the cumulative capital called from each category of Partners after giving effect to this Capital Call Notice No. 2:

  **Total**             **\$1,330,000,000**       **\$95,500,000**   **\$66,500,000**   **\$162,000,000**          **\$1,168,000,000**
  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

For the avoidance of doubt, the Second Closing Limited Partners funded their equalization catch-up contributions with respect to Capital Call Notice No. 1 at the time of the Second Closing on November 30, 2024, together with applicable equalization interest, and such amounts are reflected in prior capital account statements issued by the Fund Administrator. Such equalization catch-up amounts are not reflected in the table above, which shows only capital called under the respective Capital Call Notices. Total Capital Commitments of \$1,330,000,000 reflect all Partners admitted to the Partnership through the Second Closing and do not include any partners that may be admitted at any future Subsequent Closing.

**[VIII. General Provisions]{.underline}**

This Notice is being delivered to all Partners in accordance with the notice provisions of Section 13.1 of the Partnership Agreement and is being transmitted by email and overnight courier to each Partner at the address set forth in the books and records of the Partnership. Each Partner\'s individual Capital Call Amount is set forth on Exhibit A attached hereto.

text: I now have all the source documents. Let me verify a few key calculations and check the docx skill tooling before drafting.

tool_calls.name: bash
tool_calls.arguments:
{"command": "cd /workspace && ls -la skills/docx/scripts/ && echo "---" && python3 -c "
# Verify ERISA sum of 'Y' flags from checklist
initial_Y = [85,80,40,25,35]  # Commonwealth, Lakewood, Westfield, Ashford, Pemberton
second_Y = [45.3,25]          # Dalton, Oakdale
final_Y = [100,30]            # Meridian, Sentry
prior = sum(initial_Y)+sum(second_Y)
total = prior + sum(final_Y)
print('Prior-closings Y sum: \$%,.0f' % prior)
print('All-closings Y sum:   \$%,.0f' % total)
print('Total fund commitments: \$1,615,000,000')
print('Y-flag %% of total: %.2f%%' % (total/1615*100))
print('Checklist ERISA summary says: \$355,300,000 = 22.00%%')
print('Difference (Y-sum vs summary): \$%,.0f' % (total-355.3))
print()
# Equalization day-count check
print('CC#1 days Oct10->Jan31 (excl start):', (31-10)+30+31+31)  # 21+30+31+31=113
print('CC#2 days Dec15->Jan31 (excl start):', (31-15)+31)         # 16+31=47
print('CC#2 days Dec15->Jan31 (excl both):', (31-15)+31-1)        # 46
print()
# Equalization interest totals
cc1 = 28500000*0.08*113/365
cc2_47 = 14250000*0.08*47/365
cc2_46 = 14250000*0.08*46/365
print('SA total eq interest (113 & 47): \$%,.2f' % (cc1+cc2_47))
print('Checklist total eq interest (113 & 46): \$%,.2f' % (cc1+cc2_46))
print('Difference: \$%,.2f' % ((cc1+cc2_47)-(cc1+cc2_46)))
print('SA total due: \$%,.2f' % (42750000+cc1+cc2_47))
print('Checklist total due: \$%,.2f' % (42750000+cc1+cc2_46))
print()
# ERISA remaining capacity check
print('24.99%% of 1,615,000,000: \$%,.1f' % (0.2499*1615000000))
print('Minus 355,300,000: \$%,.1f' % (0.2499*1615000000-355300000))
print('SA states remaining capacity: \$48,287,500')
""}

prior = sum(initial_Y)+sum(second_Y)
total = prior + sum(final_Y)
print('Prior-closings Y sum: \$%,.0f' % prior)

# Equalization day-count check

print('CC#2 days Dec15->Jan31 (excl start):', (31-15)+31)         # 16+31=47

print()
# Equalization interest totals
cc1 = 28500000*0.08*113/365
cc2_47 = 14250000*0.08*47/365
cc2_46 = 14250000*0.08*46/365
print('SA total eq interest (113 & 47): \$%,.2f' % (cc1+cc2_47))
print('Checklist total eq interest (113 & 46): \$%,.2f' % (cc1+cc2_46))
print('Difference: \$%,.2f' % ((cc1+cc2_47)-(cc1+cc2_46)))
print('SA total due: \$%,.2f' % (42750000+cc1+cc2_47))
print('Checklist total due: \$%,.2f' % (42750000+cc1+cc2_46))
print()
# ERISA remaining capacity check
print('24.99%% of 1,615,000,000: \$%,.1f' % (0.2499*1615000000))
print('Minus 355,300,000: \$%,.1f' % (0.2499*1615000000-355300000))
print('SA states remaining capacity: \$48,287,500')
""}

"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"pandoc failed: {result.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"OK: wrote {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) not in (3, 4):
        print("Usage: generate_from_md.py <input.md> <output.docx> [template.docx]", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)
    template = Path(sys.argv[3]) if len(sys.argv) == 4 else None
    generate(Path(sys.argv[1]), Path(sys.argv[2]), template)
=== validate.py ===
"""Validate a .docx for delivery.

Checks ZIP integrity, XML well-formedness for every part, [Content_Types].xml
presence, and relationship consistency (no dangling rIds). Does NOT do XSD
schema validation in v1 — defer until ECMA-376 XSDs are vendored fresh from
ECMA-International.

Usage: python validate.py file.docx
Exit 0 = valid; non-zero = errors printed to stderr.
"""
import sys
import zipfile
from pathlib import Path
from lxml import etree


def validate(path: Path) -> list[str]:
    errors = []

    if not path.exists():
        return [f"File not found: {path}"]
    if not zipfile.is_zipfile(path):
        return [f"Not a valid ZIP: {path}"]

    with zipfile.ZipFile(path) as z:
        names = set(z.namelist())

        if "[Content_Types].xml" not in names:
            errors.append("Missing [Content_Types].xml")
            return errors

        # XML well-formedness for every .xml and .rels part
        for name in names:
            if name.endswith(".xml") or name.endswith(".rels"):
                try:
                    etree.fromstring(z.read(name))
                except etree.XMLSyntaxError as e:
                    errors.append(f"Malformed XML in {name}: {e}")

        # Relationship target consistency

{"file_path": "/workspace/closing-certificate.md", "content": "# FINAL CLOSING CERTIFICATE

**WHITMORE CAPITAL PARTNERS VII, LP**
*A Delaware Limited Partnership*

**Delaware Secretary of State File No. 7842319**

---

**Date of Final Closing: January 31, 2025**

This Final Closing Certificate (this "Certificate") is delivered by Whitmore Capital Management LLC, a Delaware limited liability company (the "General Partner" or "GP"), in its capacity as general partner of Whitmore Capital Partners VII, LP, a Delaware limited partnership (the "Partnership" or the "Fund"), in connection with the final closing of the Fund (the "Final Closing") held on January 31, 2025. Capitalized terms used but not defined in this Certificate have the meanings ascribed to them in the Amended and Restated Agreement of Limited Partnership of the Partnership, dated as of September 15, 2024, as amended by Amendment No. 1, dated as of November 30, 2024 (as so amended, and as further amended, restated, supplemented, or otherwise modified from time to time, the "LPA").

## I. Background and Authority

The Partnership was formed on August 1, 2024, pursuant to a Certificate of Limited Partnership filed with the Secretary of State of the State of Delaware (File No. 7842319). The General Partner is Whitmore Capital Management LLC, a Delaware limited liability company. The Fund held its Initial Closing on September 15, 2024, and its Second Closing on November 30, 2024. The Final Closing is the last Closing at which additional Limited Partners are admitted to the Partnership, and is being held within eighteen (18) months of the Initial Closing, in accordance with the definition of "Final Closing" in the LPA.

Pursuant to Section 11.2 of the LPA, the General Partner shall not consummate any Closing unless each of the conditions precedent set forth in Section 11.2 has been satisfied or waived by the General Partner (to the extent such condition is waivable in accordance with Section 11.3). The General Partner is delivering this Certificate to the Fund Administrator (Oakmere Fund Services LLC) and Fund Counsel (Bridgeford Hollis LLP) as part of the closing deliverables for the Final Closing, and to the Limited Partners admitted at the Final Closing (the "Final Closing Limited Partners").

The undersigned, Marcus J. Whitmore, in his capacity as Managing Member and Chief Investment Officer of the General Partner, hereby certifies, as of the date of this Certificate, to the matters set forth below, except as expressly qualified in Section IV (Qualifications and Exceptions) of this Certificate.

## II. Certification of Conditions Precedent (LPA Section 11.2)

Except as set forth in Section IV hereof, the General Partner hereby certifies that each of the conditions precedent to the Final Closing set forth in Section 11.2 of the LPA has been satisfied (or, where permitted by Section 11.3, waived by the General Partner) as of the date hereof, as follows:

**Condition 11.2(1) — Subscription Agreement and LPA.** Each of the six (6) Final Closing Limited Partners has duly executed and delivered (i) a Subscription Agreement, in the form provided by the General Partner, containing all representations, warranties, and covenants required by Article VI of the LPA, and (ii) a counterpart signature page to the LPA or a joinder agreement in the form attached to the LPA as Exhibit B. Executed originals of each Subscription Agreement and LPA signature page are on file with the General Partner and Fund Counsel.

**Condition 11.2(2) — Capital Contributions.** All Capital Contributions due at the Final Closing, including Catch-Up Contributions (Equalization Contributions) aggregating Forty-Two Million Seven Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars ($42,750,000) and Equalization Interest, have been received, or are being received concurrently with the Final Closing, in immediately available funds in the escrow account at Pinnacle National Bank (Account ending in 9213) designated by the General Partner. The aggregate amount due from the Final Closing Limited Partners at the Final Closing is Forty-Three Million Six Hundred Two Thousand Six Hundred Sixty-One Dollars and Sixty-Four Cents ($43,602,661.64), comprised of the Catch-Up Contributions and Equalization Interest, as set forth on Schedule 2 attached hereto. The Fund Administrator has confirmed (or is confirming concurrently with the Final Closing) receipt of all required funds prior to consummation of the Final Closing.

**Condition 11.2(3) — AML/KYC Compliance.** The Fund Administrator has confirmed in writing that satisfactory anti-money laundering and "know your customer" documentation has been received and verified for each Final Closing Limited Partner, including OFAC screening results, government-issued identification or corporate formation documents (as applicable), and, where applicable, beneficial ownership certifications under the Corporate Transparency Act and the Customer Due Diligence Rule (31 C.F.R. § 1010.230). The Fund Administrator's written AML/KYC completion confirmation is dated January 24, 2025. **This condition is non-waivable under Section 11.3 of the LPA and is subject to the qualification set forth in Section IV(A) hereof.**

**Condition 11.2(4) — Qualified Purchaser Status.** Each Final Closing Limited Partner has represented and warranted in its Subscription Agreement that it is a "qualified purchaser" as defined in Section 2(a)(51) of the Investment Company Act of 1940, as amended. The General Partner has reviewed each such representation and has no reason to believe that any such representation is untrue, and has determined that each such representation is consistent with the information provided by the applicable Final Closing Limited Partner in its Subscription Agreement and supporting documentation. **This condition is non-waivable under Section 11.3 of the LPA.**

**Condition 11.2(5) — ERISA Compliance.** The General Partner has determined that, after giving effect to the admission of all Final Closing Limited Partners, the aggregate Capital Commitments of Benefit Plan Investors do not exceed the limitation set forth in Section 6.8 of the LPA. As of the Final Closing, aggregate Benefit Plan Investor Capital Commitments are Three Hundred Fifty-Five Million Three Hundred Thousand Dollars ($355,300,000), representing approximately twenty-two percent (22.00%) of total Capital Commitments of One Billion Six Hundred Fifteen Million Dollars ($1,615,000,000), which is below the twenty-four and ninety-nine hundredths percent (24.99%) limitation set forth in Section 6.8. The General Partner's written determination and calculation are included in the closing deliverables provided to the Fund Administrator and Fund Counsel. **This condition is non-waivable under Section 11.3 of the LPA and is subject to the qualification set forth in Section IV(B) hereof.**

**Condition 11.2(6) — No Material Adverse Change; Key Persons.** No material adverse change has occurred with respect to the General Partner, the Partnership, or the General Partner's ability to manage the Partnership since the Second Closing on November 30, 2024. Each Key Person — Marcus J. Whitmore (Founder and Chief Investment Officer) and Priya S. Mehta (Managing Director) — continues to devote substantially all of his or her business time and efforts to the Partnership, and no Key Person Event (as defined in Section 9.4 of the LPA) has occurred or is reasonably expected to occur as of the date of the Final Closing. The General Partner's confirmation of compliance with this condition is set forth in Section III of this Certificate.

**Condition 11.2(7) — Legal Opinions.** Fund Counsel, Bridgeford Hollis LLP, has delivered to the General Partner and the Fund Administrator legal opinions, dated as of the date of the Final Closing, in customary form and substance for transactions of this type, addressing: (i) the due formation and valid existence of the Partnership as a limited partnership under the laws of the State of Delaware; (ii) the enforceability of the LPA against the Partnership and the General Partner; and (iii) such other matters as are customary for private equity fund closings, including the limited liability of the Limited Partners. **This condition is subject to the qualification set forth in Section IV(C) hereof.**

**Condition 11.2(8) — Updated Schedule A.** The General Partner has caused the Fund Administrator to prepare an updated Schedule A to the LPA reflecting the Capital Commitments of all Partners after giving effect to the Final Closing, including the names, Capital Commitments, dates of admission, Benefit Plan Investor status, and qualified purchaser confirmations of all Partners. The updated Schedule A is attached hereto as Schedule 1 and has been delivered (or is being delivered concurrently with the Final Closing) to all Partners and the Fund Administrator. **This condition is subject to the qualification set forth in Section IV(D) hereof.**

**Condition 11.2(9) — Hard Cap Compliance.** The General Partner has confirmed that, after giving effect to the admission of all Final Closing Limited Partners, the aggregate Capital Commitments of all Partners (including the GP Commitment) are One Billion Six Hundred Fifteen Million Dollars ($1,615,000,000), which does not exceed the Hard Cap of One Billion Seven Hundred Fifty Million Dollars ($1,750,000,000) established under Section 3.1 of the LPA. The General Partner's written confirmation is included in the closing deliverables.

**Condition 11.2(10) — Representations and Warranties.** The General Partner certifies that all representations and warranties of the General Partner contained in the LPA are true and correct in all material respects as of the date of the Final Closing as though made on and as of such date (except for representations and warranties that by their terms relate to a specific date, which are true and correct in all material respects as of such specified date). No event has occurred since the Second Closing that would render any such representation or warranty materially inaccurate. **This condition is subject to the qualification set forth in Section IV(E) hereof.**

## III. Key Person Certification (LPA Section 9.4(d))

Pursuant to Section 9.4(d) of the LPA, the General Partner hereby certifies at the Final Closing that:

(a) **Marcus J. Whitmore** (Founder and Chief Investment Officer of the General Partner) continues to devote substantially all of his business time and efforts to the affairs of the Partnership, and no Key Person Event has occurred or is reasonably expected to occur with respect to Mr. Whitmore as of the date of the Final Closing; and

(b) **Priya S. Mehta** (Managing Director of the General Partner) continues to devote substantially all of her business time and efforts to the affairs of the Partnership, and no Key Person Event has occurred or is reasonably expected to occur with respect to Ms. Mehta as of the date of the Final Closing.

The General Partner acknowledges that the Limited Partners are relying on the continued involvement of each Key Person in making their investment decisions and funding their Capital Commitments.

## IV. Qualifications and Exceptions

The certifications set forth in Section II are subject to the following qualifications and exceptions, each of which the General Partner has identified for the attention of Fund Counsel and the Fund Administrator and which must be resolved, confirmed, or reconciled as indicated:

**(A) AML/KYC — Cascade Re Insurance Ltd. (Condition 11.2(3)).** The Fund Administrator's written AML/KYC completion confirmation dated January 24, 2025 enumerates only five (5) of the six (6) Final Closing Limited Partners and does not expressly address Cascade Re Insurance Ltd. (a Bermuda-domiciled reinsurer). Because Condition 11.2(3) is non-waivable under Section 11.3, a supplemental written AML/KYC completion certificate expressly covering Cascade Re Insurance Ltd. (including OFAC/sanctions screening, beneficial ownership, and enhanced due diligence appropriate to a non-U.S. entity) must be delivered by the Fund Administrator and placed in the closing records before reliance is placed on the certification in Section II above.

**(B) ERISA / Benefit Plan Investor Reconciliation (Condition 11.2(5)).** The certification in Section II relies on the Benefit Plan Investor percentage of 22.00% ($355,300,000 / $1,615,000,000) as reflected in the Subscription Agreements and the Fund Administrator's ERISA analysis. However, the individual "ERISA Plan Investor" designations in the Fund Administrator's commitment schedule, when summed, aggregate to Four Hundred Sixty-Five Million Three Hundred Thousand Dollars ($465,300,000), or approximately 28.81% of total Capital Commitments — a One Hundred Ten Million Dollar ($110,000,000) difference that is not explained in the closing record. The General Partner and Fund Administrator must reconcile this discrepancy and confirm the correct Benefit Plan Investor population before reliance is placed on the certification in Section II. If the broader figure is correct, the 24.99% limitation in Section 6.8 would be exceeded, and the General Partner would be required to reduce or decline one or more Benefit Plan Investor commitments pursuant to Section 6.8 before the Final Closing may be consummated.

**(C) Legal Opinions (Condition 11.2(7)).** As of the date of the GP's internal closing memorandum dated January 28, 2025, the legal opinions of Fund Counsel were in draft form and had not been executed. The certification in Section II assumes that final, executed opinions — including the opinion addressing the limited liability of the Limited Partners specifically referenced in Section 11.2(7)(iii) — are delivered at or prior to the consummation of the Final Closing. The General Partner shall not consummate the Final Closing until such executed opinions are in hand.

**(D) Updated Schedule A and Escrow Funding (Conditions 11.2(2) and 11.2(8)).** As of January 30, 2025, (i) the updated Schedule A was under final review by Fund Counsel, and (ii) bank confirmation of receipt of all Final Closing wire transfers into the Pinnacle National Bank escrow account (Account ending in 9213) was pending. The certifications in Section II assume that the final Schedule A is approved and delivered and that the Fund Administrator confirms receipt of all required funds concurrently with the Final Closing. In addition, the Equalization Interest calculations are subject to a reconciliation between the amounts set forth in the Subscription Agreements (which compute the Capital Call No. 2 equalization period as 47 days and aggregate Equalization Interest of $852,661.64) and the Fund Administrator's equalization worksheet (which computes that period as 46 days and aggregate Equalization Interest of $849,534.24); the controlling amount and day-count convention must be confirmed by the Fund Administrator and Fund Counsel.

**(E) Distribution Waterfall Amendment (Condition 11.2(10)).** Amendment No. 1 purports to correct the General Partner "catch-up" tier of the distribution waterfall by amending "Section 8.3(b)" of the LPA. In the LPA, however, Section 8.3(b) is the Limited Partners' preferred return tier, and the catch-up tier is Section 8.3(c). The General Partner and Fund Counsel must confirm that the amendment has been given effect in a manner that preserves the Limited Partners' eight percent (8%) preferred return under Section 8.3(b) and corrects the catch-up mechanics under Section 8.3(c), so that the General Partner's certification under Condition 11.2(10) — including the representation in Amendment No. 1 that the correction does not materially and adversely affect any Limited Partner — is accurate.

## V. Additional Closing Matters

(a) **Target Fund Size Notification (LPA Section 3.1(d)).** The General Partner acknowledges that, after giving effect to the Final Closing, aggregate Capital Commitments ($1,615,000,000) exceed the Target Fund Size of One Billion Five Hundred Million Dollars ($1,500,000,000) by One Hundred Fifteen Million Dollars ($115,000,000). Pursuant to Section 3.1(d) of the LPA, the General Partner shall promptly, and in no event later than five (5) Business Days following the Final Closing, notify the Advisory Committee in writing of such excess, including the amount by which the Target Fund Size has been exceeded and the identities and Capital Commitments of the Partners admitted at the Final Closing.

(b) **Advisory Committee Vacancy (LPA Section 9.1(b)).** One (1) seat on the Advisory Committee remains vacant following the Final Closing. Pursuant to Section 9.1(b) of the LPA, the General Partner shall use commercially reasonable efforts to appoint a representative of a Limited Partner admitted at the Final Closing to fill such vacancy within thirty (30) days of the Final Closing.

(c) **Post-Closing Filings and Deliverables.** The General Partner shall (i) file an amendment to the Fund's Form D with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission within fifteen (15) days following the Final Closing to reflect the six additional investors and the updated aggregate offering amount; (ii) complete any required state "blue sky" notice filings; and (iii) prepare and circulate Most Favored Nation ("MFN") notices to all MFN Eligible Partners (Limited Partners with Capital Commitments of $50,000,000 or more) within thirty (30) days following the Final Closing, in accordance with Section 12.3 of the LPA.

## VI. Reliance and Limitations

This Certificate is delivered solely in connection with the Final Closing and is intended to satisfy the certification requirements of Section 11.2 and Section 9.4(d) of the LPA. This Certificate speaks as of the date hereof only, and the General Partner undertakes no obligation to update or supplement this Certificate after the date hereof. The certifications set forth herein are made by the General Partner in its capacity as general partner of the Partnership and are based on the information available to the General Partner and the records maintained by the General Partner and the Fund Administrator as of the date hereof, subject to the qualifications and exceptions set forth in Section IV.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned has executed this Final Closing Certificate on behalf of the General Partner as of the date first written above.

**WHITMORE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LLC**, in its capacity as General Partner of Whitmore Capital Partners VII, LP

By: _______________________________

Name: Marcus J. Whitmore

Title: Managing Member and Chief Investment Officer

Date: January 31, 2025

The General Partner shall cause the Fund Administrator to deliver the detailed Schedule A to all Partners within ten (10) Business Days following the Final Closing, in accordance with Section 11.1 of the LPA.*

2. The aggregate Equalization Interest of $852,661.64 (as computed by the Fund Administrator on an aggregate basis) is the controlling amount for purposes of the Final Closing; the per-Limited Partner figures above sum to $852,657.53, with the $4.11 difference attributable to rounding. The day-count convention and controlling aggregate are subject to reconciliation with the Fund Administrator's equalization worksheet as described in Section IV(D) of this Certificate.

3. The aggregate amount due from all Final Closing Limited Partners at the Final Closing is $43,602,661.64 (Catch-Up Contributions of $42,750,000 plus Equalization Interest of $852,661.64), to be wired to the Pinnacle National Bank escrow account (Account ending in 9213, ABA Routing No. 071923456).

4. Meridian State Teachers' Retirement System ($100,000,000) and Northbridge Endowment Fund ($60,000,000) are MFN Eligible Partners under Section 12.3 of the LPA.

{"file_path": "/workspace/closing-certificate.md", "content": "# FINAL CLOSING CERTIFICATE

**WHITMORE CAPITAL PARTNERS VII, LP**
*A Delaware Limited Partnership*

**Delaware Secretary of State File No. 7842319**

---

**Date of Final Closing: January 31, 2025**

This Final Closing Certificate (this "Certificate") is delivered by Whitmore Capital Management LLC, a Delaware limited liability company (the "General Partner" or "GP"), in its capacity as general partner of Whitmore Capital Partners VII, LP, a Delaware limited partnership (the "Partnership" or the "Fund"), in connection with the final closing of the Fund (the "Final Closing") held on January 31, 2025. Capitalized terms used but not defined in this Certificate have the meanings ascribed to them in the Amended and Restated Agreement of Limited Partnership of the Partnership, dated as of September 15, 2024, as amended by Amendment No. 1, dated as of November 30, 2024 (as so amended, and as further amended, restated, supplemented, or otherwise modified from time to time, the "LPA").

## I. Background and Authority

The Partnership was formed on August 1, 2024, pursuant to a Certificate of Limited Partnership filed with the Secretary of State of the State of Delaware (File No. 7842319). The General Partner is Whitmore Capital Management LLC, a Delaware limited liability company. The Fund held its Initial Closing on September 15, 2024, and its Second Closing on November 30, 2024. The Final Closing is the last Closing at which additional Limited Partners are admitted to the Partnership, and is being held within eighteen (18) months of the Initial Closing, in accordance with the definition of "Final Closing" in the LPA.

Pursuant to Section 11.2 of the LPA, the General Partner shall not consummate any Closing unless each of the conditions precedent set forth in Section 11.2 has been satisfied or waived by the General Partner (to the extent such condition is waivable in accordance with Section 11.3). The General Partner is delivering this Certificate to the Fund Administrator (Oakmere Fund Services LLC) and Fund Counsel (Bridgeford Hollis LLP) as part of the closing deliverables for the Final Closing, and to the Limited Partners admitted at the Final Closing (the "Final Closing Limited Partners").

The undersigned, Marcus J. Whitmore, in his capacity as Managing Member and Chief Investment Officer of the General Partner, hereby certifies, as of the date of this Certificate, to the matters set forth below, except as expressly qualified in Section IV (Qualifications and Exceptions) of this Certificate.

## II. Certification of Conditions Precedent (LPA Section 11.2)

Except as set forth in Section IV hereof, the General Partner hereby certifies that each of the conditions precedent to the Final Closing set forth in Section 11.2 of the LPA has been satisfied (or, where permitted by Section 11.3, waived by the General Partner) as of the date hereof, as follows:

**Condition 11.2(1) — Subscription Agreement and LPA.** Each of the six (6) Final Closing Limited Partners has duly executed and delivered (i) a Subscription Agreement, in the form provided by the General Partner, containing all representations, warranties, and covenants required by Article VI of the LPA, and (ii) a counterpart signature page to the LPA or a joinder agreement in the form attached to the LPA as Exhibit B. Executed originals of each Subscription Agreement and LPA signature page are on file with the General Partner and Fund Counsel.

**Condition 11.2(2) — Capital Contributions.** All Capital Contributions due at the Final Closing, including Catch-Up Contributions (Equalization Contributions) aggregating Forty-Two Million Seven Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars ($42,750,000) and Equalization Interest, have been received, or are being received concurrently with the Final Closing, in immediately available funds in the escrow account at Pinnacle National Bank (Account ending in 9213) designated by the General Partner. The aggregate amount due from the Final Closing Limited Partners at the Final Closing is Forty-Three Million Six Hundred Two Thousand Six Hundred Sixty-One Dollars and Sixty-Four Cents ($43,602,661.64), comprised of the Catch-Up Contributions and Equalization Interest, as set forth on Schedule 2 attached hereto. The Fund Administrator has confirmed (or is confirming concurrently with the Final Closing) receipt of all required funds prior to consummation of the Final Closing.

**Condition 11.2(3) — AML/KYC Compliance.** The Fund Administrator has confirmed in writing that satisfactory anti-money laundering and "know your customer" documentation has been received and verified for each Final Closing Limited Partner, including OFAC screening results, government-issued identification or corporate formation documents (as applicable), and, where applicable, beneficial ownership certifications under the Corporate Transparency Act and the Customer Due Diligence Rule (31 C.F.R. § 1010.230). The Fund Administrator's written AML/KYC completion confirmation is dated January 24, 2025. **This condition is non-waivable under Section 11.3 of the LPA and is subject to the qualification set forth in Section IV(A) hereof.**

**Condition 11.2(4) — Qualified Purchaser Status.** Each Final Closing Limited Partner has represented and warranted in its Subscription Agreement that it is a "qualified purchaser" as defined in Section 2(a)(51) of the Investment Company Act of 1940, as amended. The General Partner has reviewed each such representation and has no reason to believe that any such representation is untrue, and has determined that each such representation is consistent with the information provided by the applicable Final Closing Limited Partner in its Subscription Agreement and supporting documentation. **This condition is non-waivable under Section 11.3 of the LPA.**

**Condition 11.2(5) — ERISA Compliance.** The General Partner has determined that, after giving effect to the admission of all Final Closing Limited Partners, the aggregate Capital Commitments of Benefit Plan Investors do not exceed the limitation set forth in Section 6.8 of the LPA. As of the Final Closing, aggregate Benefit Plan Investor Capital Commitments are Three Hundred Fifty-Five Million Three Hundred Thousand Dollars ($355,300,000), representing approximately twenty-two percent (22.00%) of total Capital Commitments of One Billion Six Hundred Fifteen Million Dollars ($1,615,000,000), which is below the twenty-four and ninety-nine hundredths percent (24.99%) limitation set forth in Section 6.8. The General Partner's written determination and calculation are included in the closing deliverables provided to the Fund Administrator and Fund Counsel. **This condition is non-waivable under Section 11.3 of the LPA and is subject to the qualification set forth in Section IV(B) hereof.**

**Condition 11.2(6) — No Material Adverse Change; Key Persons.** No material adverse change has occurred with respect to the General Partner, the Partnership, or the General Partner's ability to manage the Partnership since the Second Closing on November 30, 2024. Each Key Person — Marcus J. Whitmore (Founder and Chief Investment Officer) and Priya S. Mehta (Managing Director) — continues to devote substantially all of his or her business time and efforts to the Partnership, and no Key Person Event (as defined in Section 9.4 of the LPA) has occurred or is reasonably expected to occur as of the date of the Final Closing. The General Partner's confirmation of compliance with this condition is set forth in Section III of this Certificate.

**Condition 11.2(7) — Legal Opinions.** Fund Counsel, Bridgeford Hollis LLP, has delivered to the General Partner and the Fund Administrator legal opinions, dated as of the date of the Final Closing, in customary form and substance for transactions of this type, addressing: (i) the due formation and valid existence of the Partnership as a limited partnership under the laws of the State of Delaware; (ii) the enforceability of the LPA against the Partnership and the General Partner; and (iii) such other matters as are customary for private equity fund closings, including the limited liability of the Limited Partners. **This condition is subject to the qualification set forth in Section IV(C) hereof.**

**Condition 11.2(8) — Updated Schedule A.** The General Partner has caused the Fund Administrator to prepare an updated Schedule A to the LPA reflecting the Capital Commitments of all Partners after giving effect to the Final Closing, including the names, Capital Commitments, dates of admission, Benefit Plan Investor status, and qualified purchaser confirmations of all Partners. The updated Schedule A is attached hereto as Schedule 1 and has been delivered (or is being delivered concurrently with the Final Closing) to all Partners and the Fund Administrator. **This condition is subject to the qualification set forth in Section IV(D) hereof.**

**Condition 11.2(9) — Hard Cap Compliance.** The General Partner has confirmed that, after giving effect to the admission of all Final Closing Limited Partners, the aggregate Capital Commitments of all Partners (including the GP Commitment) are One Billion Six Hundred Fifteen Million Dollars ($1,615,000,000), which does not exceed the Hard Cap of One Billion Seven Hundred Fifty Million Dollars ($1,750,000,000) established under Section 3.1 of the LPA. The General Partner's written confirmation is included in the closing deliverables.

**Condition 11.2(10) — Representations and Warranties.** The General Partner certifies that all representations and warranties of the General Partner contained in the LPA are true and correct in all material respects as of the date of the Final Closing as though made on and as of such date (except for representations and warranties that by their terms relate to a specific date, which are true and correct in all material respects as of such specified date). No event has occurred since the Second Closing that would render any such representation or warranty materially inaccurate. **This condition is subject to the qualification set forth in Section IV(E) hereof.**

## III. Key Person Certification (LPA Section 9.4(d))

Pursuant to Section 9.4(d) of the LPA, the General Partner hereby certifies at the Final Closing that:

(a) **Marcus J. Whitmore** (Founder and Chief Investment Officer of the General Partner) continues to devote substantially all of his business time and efforts to the affairs of the Partnership, and no Key Person Event has occurred or is reasonably expected to occur with respect to Mr. Whitmore as of the date of the Final Closing; and

(b) **Priya S. Mehta** (Managing Director of the General Partner) continues to devote substantially all of her business time and efforts to the affairs of the Partnership, and no Key Person Event has occurred or is reasonably expected to occur with respect to Ms. Mehta as of the date of the Final Closing.

The General Partner acknowledges that the Limited Partners are relying on the continued involvement of each Key Person in making their investment decisions and funding their Capital Commitments.

## IV. Qualifications and Exceptions

The certifications set forth in Section II are subject to the following qualifications and exceptions, each of which the General Partner has identified for the attention of Fund Counsel and the Fund Administrator and which must be resolved, confirmed, or reconciled as indicated:

**(A) AML/KYC — Cascade Re Insurance Ltd. (Condition 11.2(3)).** The Fund Administrator's written AML/KYC completion confirmation dated January 24, 2025 enumerates only five (5) of the six (6) Final Closing Limited Partners and does not expressly address Cascade Re Insurance Ltd. (a Bermuda-domiciled reinsurer). Because Condition 11.2(3) is non-waivable under Section 11.3, a supplemental written AML/KYC completion certificate expressly covering Cascade Re Insurance Ltd. (including OFAC/sanctions screening, beneficial ownership, and enhanced due diligence appropriate to a non-U.S. entity) must be delivered by the Fund Administrator and placed in the closing records before reliance is placed on the certification in Section II above.

**(B) ERISA / Benefit Plan Investor Reconciliation (Condition 11.2(5)).** The certification in Section II relies on the Benefit Plan Investor percentage of 22.00% ($355,300,000 / $1,615,000,000) as reflected in the Subscription Agreements and the Fund Administrator's ERISA analysis. However, the individual "ERISA Plan Investor" designations in the Fund Administrator's commitment schedule, when summed, aggregate to Four Hundred Sixty-Five Million Three Hundred Thousand Dollars ($465,300,000), or approximately 28.81% of total Capital Commitments — a One Hundred Ten Million Dollar ($110,000,000) difference that is not explained in the closing record. The General Partner and Fund Administrator must reconcile this discrepancy and confirm the correct Benefit Plan Investor population before reliance is placed on the certification in Section II. If the broader figure is correct, the 24.99% limitation in Section 6.8 would be exceeded, and the General Partner would be required to reduce or decline one or more Benefit Plan Investor commitments pursuant to Section 6.8 before the Final Closing may be consummated.

**(C) Legal Opinions (Condition 11.2(7)).** As of the date of the GP's internal closing memorandum dated January 28, 2025, the legal opinions of Fund Counsel were in draft form and had not been executed. The certification in Section II assumes that final, executed opinions — including the opinion addressing the limited liability of the Limited Partners specifically referenced in Section 11.2(7)(iii) — are delivered at or prior to the consummation of the Final Closing. The General Partner shall not consummate the Final Closing until such executed opinions are in hand.

**(D) Updated Schedule A and Escrow Funding (Conditions 11.2(2) and 11.2(8)).** As of January 30, 2025, (i) the updated Schedule A was under final review by Fund Counsel, and (ii) bank confirmation of receipt of all Final Closing wire transfers into the Pinnacle National Bank escrow account (Account ending in 9213) was pending. The certifications in Section II assume that the final Schedule A is approved and delivered and that the Fund Administrator confirms receipt of all required funds concurrently with the Final Closing. In addition, the Equalization Interest calculations are subject to a reconciliation between the amounts set forth in the Subscription Agreements (which compute the Capital Call No. 2 equalization period as 47 days and aggregate Equalization Interest of $852,661.64) and the Fund Administrator's equalization worksheet (which computes that period as 46 days and aggregate Equalization Interest of $849,534.24); the controlling amount and day-count convention must be confirmed by the Fund Administrator and Fund Counsel.

**(E) Distribution Waterfall Amendment (Condition 11.2(10)).** Amendment No. 1 purports to correct the General Partner "catch-up" tier of the distribution waterfall by amending "Section 8.3(b)" of the LPA. In the LPA, however, Section 8.3(b) is the Limited Partners' preferred return tier, and the catch-up tier is Section 8.3(c). The General Partner and Fund Counsel must confirm that the amendment has been given effect in a manner that preserves the Limited Partners' eight percent (8%) preferred return under Section 8.3(b) and corrects the catch-up mechanics under Section 8.3(c), so that the General Partner's certification under Condition 11.2(10) — including the representation in Amendment No. 1 that the correction does not materially and adversely affect any Limited Partner — is accurate.

## V. Additional Closing Matters

(a) **Target Fund Size Notification (LPA Section 3.1(d)).** The General Partner acknowledges that, after giving effect to the Final Closing, aggregate Capital Commitments ($1,615,000,000) exceed the Target Fund Size of One Billion Five Hundred Million Dollars ($1,500,000,000) by One Hundred Fifteen Million Dollars ($115,000,000). Pursuant to Section 3.1(d) of the LPA, the General Partner shall promptly, and in no event later than five (5) Business Days following the Final Closing, notify the Advisory Committee in writing of such excess, including the amount by which the Target Fund Size has been exceeded and the identities and Capital Commitments of the Partners admitted at the Final Closing.

(b) **Advisory Committee Vacancy (LPA Section 9.1(b)).** One (1) seat on the Advisory Committee remains vacant following the Final Closing. Pursuant to Section 9.1(b) of the LPA, the General Partner shall use commercially reasonable efforts to appoint a representative of a Limited Partner admitted at the Final Closing to fill such vacancy within thirty (30) days of the Final Closing.

(c) **Post-Closing Filings and Deliverables.** The General Partner shall (i) file an amendment to the Fund's Form D with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission within fifteen (15) days following the Final Closing to reflect the six additional investors and the updated aggregate offering amount; (ii) complete any required state "blue sky" notice filings; and (iii) prepare and circulate Most Favored Nation ("MFN") notices to all MFN Eligible Partners (Limited Partners with Capital Commitments of $50,000,000 or more) within thirty (30) days following the Final Closing, in accordance with Section 12.3 of the LPA.

## VI. Reliance and Limitations

This Certificate is delivered solely in connection with the Final Closing and is intended to satisfy the certification requirements of Section 11.2 and Section 9.4(d) of the LPA. This Certificate speaks as of the date hereof only, and the General Partner undertakes no obligation to update or supplement this Certificate after the date hereof. The certifications set forth herein are made by the General Partner in its capacity as general partner of the Partnership and are based on the information available to the General Partner and the records maintained by the General Partner and the Fund Administrator as of the date hereof, subject to the qualifications and exceptions set forth in Section IV.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned has executed this Final Closing Certificate on behalf of the General Partner as of the date first written above.

**WHITMORE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LLC**, in its capacity as General Partner of Whitmore Capital Partners VII, LP

By: _______________________________

Name: Marcus J. Whitmore

Title: Managing Member and Chief Investment Officer

Date: January 31, 2025

---

## SCHEDULE 1

**(As of the Final Closing — January 31, 2025)**

|---|---|---|---|---|---|

The General Partner shall cause the Fund Administrator to deliver the detailed Schedule A to all Partners within ten (10) Business Days following the Final Closing, in accordance with Section 11.1 of the LPA.*
